sessions.name: 1.B - Symposium: Principles to Action: Leadership in Uniting Integrity and Ethics for Responsible Research
sessions.details:
International collaborations on research integrity often span diverse cultural, regulatory, and epistemological traditions. This symposium will explore the methodological challenges in creating consensus in such collaborations while maintaining local relevance. It will draw on key insights from our multinational initiatives to develop open access online research ethics and integrity resources, exemplified by the CORRIE project (Collaborative Open Resources on Research Integrity and Ethics developed by institutions across Ireland and Canada) and the MARIE project (Multidisciplinary Approach to Research Integrity and Ethics) involving ten institutions across the INGENIUM European University. 
In the era of generative AI and research security, international collaboration on research integrity has become increasingly urgent—these projects demonstrate how shared governance frameworks address emerging technological challenges through collective wisdom, yielding tangible outputs such as the CORRIE open-access learning resources (https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/FTW8C) and MARIE’s Munster Statement on the Principles of Research Ethics and Research Integrity. With generative AI and research security rapidly transforming research practices, our cross-jurisdictional work exemplifies how international cooperation in developing integrity standards is essential for maintaining public trust in science while enabling innovation across global research communities. Conference delegates will be invited to engage via digital backchannels, culminating in a panel answering questions. 

This symposium addresses critical challenges and opportunities at the intersection of generative AI, open science, and research integrity. Speakers will explore the methodological complexities of integrating AI into research while upholding ethical standards and ensuring research security. A key focus will be fostering international collaboration in research integrity, particularly within the dynamic landscape of AI. The symposium team is uniquely positioned to apply international lessons learned from the MARIE and CORRIE projects, using their insights to balance the global advancement of AI in research with the need to maintain local relevance and address unique regional considerations.

TIME SCHEDULE
10:30am - 10:50am: Introductions and overview - Dr Tom Farrelly 
10:50am - 11:05am: Learnings from collaborating with international stakeholders to establish agreed-upon principles for - Dr Seán Lacey 
11:05am - 11:25am:  Agreeing on what is responsible and ethical use of generative AI in research - Dr Suzanne McMurphy & Dr Tom Farrelly 
11:25am - 11:40am:  Navigating research collaboration across multiple jurisdictions - Dr Vasiliki Petousi 
11:40am - 12:00pm: Panel discussion with conference delegate engagement

speakers.name: Seán Lacey
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr Seán Lacey received his BSc in Mathematical Sciences and Computing from the University of Limerick in 2003, followed by a PhD in Applied Mathematics in 2007. He has lectured at numerous Higher Education Institutions in Ireland. In March 2011, he joined the Department of Mathematics at Cork Institute of Technology, where he served as Chair of the Research & Innovation Committee (2016–2020) and Chair of the Department of Mathematics Research Committee (2016–2021). He was also a member of the National Forum for the Enhancement of Teaching and Learning in Higher Education Research Ethics Committee (2019–2021) and the National Research Ethics Committee (2023–2025). In 2022, he completed an MBA in Educational Leadership (International) at the Institute of Education, University College London.

In June 2021, Seán was promoted to Senior Lecturer at Munster Technological University (MTU, formerly Cork Institute of Technology). In October that year, he was elected the inaugural Chair of MTU’s Research Council. In January 2022, he was appointed as the University’s Research Integrity & Compliance Officer (RICO). In this role, Seán is responsible for fostering a research environment that upholds the highest standards of integrity, leading the development, implementation, and review of research-related policies and procedures. Working with relevant offices and postholders, he ensures that the University’s research training emphasises the importance of research integrity, promotes responsible conduct of research, discourages research misconduct, and raises awareness of the consequences of same.

Seán has played a pivotal role in shaping MTU’s research governance. He oversaw the creation of its Human Research Ethics Committee in 2022, and the Animal Research and Education Ethics Committee in 2025. He also served as co-lead for the European Commission-supported Network for Education and Research Quality, with a focus on Research Supervision and Leadership.

With over 50 peer-reviewed publications in partnership with local, national, and international collaborators, Seán is an active researcher and mentor. He is currently supervising two PhD students and has successfully guided six MSc students to completion. His expertise in statistical analysis has made him the lead statistician on more than 40 industry collaboration studies, and he has designed and delivered over 70 Continuing Professional Development (CPD) workshops for industry professionals.

speakers.name: Tom Farrelly
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Tom is an academic developer, particularly in eLearning, and a lecturer at Munster Technological University in Ireland where he teaches in the School of Health and Social Sciences, and is co-chair of the institutional Human Research Ethics Committee. He has a long history of working in e-learning and is particularly committed to supporting the transition of non-traditional learners to higher education (he does amazing work with the Irish Traveller communities), and international students. He’s also an open access advocate, and the Gasta Master (if you don’t know what Gasta is, check it out!). Tom has an academic background in social sciences and education.

speakers.name: Vasiliki Petousi
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Vasiliki Petousi is an Associate Professor of Sociology of Law and Deviance at the Department of Sociology, University of Crete, Greece.

speakers.name: Suzanne McMurphy
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Suzanne McMurphy has been at the University of Windsor since 2009 and is currently an Associate Professor in the Department of Sociology and Criminology. Prior to coming to the University of Windsor, she was a member of the research faculty in the Department of Criminology and Senior Research Investigator in the School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania. Her doctoral research was funded by a Fulbright Scholarship to Sweden where she studied the integration of law and social work in child welfare and criminal justice systems across Europe and North America. In addition to her faculty position, she has also served as the Chair of the Research Ethics Board at the University of Windsor and has served on research ethics committees for over 25 years in Canada and the US, including over four years as a committee member of the Social and Behavioral Institutional Review Board at the University of Pennsylvania. She has an undergraduate degree in Religious Studies, a Master’s degree in Social Service, Master’s degree in Law and Social Policy, Master’s degree in Bioethics, and a Ph.D. in Social Policy.

speakers.name: Suzanne McMurphy
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Suzanne McMurphy has been at the University of Windsor since 2009 and is currently an Associate Professor in the Department of Sociology and Criminology. Prior to coming to the University of Windsor, she was a member of the research faculty in the Department of Criminology and Senior Research Investigator in the School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania. Her doctoral research was funded by a Fulbright Scholarship to Sweden where she studied the integration of law and social work in child welfare and criminal justice systems across Europe and North America. In addition to her faculty position, she has also served as the Chair of the Research Ethics Board at the University of Windsor and has served on research ethics committees for over 25 years in Canada and the US, including over four years as a committee member of the Social and Behavioral Institutional Review Board at the University of Pennsylvania. She has an undergraduate degree in Religious Studies, a Master’s degree in Social Service, Master’s degree in Law and Social Policy, Master’s degree in Bioethics, and a Ph.D. in Social Policy.

presentations.title: Navigating Research Collaboration across Multiple Jurisdictions

speakers.name: Vasiliki Petousi
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Vasiliki Petousi is an Associate Professor of Sociology of Law and Deviance at the Department of Sociology, University of Crete, Greece.

speakers.name: Isamme Al-Fayyad
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Isamme AlFayyad is a Senior Research Specialist and Chairperson of the Clinical Research Performance Assurance Department at King Fahad Medical City, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. He is also an external PhD candidate at the Department of Epidemiology, Care and Public Health Research Institute (CAPHRI), Maastricht University, The Netherlands, where his research focuses on the use of Artificial Intelligence in biomedical research.
He has extensive expertise in regulatory compliance and clinical research governance. Mr. AlFayyad has published widely and actively contributes to initiatives that promote research integrity and the responsible use of AI in healthcare research.

speakers.name: Emily Anderson
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Emily E. Anderson, PhD, MPH, is professor of bioethics at Loyola University Chicago. She teaches research ethics and responsible conduct of research to graduate and medical students as well as courses in empirical and global bioethics. Her areas of interest and expertise include researcher and physician professionalism and misconduct; ethical issues in research with vulnerable populations; informed consent; and institutional review board (IRB) decision-making and quality.  A mixed-methods but primarily qualitative researcher, she serves as associate editor for Narrative Inquiry in Bioethics. She also has over 20 years of experience serving on six different IRBs and has been PI or Co-I on several federally-funded research and educational projects. She is currently PI of the Loyola University Chicago-Ukrainian Catholic University (LUC-UCU) International Bioethics Training Program, which provides advanced training in research ethics in English to scholars and professionals from Ukraine.

speakers.name: Mike Perkins
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mike Perkins serves as Associate Professor and Head of the Centre for Research & Innovation at British University Vietnam (BUV). With a PhD in Management from the University of York, he has emerged as a leading global voice on AI in assessment and the ethical integration of AI in education. Dr. Perkins is renowned for developing the AI Assessment Scale (AIAS), translated into 30 languages and implemented across more than 350 schools and universities worldwide. His work addresses the critical intersection of technology, academic integrity, and ethical implementation of AI in educational settings. He leads research in the equitable application of GenAI, and provides guidance to educators and policymakers responding to the challenges of the new GenAI landscape. Dr. Perkins' expertise has established him as a sought-after advisor to educational institutions globally, supporting them in ethically integrating Generative AI to enhance student learning while preserving academic integrity.

speakers.name: Frits Rosendaal
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Prof. Rosendaal studied medicine in Rotterdam, and obtained his PhD in Leiden (1989), with a thesis on haemophilia, and was appointed professor in 1997. His research is mainly focused on causes of cardiovascular disorders. He initiated several epidemiological studies, such as LETS and MEGA, that led to the discovery of a large number of causes of thrombosis, such as Factor V Leiden. An important long-standing research line is on the safety of hormonal drugs, notably oral contraceptive drugs, with regard to venous thrombosis. He developed models to understand how thrombosis occurs as a multicausal disorder, and to evaluate the quality of oral anticoagulant treatment. He takes part in large international consortia that search for genetic causes of several frequent disorders. He is initiator of the NEO-study (Netherlands Epidemiology of Obesity) in which 6700 individuals with overweight have been extensively examined and are followed through time. He is co-chair of the Committee on Scientific Integrity of LUMC and Leiden University.

sessions.name: 1.A - Symposium: Research Integrity and Academic Integrity: Connecting the Dots
sessions.details:
This panel brings together scholars with lived experience from three distinct regions—Asia, South America, and post-Soviet Europe—to explore the interconnections between academic and research integrity across cultural contexts.
Panelists will address how regional histories, institutional structures, and socioeconomic factors shape integrity challenges in higher education and research. By examining both commonalities and differences, the discussion will illuminate how integrity violations in one domain can cascade into the other. 
Topics include: 
•	How political pressures influence academic freedom and research reporting 
•	Economic constraints that create integrity vulnerabilities 
•	Cultural attitudes toward plagiarism, data fabrication, and citation practices 
•	The role of mentorship in cultivating ethical conduct 
•	Regional approaches to integrity education and policy enforcement 
The panel aims to move beyond Western-centric frameworks by centering diverse perspectives from regions underrepresented in integrity discourse. Through this comparative lens, participants will develop a more nuanced understanding of integrity as a global concern requiring contextually sensitive solutions. 
This conversation is particularly timely as international research collaboration increases while integrity standards and enforcement mechanisms remain largely localized.

Our symposium is directly relevant to the topic of research integrity. We include men and women from different regions (Asia, Europe, South America and Canada). 

10:30am - 10:35am: Opening Remarks
10:35am - 10:55am: Factors of research misconduct in non-Western
contexts: Perceptions of faculty from Kazakhstan 
10:55am - 11:15am: Trends in Research Misconduct in Ibero-America 
11:15am - 11:35am: The Whole-Nation System, Academic Freedom,
and Research Misconduct: Findings from a Field Study in China 
11:35am - 12:00pm: Q&A

speakers.name: Beatriz Antonieta Moya Figueroa
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Beatriz Moya Figueroa es doctora (Ph.D.) en Investigación con especialización en Liderazgo Político y Gobernanza por la University of Calgary (Canadá). Posee un magíster en Enseñanza del Inglés como Lengua Extranjera y es licenciada en Educación de la Universidad Católica de Temuco.

speakers.name: Gengyan Tang
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
My name is Gengyan Tang, and my academic journey has been shaped by a deep concern for integrity in research and education. During my master’s studies in China, I investigated predatory journals and the motivations behind authorship, which gave me first-hand exposure to the systemic challenges of misconduct. These experiences convinced me that questions of integrity cannot be reduced to individual failings—they are embedded in broader institutional and cultural contexts.

As a Ph.D. student at the University of Calgary, my current work weaves together three strands. I study how paper mills reorganize scholarly collaboration and reshape the trust networks of science. I also analyze research integrity policies, with particular attention to the global shift from punitive sanctions toward more restorative approaches that emphasize learning, dialogue, and reintegration. In parallel, I explore how minority and Indigenous cultural traditions might enrich integrity education by offering relational and community-centered perspectives that differ from dominant Western models.

Through these inquiries, I aim to reimagine integrity not only as compliance with rules, but as a shared practice sustained by cultures, policies, and communities.

speakers.name: Aliya Kuzhabekova
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Aliya Kuzhabekova is an Associate Professor in Leadership, Policy, and Governance specialization in the Werklund School of Education. Dr. Kuzhabekova has extensive experience as a faculty member, administrator and scholar in international and Canadian contexts. Upon graduation from PhD in Higher Education Policy and Administration, Aliya was a Postdoctoral Scholar at the Centre for Science, Technology and Public Policy in her alma mater in the U.S.. She then worked as an Assistant and then Associate Professor of Higher Education Leadership at Nazarbayev University in Kazakhstan, where she became a recognized scholar in international education and higher education reform in post-Soviet Central Asia. She has produced a number of publications on such topics as 1) research capacity building in higher education in transitional contexts, doctoral education reform and researcher development; 2) internationalization and international mobility (faculty mobility, mobility of graduate students, immigrant student experiences); 3) equity issues in higher education (women in higher education leadership, women and international mobility, women in STEM fields). After relocation to Canada in 2018, Aliya worked as a Postdoctoral Scholar at Carleton University and a part-time Professor at the University of Ottawa. Aliya has supervised over 30 Master's and graduate students, many of whom were non-native speakers of English. She has managed 10 funded research projects, including grants from the Ministry of Education of Kazakhstan, Norwegian Institute for International Affairs, and Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (Insight grant, Partnership Engage and Partnership Development grants). Aliya's research and teaching are based on her diverse administrative in higher education settings, including experience as an Executive Assistant to university presidents of two international universities in Kazakhstan, leadership positions on university committees and service as a member of the National Research Council in the area of education in Kazakhstan. Aliya is an active member of the Comparative and International Education Society and Canadian Society for the Study of Higher Education.

speakers.name: Anu Tammeleht
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr Anu Tammeleht is a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Helsinki. She is also a research fellow in ethics and a methodologist/trainer of research ethics at the Centre for Ethics, University of Tartu, Estonia. She is a designer of research ethics and integrity training resources and a facilitator of trainings of different formats at higher education institutions in Estonia and at the University of Helsinki. Her research area involves development of ethics competencies and evaluating the effectiveness of research ethics and integrity training. She also contributes to developing the national research ethics and integrity system (Estonia) through national surveys, policy recommendations and presentations.

speakers.name: Sarah Elaine Eaton
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Sarah Elaine Eaton, PhD, is a Professor and Research Chair in the Werklund School of Education and Chair, Leadership, Policy, and Governance specialization area. She holds a concurrent appointment as an Honorary Associate Professor, Deakin Learning Futures, Deakin University, Australia.

Professor Eaton’s research focuses on academic ethics in higher education. Her work can be found in the Canadian Journal of Higher Education, the British Educational Research Journal, Educational Policy, and the Journal of Academic Ethics, among other places.

Dr. Eaton is the Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal for Educational Integrity (Springer Nature), a Q1 (Education) journal published by BMC Springer. Dr. Eaton was the co-founder (2018) and co-editor (2018-2020) of Canadian Perspectives on Academic Integrity.

Dr. Eaton is an internationally recognized authority in the field of academic ethics. In 2020 she received the National Research and Scholarship award from the Canadian Society for the Study of Higher Education (CSSHE) for her contributions to research on academic integrity in Canadian higher education.

speakers.name: Anu Tammeleht
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr Anu Tammeleht is a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Helsinki. She is also a research fellow in ethics and a methodologist/trainer of research ethics at the Centre for Ethics, University of Tartu, Estonia. speakers.name: Marina Lambert
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Marina Lambert is a postdoctoral researcher at the Danish Centre for Studies in Research and Research Policy at Aarhus University, Denmark. Her current work on the ETHOS project - Exploring Research Integrity Policies and Practices in “the Houses of Science” – focuses on the analysis of academic and policy discourses on research integrity. Dr. Lambert’s work on the RE4GREEN project - Research Ethics and Integrity for the Green Transition – aims to contribute to establishing an integrity and ethics framework for research and innovation on green energy transition, within the scope of the European Green Deal agenda. She holds a PhD in political science from the University of Copenhagen.

speakers.name: Emmanuelle Marceau
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Emmanuelle Marceau, specialist in ethics and law, is adjunct professor in the Bioethics Program of the Department of Social and Preventive Medicine at the School of Public Health of the Université de Montréal (ESPUM). Particularly interested in modes of governance and their interactions from a justice-seeking perspective, her work explores the ethics of artificial intelligence, responsible conduct in research, professional ethics and public health. Alongside her graduate teaching in bioethics, she teaches philosophy at the Cégep du Vieux Montréal and is a lecturer at the Université de Sherbrooke. Co-director of the Ethics, Governance and Democracy branch at the International Observatory on the Societal Impacts of AI and Digital Technologies (Obvia), she is a member of the Centre de recherche en droit public (CRDP) and the Institut d'éthique appliquée (IDÉA), and completed a residence at the Institut des études et de la recherche sur le droit et la justice (IERDJ).

speakers.name: Matt McCoy
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Matt McCoy is a Director at Proofig AI, a platform using artificial intelligence (AI) to detect image manipulation and strengthen research integrity. He works with universities, research institutes, and publishers to integrate AI enabled screening into institutional workflows, focusing on behavioural, cultural, and systems factors that support ethical researcher practices. With over two decades of experience across education, research, and technology, Matt’s current work examines how AI can move beyond a compliance utility to build a proactive and sustainable integrity culture.

speakers.name: Michael Lentze
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Head Of The Department: Mathematics, Systems Engineering, Computer Science
speakers.bio:
Michael Lentze (born in 1974) studied physics at the Universities of Würzburg and Grenoble and received his PhD in experimental solid-state physics. Since 2007, he has been working at the German Research Foundation (DFG), where he initially oversaw the fields of electrical and systems engineering before taking responsibility for fluid mechanics from 2008 to 2019. From 2020 to 2023, he headed the team for process engineering, thermodynamics, and fluid mechanics. Since 2024, he has been serving as Head of the Mathematics and Computer Science Group.
His areas of special responsibility include artificial intelligence in research and administration, research security and dual use, and technology transfer.

speakers.name: Lorrilee McGregor
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Lorrilee McGregor is an Anishinaabe from Whitefish River First Nation. She is an Associate Professor at the Northern Ontario School of Medicine where she teaches about Indigenous peoples’ health. For the past 30 years, she has been working with First Nation communities and Indigenous organizations in northern Ontario on research and evaluation projects.

speakers.name: Paula Saner
speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.bio: Paula Saner is the Research Integrity Manager in the University of Cape Town’s Office of Research Integrity (UCT ORI) and a PhD candidate at Maastricht University. She holds a Master of Arts in Classics, a Master of Education in Higher Education Studies from UCT, and is a certified VIRT2UE Research Integrity trainer. Paula oversees ORI operations and liaises with key government stakeholders. She advises Senate-level Ethics Committees and leads UCT’s responsible conduct of research (RCR) education programme. Paula supports the UCT community on research integrity (RI) matters and helps maintain a strong, current RI policy framework. She served on the Local Organising Committee for the 7th World Conference on Research Integrity (WCRI) and at the 8th WCRI co-hosted a pre-conference workshop on RI leadership, presented a symposium on RI in the Global South, and helped launch a research integrity network for early career researchers and professionals.

speakers.name: Michael Lentze
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Head Of The Department: Mathematics, Systems Engineering, Computer Science
speakers.bio:
Michael Lentze (born in 1974) studied physics at the Universities of Würzburg and Grenoble and received his PhD in experimental solid-state physics. Since 2007, he has been working at the German Research Foundation (DFG), where he initially oversaw the fields of electrical and systems engineering before taking responsibility for fluid mechanics from 2008 to 2019. From 2020 to 2023, he headed the team for process engineering, thermodynamics, and fluid mechanics. Since 2024, he has been serving as Head of the Mathematics and Computer Science Group.
His areas of special responsibility include artificial intelligence in research and administration, research security and dual use, and technology transfer.

presentations.theme: Research integrity and research ethics structures

speakers.name: Brenda Odero
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Brenda Adhiambo Odero is an interdisciplinary researcher focused on applied ethics, AI governance, and health policy, especially in medical AI. Her work investigates how governance models can effectively balance AI use to promote safety, fairness, and accountability in local settings. With a background in health research ethics and increasing interest in AI ethics within healthcare, she combines research and practical experience in ethics and regulation. Her goal is to help develop trustworthy, human-centred AI systems.

speakers.name: Ilinca Ciubotariu
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Ilinca Ciubotariu is an infectious disease scientist by training and currently an interdisciplinary faculty member at Johns Hopkins University, specializing in communication and research methods. Currently, Dr. Ciubotariu serves as the Assistant Director of the R³ Center for Innovation in Science Education (R³ISE). In this role, she is dedicated to training the next generation of STEM professionals, focusing on rigorous methods, data science, and ethics.
Her research encompasses genomic epidemiology, particularly in the context of malaria transmission dynamics and vaccine development, and SARS-CoV-2 variant tracking. Beyond her research, Dr. Ciubotariu is actively involved in enhancing science education. She has developed curricula for multiple graduate courses at JHSPH, integrating elements of philosophy to foster critical thinking in scientific training. Originally from Bucharest, Romania, she holds a PhD in Biological Sciences from Purdue University, an MHS in Molecular Microbiology & Immunology from JHSPH, and a BA in Biology from Columbia University.

speakers.name: Dena Plemmons
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr Dena Plemmons is the founding Director of the Research Ethics Education Program at the  University of California, Riverside.

speakers.name: Paul Kutyabami
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Paul Kutyabami is a Pharmacist and Senior Lecturer at Makerere University, specializing in Bioethics and health systems strengthening. He holds a Master's in Bioethics and is currently pursuing a PhD focused on ethical data-sharing in resource-limited settings.

Dr. Kutyabami is a key leader in research governance, currently chairing the School of Health Sciences Research and Ethics Committee and previously serving on the School of Medicine's REC. A pioneer in research ethics capacity building, he spearheaded national training programs in Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR), GCP, GCLP, and GRRP. His work, supported by grants from agencies like NIH and EDCTP, focuses on policy development, research regulation, and mentoring the next generation of health researchers and ethics professionals in Uganda.

speakers.name: David Malwitz
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: David Malwitz is the Global Non-Regulated External Audit Program Lead at Johnson & Johnson, where he drives quality initiatives supporting global preclinical research. With a B.S. in Chemistry from Cedarville University and an M.S. in Organic Chemistry from Wright State University, David began his career as a medicinal chemist at Wyeth and Amgen, contributing to drug discovery in neuroscience, immunology, and oncology. At J&J, he joined a pioneering team that developed a novel quality system for non-regulated environments. He later led the creation of an external audit program, resulting in over 150 audits of external partners, including industry partners, contract labs, and academic institutions. David continues to lead and support cross-functional initiatives to enhance data quality and reproducibility in early research. Outside of work, he enjoys cycling, backpacking, woodworking, and spending time with his family in North County San Diego.

speakers.name: Bert Seghers
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Bert Seghers, mathematician by training, coordinates the Flemish Commission for Research Integrity, the second-advice body for research integrity complaints in Dutch-speaking Belgium. He is the President of ENRIO, the European Network of Research Integrity Offices.

sessions.name: Monday Lunch and Poster Meet and Greet

speakers.name: Philippe Emmanuel Mounmbegna Pouonkoussou
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio:
Philippe Mounmbegna holds a BSc in Biochemistry from the University of Yaoundé I, Cameroon, and MSc and PhD in Medical Biochemistry from the University of Benin, Nigeria. He joined Madonna University, Nigeria, in 2002, becoming a Professor of Biochemistry in 2018. His research focuses on Toxicology, Phytochemistry, and Phytomedicine, with over 80 published articles and numerous book chapters. He has presented over thirty papers at conferences globally.
He has gained international experience through fellowships and visits to laboratories in Cameroon, the UK, Ghana, Kenya, and Rwanda, and holds certifications in academic leadership and quality assurance. He has secured research grants and served as an external examiner for various universities. An active member of multiple professional societies, he has held leadership roles, including Deputy Vice Chancellor, Dean of the Postgraduate School at Madonna University. He is now a faculty member in the Department of Biochemistry at Kampala International University, Uganda

speakers.name: Allen Mukhwana
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.position: Head Of Programmes, Strengthening Science Ecosystems
speakers.bio:
Allen Mukhwana is a Senior Research Management Professional and Head of Programmes for Strengthening Science Ecosystems at the Science for Africa Foundation. She leads a pan-African portfolio focused on advancing research governance, institutional capacity, and evidence-informed policy. Her work spans science policy, global partnerships, and systems-level capacity building across African research institutions.
With over 20 years of experience, Allen has driven initiatives in research integrity, science communication, and institutional reform. She is a leading advocate for Open Research and decolonised research security, promoting indigenous data sovereignty, equitable global partnerships, and ethical governance rooted in African epistemologies.
Allen’s participation in WCRI 2026 reflects her commitment to amplifying African perspectives in global research integrity discourse. She champions inclusive, trust-based collaborations that move beyond deficit narratives and strengthen responsible research ecosystems across the continent.

speakers.name: AKM Moinul Haque Meaze
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
AKM Moinul Haque Meaze is a Professor of Physics at the Asian University for Women (AUW), Bangladesh. He earned first class first positions in both his BSc (Honors) and MSc (Thesis) in Physics from the University of Chittagong (CU). He received his PhD in Physics from Kyungpook National University (KNU), Korea, and later completed a two-year postdoctoral fellowship at INFN, Bari, Italy, where he worked on the n_TOF experiment at CERN, Geneva. 
Professor Meaze is a permanent faculty member in the Department of Physics at CU. He is currently on lien leave from CU and serves as a full-time faculty member at AUW. His research interests include Nuclear and Medical Physics, as well as multidisciplinary areas such as STEM higher education and science policy. 
He was a regular associate of the ICTP, Trieste, for seven years. Professor Meaze is a Fellow of the Bangladesh Physical Society and the author of four peer-reviewed books and more than 50 journal articles.

speakers.name: Martin Nganga Muigano
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Martin Ng'ang'a Muigano is a Kenyan biotechnologist and educator. He obtained his PhD in biotechnology from Jomo Kenyatta University in Kenya in 2025. He has taught at Kenyan universities and held research positions in various institutions. Currently, he is a lead scientist at Bio One Scientific Ltd in Kenya. His vision is to promote innovation in Africa through education, research, and collaborative partnerships.

speakers.name: Thando Mdaka
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
Thando currently serves as a Research Integrity Coordinator: Investigations at the University of Cape Town (UCT), a position she has held since October 2024. In this capacity, she oversees research integrity investigations and contributes to the development, implementation, and enhancement of institutional policies and procedures that promote responsible research conduct.
Prior to joining UCT, Thando was a Research Integrity Officer in the Research Ethics and Integrity Unit at the University of South Africa (UNISA) (April 2021 – September 2024). Her work focused on strengthening institutional compliance through the design and delivery of research ethics and integrity training programmes and fostering a culture of ethical research practice across the institution.
Thando began her career in research integrity as an Intern at the National Research Foundation (NRF), where she gained foundational experience in research governance and integrity processes.
She holds a Postgraduate Degree in Operations Management, and her expertise in research integrity has been recognised internationally through poster presentations at the World Conference on Research Integrity (WCRI) and the International Network of Research Management Societies (INORMS).
Thando is dedicated to advancing research integrity frameworks, strengthening institutional capacity, and promoting ethical research practices in higher education.

speakers.name: Langelihle Mlotshwa
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Langelihle Mlotshwa is a public health researcher specializing in research on vulnerable and marginalized populations. Her work examines the ethical challenges of conducting health research in low-resource and emergency contexts, including issues of informed consent, community engagement, and equitable partnerships. She has contributed to studies on strengthening ethical preparedness during public health crises and ensuring research practices uphold integrity and social justice. Dr. Mlotshwa is committed to bridging the gap between ethical principles and practical research, promoting approaches that protect participants’ rights, build trust, and enhance the impact of health research in communities often overlooked by traditional systems. Through her work, she seeks to advance responsible research that is both ethically rigorous and socially responsive, fostering collaborations that benefit those most affected by health inequities.

speakers.name: Jonathan Soeharno
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Professor Soeharno is full professor  at the law faculty of the University of Amsterdam. He has specific expertise on the subject of scientific integrity. He currently chairs the Academic Integrity Committee of The Dutch Research Council (NWO) and was member and chair of the UvA Committee for Scientific Integrity from 2013 until 2022 (chair since 2017). He forms part of the current (re)writing committee of the Dutch Scientific Integrity Code.

speakers.name: Janina Bau
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.organization: European Association of Research Managers and Administrators
speakers.bio: Janina is an EU Projects Officer and Research Analyst at EARMA. She contributes to several EU-funded projects focused on ethics and research integrity, sustainability, as well as the evolving role of research management within the European Research Area. She is also part of the Community of Early career Researchers working within Research Ethics as part of the irecs project. Janina holds academic qualifications in European Studies (Germany and Sweden), Political Science (Belgium), and Cultural Studies (United States).

speakers.name: John Barugahare
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: John Barughare, Ph.D., is a Senior Lecturer of Philosophy and Bioethics, and heads the Department of Philosophy at Makerere University. After a BA Honors (Philosophy) from Makerere University, he received his MPhil and Ph.D. in philosophy from the University of Bergen, Norway. He is a former Fogarty Post-doc fellow at the Johns Hopkins Berman Institute of Bioethics, and a CAPREx visiting Scholar at the University of Cambridge. He teaches African philosophy and ethics, philosophy of method, research ethics, among others. His research interests are on conceptual, analytic, normative, and methodological aspects in ethics in international collaborative health research, and public health. With a goal of fostering health justice within and between countries. He is a member of UNESCO’s International Bioethics Committee (IBC); Africa CDC’s Ethics Technical Working Group; National Bioethics Committee, and the Mulago National Referral Hospital, and the Makerere University School of Health Sciences Research Ethics Committees.”

speakers.name: Samson Mabuchi Banda
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Mabuchi Samson Banda is a seasoned public health professional and research ethics expert with over seven years of experience in health research, data analysis, and project management. Holding a Master of Public Health from the University of Zambia and an Advanced Diploma in Clinical Research from the Vienna School of Public Health, Mabuchi specializes in research protocol development, ethical oversight, and monitoring & evaluation.  

Currently serving as a Senior Research Promotions Officer at Zambia’s National Health Research Authority, he has previously contributed to institutions like the University of Maryland Baltimore and the Centre for Infectious Disease Research in Zambia (CIDRZ). His expertise spans randomized evaluations, IRB submissions, and fieldwork coordination, supported by proficiency in STATA, SPSS, and multilingual communication (English and Zambian languages).  

Mabuchi is certified in human subjects protection (CITI), epidemiology, and health research ethics. His work aligns with global health priorities, particularly bioethics and infectious disease research. Referees highlight his analytical rigor and leadership in regulatory compliance.

speakers.name: Christian Anyiador
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Christian Anyiador is a final-year medical student at Ambrose Alli University, Nigeria, and the founder of the Nigeria Association of Undergraduate Medical Researchers (NAUMR). He is committed to promoting research integrity, digital health literacy, and mentorship for medical undergraduates. His work focuses on innovative approaches to medical education and research capacity building in Nigeria. Beyond research, Christian is passionate about applying medical knowledge to community development and inspiring the next generation of healthcare professionals.

speakers.name: Nicole Au
speakers.title: Miss
speakers.bio: Nicole is a PhD student and part-time research fellow in the Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at Monash University. Her research centres on evidence-based approaches to women's healthcare, with a focus on randomised controlled trials, individual participant data meta-analysis, and research integrity. She holds a Bachelor of Biomedical Science from Monash University and a Master of Biostatistics from the University of Sydney.

sessions.name: 2.A - Symposium: Collaborative and Global Approaches to Research Integrity: Bridging Disciplines, Borders, and Perspectives
sessions.details:
Challenges to research integrity such as the proliferation of paper mills, the misuse of generative AI, and the rise of predatory practices—are global, interdisciplinary, and evolving rapidly. These challenges demand coordinated, cross-border, and cross-sector solutions. This symposium will explore how collaborative frameworks between academia, publishers, research institutions, and policymakers can effectively tackle emerging threats to research integrity. The session will draw on real-world examples of ongoing global cross-sector collaborations on improving research integrity practices. Expert insights will be shared by representatives of different stakeholders examining both the successes and the barriers faced by cross-stakeholder partnerships. As use of generative AI has introduced many opportunities as well as challenges across different sectors, the panelists will also highlight how these impacts research integrity challenges in their sectors, considering language, legislation, culture and technology accessibility. The symposium will explore practical, scalable strategies for fostering global cooperation on research integrity. The theme of this session aligns with the overall focus of WCRI 2026 on research integrity, as it explores how improvements can be made via: • Identification of key enablers and barriers to international collaboration on research integrity • Insights into how global partnerships can adapt to and anticipate evolving threats such as AI misuse and paper mills • Actionable recommendations for stakeholders (funders, institutions, journals, publishers) to support global research integrity initiatives.

The presentations will be followed by a 20 minute panel discussion and Q&A with the audience on cross-stakeholder collaboration, considerations for practical implementation and overcoming regional or resource challenges. Audience from a different stakeholder and/or region to that represented by the speakers will be especially encouraged to share their views during the Q&A. 1:30pm - 1:50pm: Working Together to Tackle Cross-stakeholder and Regional Challenges to Research Integrity - Outcomes from Joint Actions 1:50pm - 2:10pm: Collaborative Governance of Research Integrity: China’s Practice in Performance Evaluation Reform 2:10pm - 2:30pm: What Academic Research Integrity Officers Want You to Know - Working Together to Foster Research Integrity 2:30pm - 2:50pm: The Power of Education: United2Act’s Collective Actions on Research Integrity Training 2:50pm - 3:00pm: Q&A

speakers.name: Jason Hu
speakers.title: Mr
speakers.bio:
Jason Hu is the Director of Research Integrity Engagement at Taylor & Francis Publishing Group. Prior to this role, he has held several key positions at ORCID and Wiley. He has been leading strategic partnership, journal management, business development, editorial outreach, market insights and strategy, with extensive industry experience and deep insights of the global academic publishing landscape.
Mr. Hu has been serving as a Council Member of the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE) since 2016, actively participating in the development of key ethical guidelines and discussions of challenging cases. In parallel, he also serves as the Chair of United2Act Education Working Group, Trustee of the Publishing Training Centre (UK), Advisor of the Asian Council of Science Editors and Advisor of the Publishing Journal.
Mr. Hu graduated from the University of Chicago with a Master of Business Administration (MBA) degree.

speakers.name: Liying Yang
speakers.title: Dr
speakers.organization: National Science Library, Chinese Academy Of Sciences
speakers.bio:
Dr. Liying Yang got her PhD degree in Scientometrics field from the Library of CAS in 2007. She is the director of Department for Scientometrics and Research evaluation, Library, CAS.
The research of Dr. Yang focuses on quantitative analysis to provide decision making support. She has published more than 120 research articles in international journals of scientometrics field. In addition, she has been conducted abundant projects supported by Ministry of Science and Technology of the People´s Republic of China (MOST), National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC).
Dr. Liying Yang was awarded research fellow of Fraunhofer Institute for Systems and Innovation Research ISI, Karlsruhe, Germany. She is the co-editors in chief of Journal of Data and Information Science (JDIS). Over the past 20 years, she has led and released of a series of journal evaluation standards that have had a broad impact on the academic community in China.

speakers.name: Sabina Alam
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: VP of Publishing Ethics And Integrity
speakers.bio: Sabina Alam is the Director of Publishing Ethics and Integrity at Taylor and Francis Group. After a research career in neuroscience, Sabina moved into scientific publishing in 2008 at the open access publisher BioMed Central where she served as Chief Editor of BMC Medicine from 2011-2016, and then to F1000 in 2017 where she was the Editorial Director of F1000 Research. Sabina joined Taylor and Francis Group in 2018 where she leads the Publishing Ethics and Research Integrity team on providing investigative support and expert guidance on editorial policies, publishing ethics and research integrity, and ensures our global outreach on research integrity issues with key researcher communities fosters trust and knowledge exchange. Sabina also serves as an Advisor for Committee of Publication Ethics (COPE), and is a member of the Expert committee on STM Integrity Hub.

speakers.name: Lauran Qualkenbush
speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.position: Senior Director, Research Integrity And Training
speakers.bio: Lauran Qualkenbush is Northwestern University’s Senior Director, Research Integrity and Training and Research Integrity Officer. She is responsible for and involved in Responsible and Ethical Conduct of Research education and coordination. She oversees Research Integrity, handling research misconduct and other research non-compliance, and the Research Training unit. She has been engaged in research integrity for over 20 years. Lauran previously worked as a clinical research coordinator and in Northwestern’s IRB Office. She is a founding member and current Vice President of the Association of Research Integrity Officers. She has been a teaching RIO for HHS ORI boot camps and served on ORI and NSF grant reviews.

sessions.name: 2.B - Focus Track (Part 1)
sessions.details:
As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes increasingly integrated into research workflows, questions arise around ensuring honesty and transparency in AI use. There is a growing global consensus that AI disclosure is an integrity requirement for the use of (generative) AI in research, in publications and in other outputs. While some guidance exists regarding disclosure at the journal or editorial level, there is no global agreement on a reporting standard for disclosure of AI use.

This World Conference on Research Integrity Focus Track takes a participatory approach to the development of such an international reporting standard. The consultation would invite all relevant sector parties, of institutions, funders, publishers, governments, academies, regulators, libraries, AI industry, specialised research integrity bodies and specialised standardization bodies (like ISO and NISO) to contribute their perspective towards such a reporting standard with the Conference serving as a critical feedback mechanism to ensure a draft standard is meaningful and replicable across disciplines and contexts. 

After participatory consultation rounds, two sessions are held physically in Vancouver. The two sessions' topics:
A) Presentation of the drafted Vancouver Standard, discussion of important choices, collection of further comments
B) Futureproofing the Vancouver Standard: Accountability and responsibility of humans in a world of AI-first-science

After the WCRI, the standard will be published and all contributing actors will be invited to adopt it as their own policy.

speakers.name: Bert Seghers
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Bert Seghers, mathematician by training, coordinates the Flemish Commission for Research Integrity, the second-advice body for research integrity complaints in Dutch-speaking Belgium. He is the President of ENRIO, the European Network of Research Integrity Offices.

speakers.name: Kari Weaver
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Program Manager, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning and Learning, Teaching, and Instructional Design Librarian
speakers.bio: Kari D. Weaver is a librarian, educator, and currently the Program Manager, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning with the Ontario Council of University Libraries (OCUL) and the Learning, Teaching, and Instructional Design Librarian at the University of Waterloo in Canada. A noted scholar in the fields of information science and education, much of her recent work has focused on artificial intelligence citation, attribution, and disclosure practices, leading to the development of the widely adopted Artificial Intelligence Disclosure (AID) Framework.

speakers.name: Ann Beynon
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Ann Beynon has held various roles at Clarivate for 19 years, specializing in bibliometrics, research evaluation, and research information management. She currently runs customer success operations for the Research and Analytics business.  She has also managed partnerships in the Institute for Scientific Information (ISI), led the North American solutions consulting team, and managed global commercial training. Her current focus areas are research integrity, research security, open science, and research evaluation reform.

speakers.name: Eby Aluckal
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Eby Aluckal is a Professor in the Department of Public Health Dentistry at Mar Baselios Dental College, Kothamangalam, affiliated with the Kerala University of Health Sciences, India. He completed his Master’s in Public Health Dentistry from KLE University, Belgaum, after graduating in dentistry, and went on to pursue his PhD at Annamalai University, Chidambaram, with his research focusing on smokeless tobacco use among youth.

Dr. Aluckal further enhanced his expertise in global tobacco control by completing a fellowship in the Tobacco Control Program at Johns Hopkins University, Baltimore, USA, under the prestigious WHO–Bloomberg Initiative Scholarship.

With a strong academic and research background, Dr. Aluckal has authored and co-authored several books and numerous peer-reviewed journal articles. His contributions extend beyond academia, as he actively holds key positions in various national organizations connected to dentistry and public health.

speakers.bio: Cédric Lemarchand is a PhD student in public health at the Université de Rennes and the IRSET (Research Institute for Environmental and Occupational Health), currently focusing on nepotistic practices among editors. His previous work includes research on the resurgence of hallucinogens in psychiatry and the French system for early access approvals, with an emphasis on research integrity.

speakers.name: Rene Aquarius
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Rene Aquarius is a biomedical scientist working at the Neurosurgery department of the Radboud university medical center in Nijmegen, The Netherlands. 

Besides his regular work - which focuses mainly on systematic reviews, as well as vivo, in vitro, and clinical research in the field of vascular neurosurgery -, he has also been active as a so-called ‘science sleuth’ for the last two years. 

As a science sleuth, he is focusing mostly on finding image-related issues in scientific articles.

speakers.name: Abiodun Akindele
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Abiodun Tunde AKINDELE is a seasoned generalist university administrator with 27 years of experience, including 15 years in Research Management and Administration (RMA) at the University of Ibadan, Nigeria. His expertise spans research ethics, compliance, and integrity. He has presented on RMA topics at notable conferences such as the Conference of Rectors, Vice Chancellors, and Presidents of African Universities, the World Conference on Research Integrity, the EARIMA Conference, and NCURA’s annual meeting. Akindele has also trained researchers and administrators at institutions in Nigeria and the United States, including Northwestern University and Obafemi Awolowo University. His publications focus on RMA practices in Nigeria and Africa, with comparative indices in a global context. He is an alumnus Fellow of the International Research and Exchanges Board, supported by the Carnegie Corporation of New York and a member of the  African Research Integrity Network (ARIN).

speakers.name: Reda Cimmperman
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: Office of the Ombudsperson for Academic Ethics and Procedures of the Republic of Lithuania
speakers.bio:
I am the Ombudsperson for Academic Ethics and Procedures. I hold a PhD in Biomedical Sciences from Vilnius University (VU), a Master’s degree in Management from ISM, a Master’s degree in Microbiology, and a Bachelor's degree in Biochemistry from VU. My career of more than 15 years at the Research Council of Lithuania (RCL) includes a variety of positions. At the RCL, I have served as Scientific (General) Secretary, Board Member, and Head of the Science Fund Secretariat. I have led projects funded by the European Commission on academic ethics (Pro-Ethics and IANUS), open science (OPUS), and enhancing gender equality in science and innovation (GENDERACTIONplus). I am an active member of various working groups and councils with important roles in science, policy, and academic ethics, e.g., I am a member of the project board of the European Commission’s iRECS: Improving Research Ethics Expertise and Competences to Ensure Reliability and OPUS: Open Universal Science, and a member of the European Network of Research Integrity Offices (ENRIO). I have been Vice-Chair of the Lithuanian National Commission for UNESCO, member of the working group on the European Commission ERA Policy 'Inclusive Gender Equality in the European Research Area’, Council Representative in the CoARA Coalition, member of the Lithuanian National Committee on Climate Change, member of the Steering and Coordination Committees of the Biodiversa, BiodivClim and BiodivRestore programmes of the ERA-NET Cofund H2020 scheme, etc.
I often host workshops on the future challenges of academic ethics (artificial intelligence). In this capacity, I am continuously studying the legal framework of the scientific system and I have published more than 10 scientific articles. 
In order to improve my professional performance, I try to actively present at conferences and continuously improve my knowledge during various trainings, including the two-year Mutual Learning Exchange Training ‘Research Ethics’ of the H2020 Policy Support Facility and the training ‘Academic Ethics in a Changing Society’ of the Mykolas Romeris University Centre for Academic Ethics.

speakers.name: Sabine Chai
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Sabine Chai is the managing director of the Austrian Agency for Research Integrity (OeAWI). As such, she collaborates with national and international  stakeholders to foster an environment conducive to improving and protecting research integrity, conducts trainings on research integrity and related issues, and offers advising in the context of alleged research misconduct. She is a social scientist by training (PhD from University of Maryland) and has extensive experience in teaching intercultural communication, research methods, scientific writing and dissemination skills.

speakers.name: Rosemarie De La Cruz Bernabe
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Rosemarie Bernabe is professor of research ethics and research integrity at the Centre for Medical Ethics of the University of Oslo. She leads/has led several European and national projects addressing responsible open science, reviewing research misconduct from a complexity perspective, ethical issues in the development of immersive technologies, ethical issues in late-phase clinical trials, research ethics capacity-building, equitable access to care, and responsible innovation in crisis-affected regions, among others. Key projects include TRUSTparency, AccessAfrica 1 and 2, ROSiE, XR4Human, Pandemic Ethics, and BEYOND.

speakers.name: Rene Aquarius
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Rene Aquarius is a biomedical scientist working at the Neurosurgery department of the Radboud university medical center in Nijmegen, The Netherlands. 

Besides his regular work - which focuses mainly on systematic reviews, as well as vivo, in vitro, and clinical research in the field of vascular neurosurgery -, he has also been active as a so-called ‘science sleuth’ for the last two years. 

As a science sleuth, he is focusing mostly on finding image-related issues in scientific articles.

speakers.name: Abiodun Akindele
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Abiodun Tunde AKINDELE is a seasoned generalist university administrator with 27 years of experience, including 15 years in Research Management and Administration (RMA) at the University of Ibadan, Nigeria. His expertise spans research ethics, compliance, and integrity. He has presented on RMA topics at notable conferences such as the Conference of Rectors, Vice Chancellors, and Presidents of African Universities, the World Conference on Research Integrity, the EARIMA Conference, and NCURA’s annual meeting. Akindele has also trained researchers and administrators at institutions in Nigeria and the United States, including Northwestern University and Obafemi Awolowo University. His publications focus on RMA practices in Nigeria and Africa, with comparative indices in a global context. He is an alumnus Fellow of the International Research and Exchanges Board, supported by the Carnegie Corporation of New York and a member of the  African Research Integrity Network (ARIN).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Ioana Alina Cristea is Associate Professor at the Department of General Psychology, University of Padova, Italy and a Research Affiliate at the Meta-Research Innovation Center at Stanford University, USA (METRICS). She is trained as a clinical psychologist and cognitive behavioral psychotherapist and has previously worked at Babes-Bolyai University, Romania, the Universities of Pisa and Pavia, Italy, Stanford University, USA and Vrije Universiteit, Amsterdam, the Netherlands. Between 2016 and 2017, she was a Fulbright Visiting Senior Scholar at Stanford University. Her field is best defined as meta-research (“research on research”), taking a bird’s eye view on how research is planned, conducted, reported, and used within and across different disciplines. She applies meta-research methods, such as meta-analyses, to clinically important questions, as for example how to best treat or prevent mental disorders in adults and children or how to improve psychotherapies.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Daniele Fanelli is Assistant Professor in Social Research Methods at Heriot-Watt University and visiting Fellow in Quantitative Methodology at the London School of Economics. He has published numerous influential studies assessing the prevalence and causes of research misconduct and bias, and has proposed novel methodologies to forecast and quantify scientific progress and the level of scientific consensus. He is also member of the Committee for Ethics and Bioethics of Italy's CNR and of the Luxembourg Agency for Research Integrity, for which he advises and investigates allegations of research misconduct, and the creator of comCensus.org, a platform that aims to promote an inclusive approach to policy making.

speakers.title: Miss
speakers.bio:
Lauren Flintoft, Research Integrity Manager, IOP Publishing

Lauren manages the Research Integrity Team at IOP Publishing, which is responsible for investigating ethical cases and developing policies and processes to uphold research integrity. After completing her Master’s degree in Modern History in 2017, she has worked in research integrity focused roles at both Emerald Publishing and IOP Publishing. She is currently involved in several initiatives, including the NISO CRediT taxonomy group and an STM Integrity Hub working group. Her particular areas of interest include special issue ethics and papermill prevention.

speakers.name: Elisabeth Bik
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Elisabeth Bik, PhD, is a Dutch-American microbiologist who has worked for 15 years at Stanford University and 2 years in industry. Since 2019, she is a science integrity consultant who scans the biomedical literature for images or other data of concern. She has found over 9,000 problematic scientific papers.  Her work has resulted in over 1,500 retractions and another 1,100 corrections. For her work on exposing threats to research integrity, she received the 2021 John Maddox Prize and the 2024 Einstein Foundation Award.

Dr. Eaton is the Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal for Educational Integrity (Springer Nature), a Q1 (Education) journal published by BMC Springer. Dr. Eaton was the co-founder (2018) and co-editor (2018-2020) of Canadian Perspectives on Academic Integrity. 

Dr. Eaton is an internationally recognized authority in the field of academic ethics. In 2020 she received the National Research and Scholarship award from the Canadian Society for the Study of Higher Education (CSSHE) for her contributions to research on academic integrity in Canadian higher education.

speakers.bio: I am a postdoctoral researcher at the BIH-QUEST Center for Responsible Research at Charité - Universitätsmedizin in Berlin. After studying Neuroscience at the University of Edinburgh, I completed a PhD in Systemic Neuroscience at LMU Munich, specializing in auditory neurophysiology and sound localization. My current research explores ways to support the uptake of responsible research practices, with a focus on improving transparency in clinical trials. I conduct meta-research to understand current practice and pilot communication strategies to support improvement. My broader interests include open research, scholarly communication, research assessment and culture.

Shawn also represents NSERC as co-chair of the Global Research Council’s Responsible Research Assessment Working Group. He joined NSERC in 2018 as a Canadian Science Policy Fellow and is an alumnus of the Government of Canada’s Recruitment of Policy Leaders program. speakers.organization: Faculty Of Humanities And Social Sciences, Split
speakers.bio: I am a Master’s student in Psychology at the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences at the University of Split, Croatia, with a strong interest in improving research quality and promoting data sharing practices. My work focuses on understanding the factors that influence open data behavior among researchers and on developing ways to enhance transparency and reproducibility in science. I am passionate about advancing research integrity and fostering a culture of openness that supports better scientific outcomes. I have presented my work at several conferences and recently published my first peer-reviewed article.

speakers.name: Sabuj Bhattacharyya
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Scientist And Research Ethics And Integrity Officer
speakers.organization: Institute For Stem Cell Science And Regenerative Medicine
speakers.bio: Dr. Sabuj Bhattacharyya is currently working as a Scientist and Research Ethics and Integrity Officer at BRIC-inStem, Bangalore. He received training in ecology, population genomics, and research ethics and integrity during his Ph.D. and postdoctoral tenure. He has established India’s first dedicated Research Ethics and Integrity Office and is actively engaged in teaching research ethics, conducting research on retractions, and promoting integrity in scientific practice. Dr. Bhattacharyya is an alumnus of the Fulbright Fellowship (USA) and the Commonwealth Fellowship (UK). He also serves as a member of the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission Alumni Advisory Panel and the European Network for Academic Integrity. He has published more than 25 articles in international peer-reviewed journals and has presented his research at several international conferences.

speakers.name: Tiffany Chenneville
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Professor Of Psychology And Marie E. And E. Leslie Cole Endowed Chair In Ethics
speakers.bio: Dr. Tiffany Chenneville is the Marie E. and E. Leslie Cole Endowed Chair in Ethics and Professor of Psychology and Pediatrics at the University of South Florida (USF). She also holds an Honorary Professorship in the Perinatal HIV Research Unit at the University of the Witwatersrand (WITS) in Johannesburg, South Africa. Her scholarship focuses on ethics and sexual health, with recognized contributions to professional, research, and treatment ethics, particularly within psychological and social-behavioral health contexts. Her research addresses psychosocial issues affecting youth living with or at risk for HIV and broader issues of sexual health among marginalized communities. Committed to global health, Dr. Chenneville collaborates with colleagues in Kenya, South Africa, India, England, and Canada. She has produced nearly 100 publications and 250 presentations and is a former chair of the APA Ethics Committee. She recently completed APA Congressional and AAAS Executive Branch Fellowships.

speakers.name: Vivian Mbanya
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Vivian Mbanya works as a senior researcher in research ethics and research integrity at the Center for Medical Ethics, Institute for Health and Society, University of Oslo. She has a background in Public Health. She also serves as a board member and director of the post-graduate program at the Somali Institute for Health Research. She has a PhD in global health and community medicine (Norway), MPhil International Community Health (Norway), MPH (Nigeria). Her research interests include public health, chronic diseases, health equity, gender and issues of sexuality, research ethics, research integrity, healthcare service, minority groups, and epidemiology. She is an experienced researcher in mixed-methods research. Her current research focuses on responsible open science, promoting research integrity.

sessions.name: 3.A - Symposium: Safe and Responsible use of AI in Evidence Synthesis
sessions.details:
Primary research has real-world impact when it impacts policy either directly or through formal evidence synthesis, with the later being considered to provide the highest quality of evidence. The provenance, or integrity, of such evidence syntheses depends both on the integrity of the primary research and on the integrity of the evidence synthesis process. As well as being fundamental to the effective conduct of evidence synthesis, research integrity issues often emerge through the critical appraisals conducted as part of evidence synthesis projects.

Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence have the potential to transform the conduct of evidence synthesis, but the uncritical adoption of these tools represents a new questionable research practice. The Evidence Synthesis Infrastructure Collaboration (ESIC) was established to guide substantial investment in establishing a global ecosystem for evidence synthesis, including a focus on the safe and responsible use of AI. 

Equally, AI approaches have been pivotal in identifying widespread problems with research integrity, particularly in the identification of duplicate or manipulated images within and between publications. 

Content
Prof Malcolm Macleod: Where are we now
Evaluating the current performance of the platforms claiming to provide AI-assisted evidence synthesis, Malcolm will present the ESIC readiness framework, alongside a pre-registered evaluation of 3 online AI evidence synthesis platforms across three recent reviews (one each from Campbell, Cochrane and GALENOS). 

Dr. Kim Wever: Fraudulent images detect through systematic review
Kim will outline how a systematic review of animal models of subarachnoid haemorrhage (10.1371/journal.pbio.3003438) led to suspicions of widespread image manipulation, confirmed through AI analysis (using ImageTwin) to affect 40% of included manuscripts; then reflect more broadly on how to detect and handle image problems in the context of preclinical systematic reviews. 

Associate Prof Jodi Schneider: The problem of retractions
The propagation of error from primary research to evidence syntheses negatively impacts the usefulness of such summaries. Jodi will discuss the problem of retractions, and how these might be addressed in the conduct of evidence synthesis. She will also introduce the broader area of citation integrity and emerging approaches to audit citations at scale.

Dr. Ashritta Saran: How to get the best from AI for evidence synthesis while retaining research integrity
In addition to presenting the ESIC Working Group recommended framework for the development, validation, selection and application of AI digital evidence synthesis tools within evidence synthesis pipelines, Ashritta will introduce the RAISE guidelines for the responsible use of AI in evidence synthesis.

speakers.name: Jodi Schneider
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Associate Prof Jodi Schneider is at the Information School, University of Wisconsin-Madison US, where her research explores information quality, with the ultimate goal of ensuring public policy is informed by the best available scientific evidence. Recently, she has studied how unreliable research lingers in science, often through accidental citations to retracted research.

speakers.name: Sonja Ochsenfeld-Repp
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Sonja Ochsenfeld-Repp is Head of Division “Research Culture”, German Research Foundation (Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft | DFG). Before that, she served as Deputy Head of Division “Quality and Programme Management”. Her focus is on the promotion of equity, diversity, and inclusion, sustainability in research processes, research assessment and safeguarding research integrity. From 2012 to 2015 she was Deputy Director of the European Liaison Office of the German Research Organisations (Kowi) and Head of the Bonn office. She was co-coordinator of the National Contact Point for the European Research Council (ERC). Sonja is a member of the Science Europe (SE) Working Groups on Research Culture and Artificial Intelligence; she is engaged in the Coalition on Advancing Research Assessment (CoARA). Sonja is a fully qualified lawyer by training and has been awarded a doctoral degree from the University of Bonn. She has participated as a speaker or chair in many national, European and international conferences.

speakers.name: Edwin Onyango Were
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: A highly proficient reproductive health practitioner with over 30 years of clinical, public health research and teaching experience in the context of a medical school. Have conducted HIV prevention clinical trials and public health evaluations employing rapid assessment, quantitative and qualitative methodologies for both local and international organizations. Have led the Institutional Research Ethics Committee for Moi University over the last 20 years, first as Secretary, and as Chairperson up to July 2025. Have developed and launched, with funding from NIH, Moi University Research Integrity Oversight Office to prevent and manage research misconduct. Also serving on the Medicins sans Frontiers Ethics Review Board since 2015. Currently involved in ongoing reproductive health research in the areas of Pharmacovigilance for Antiretroviral treatment (ART) in pregnancy and a pharmacokinetic and implementation science study on Long Acting ART and Long Acting Reversible Contraception among adolescent girls and young women in Kenya as co-Principal Investigator. Also lead Principal investigator in SAFE-T Implementation Research Project seeking to evaluate ways gender transformative approaches to shift community attitudes to child early and forced marriage among 10 -14 year old girls in Turkana County Kenya.

speakers.name: Kasphia Nahrin
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: I am a professor of urban and regional planning, possessing 20 years of research and teaching experience in the fields of housing, urban planning, environmental management, gender studies, and development in Bangladesh and the Global South. I have authored over 20 peer-reviewed publications and have been the recipient of numerous international fellowships and grants. I have gained experience in leading funded research projects, supervising postgraduate research, and actively participating in global policy debates on inclusive and sustainable urban development. I have completed my PhD in the UK and a TWAS–IsBD postdoctoral fellowship in Malaysia.

speakers.name: Ayodeji Michael Obadire
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Obadire is a dynamic scholar and research leader with keen interests in sustainability, research integrity and ethics, research management, and ESG. With more than a decade of experience in academia and management consulting, he combines strategic leadership with people-focused problem-solving skills. He currently manages his institution’s research and innovation portfolio, coordinating ideation processes and translating them into impactful projects and innovations. In this role, he fosters a culture of research excellence, advances knowledge creation, and provides strategic guidance for the development and implementation of institutional research policies and strategies.
A Chartered Accountant and Chartered Management Accountant, Obadire holds a PhD in Finance and Accounting from the University of Venda, South Africa. He has complemented this with a Certificate in Statistics from Stanford University (USA) and a Certificate in Research Ethics from North-West University (SA). His scholarly contributions include over 30 publications in peer reviewed journals, books and conference proceedings.

speakers.name: Florence Navidad
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: She is a Professor at the Department of Medical Technology at the Faculty of Pharmacy and Graduate School and a Resident Researcher at the Research Center for Social Sciences and Education (RCSSED) of the University of Santo Tomas (UST). She holds the position of UST Institutional Research Ethics Board Chair. She is also the Research Management Committee chair and co-chairman of the Research Ethics Committee of the Faculty of Pharmacy. She is a medical technologist and nurse by profession and teaches medical technology courses, including research, biostatistics, epidemiology, health information systems, and research ethics. In addition to teaching, she also serves as a research advisor and statistics consultant for students and fellow professors. Her research interests include social and health sciences, health education, public health, epidemiology, medical and nursing, and natural sciences. She is also involved in educational management, organizational dynamics, cooperative and union dynamics, and human resource development.

speakers.name: Jun Fudano
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Jun Fudano was Professor at the Center for Higher Education Studies, Waseda University, until March, 2026.  He is Professor Emeritus of the Kanazawa Institute of Technology.
