speakers.name: Marie Alavi
speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.bio:
Marie Alavi is a research associate at Kiel University. Her research focus is the intersection of RCR, Open Science, Intellectual Property and AI. She is the co-developer of the GATE and member of NERQ. 

Marie is currently involved in the IP4OS project and GATE initiative. Prior to that, she was involved with Path2Integrity and HAnS, conducting research on research integrity and the role of AI in higher education.

speakers.name: Lois Klassen
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: Emily Carr University Of Art + Design
speakers.bio: Lois Klassen is a research ethics coordinator, writer and artist working and living on Coast Salish territory (Vancouver, Canada). She has published and presented widely on research ethics in the context of art and design scholarship. Klassen is an Adjunct Professor at Simon Fraser University’s Critical Media Arty Studio and an Adjunct Researcher at Emily Carr University. Her texts have appeared in The Conversation, Media-N, Public Journal, Parse Journal, Fillip Magazine, Journal of Canadian Studies, and in the books Forced Migration In/To Canada (C. Clark-Kazak, ed), and Diffracting the North (G. Aceves Sepúlveda, A. Álvarez Hernández, Z. Zarza, eds.). Her art and research have been presented at Plymouth University, Concordia University, The Reach Gallery and Museum, Agnes Etherington Art Centre, SOMA (Mexico), and more.

speakers.name: Jesus Rodríguez-Pomeda
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio:
Professor of Business Organization at Universidad Autonoma de Madrid (UAM).
He has served as Vice-rector for Strategy and Planning, UAM. He was also Consultant for the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
His production has been published in Journal of Cleaner Production, Higher Education Research & Development, Studies in Higher Education, Business Ethics-A European Review, Egitim Arastirmalari-Eurasian Journal of Educational Research, The Energy Journal, International Journal of Technology Management, International Journal of Innovation Management, among other outlets.
Member of the Research Institute on Higher Education and Science (INAECU, www.inaecu.com, ror.org/004aqbt71).
His current research interests cover Higher Education management and leadership, as well as Business models and Organizational Studies.

speakers.name: Olavo Amaral
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.organization: Federal University Of Rio De Janeiro
speakers.bio: Olavo Amaral is an associate professor at the Institute of Medical Biochemistry Leopoldo de Meis, at the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro since 2009. He graduated as a medical doctor (2002) and PhD in Biochemistry (2007) in the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul, is a Radiology and Diagnostic Imaging Specialist (2006). After 15 years of research in neuroscience, with a focus on the neurobiology of memory, he now dedicates himself to meta-research applied to research reproducibility, as well as to activism and training of researchers in open and reproducible science. He is the founder of multiple initiatives to promote this goal in Brazil, including the Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative, the Brazilian Reproducibility Network, and the Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative in Systematic review and meta-Analysis (BRISA).

speakers.name: Tamarinde Haven
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Tamarinde Haven is an Assistant Professor in Research Methodology for the Social Behavioural Sciences. She has a background in psychology, philosophy and epidemiology. Her PhD focused on fostering a responsible research climate for integrity. After that, she worked as a postdoc at the Charité UMC in Berlin where she started her research into responsible supervision. This led to a Rubicon grant from NWO to stay at Aarhus University to further investigate the concept of responsible supervision. She recently obtained a VENI grant to strengthen rigour in mixed methods research. Tamarinde’s research interests include research integrity, methodology, open science, and qualitative and mixed methods research.

speakers.name: Ilse De Jong
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Ilse de Jong is a PhD candidate at the Department of Ethics, Law, and Humanities at Amsterdam UMC. Her research addresses the quality and reproducibility of research. As part of her PhD research, she is involved in the TRUSTparency project, which is a three-year EU-funded project aiming to improve reproducibility through the co-creation of interventions that support a transparent and trustworthy research ecosystem. Before starting her PhD, Ilse completed a BA in Philosophy, a BSc in Pedagogical Sciences, and a MSc in Behavioural Science at Radboud University. During her Research Master, Ilse worked as a research data manager for the Behavioural Science Institute at Radboud University.

speakers.name: Ilaria Cianchetta
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Ilaria Cianchetta leads the Research Integrity and Trust Innovation Program at Elsevier, aimed at developing solutions to improve the credibility and reliability of scholarly publishing. With a PhD in chemistry and ten years of experience in the field, Ilaria has a strong background in scientific communication and publishing. She started her career as a , scientific editor before advancing to the role of Editor-in-Chief, gaining valuable insights into the needs of researchers to ensure the dissemination of high-quality scientific knowledge. Ilaria is currently dedicated to creating innovative strategies and tools that foster trust, transparency, and clarity in scholarly publishing. Her work aims to ensure that valuable and meaningful research findings are accessible, impactful, and trustworthy for the global research community.

speakers.name: Wei Mun Chan
speakers.title: Mr
speakers.bio: Research Integrity Manager at eLife. Overseeing research integrity and editorial ethics at the journal.

speakers.name: Laura Caquelin
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Postdoctoral researcher at Karolinska Institutet, Sweden. Current work focuses on testing an intervention aimed at improving research reproducibility. Holds a PhD in Clinical Research from the University of Rennes, France, with a specialization in metascience. Background in biomedical sciences and clinical research methodology.

speakers.name: Ayu Putu Madri Dewi
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Ayu Putu Madri Dewi is a doctoral researcher at Amsterdam UMC, Department of Epidemiology and Data Science, within the OSIRIS project (Open Science to Improve Reproducibility in Science). Her research focuses on assessing how open science practices, such as data sharing, preregistration, and code sharing in peer review, can enhance reproducibility in biomedical research. She leads studies evaluating the use of open science checklists in journal peer review and in grant proposal assessment, combining quantitative analysis with meta-research methods. Trained as a veterinarian with a Master’s in Public Health majoring Biostatistics, Ayu brings an interdisciplinary background bridging public health, epidemiology, and data transparency. She is passionate about fostering a more open, ethical, and reproducible research culture through evidence-based policy and practical tools that support researchers, journals, and funders.

speakers.name: Karla Hagan
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Study Director, Consensus Study On Corrections And Retractions
speakers.bio: Karla Hagan is a Senior Program Officer at the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine. In this role, she served as Staff Director of the National Science, Technology, and Security Roundtable, a Congressionally-mandated activity that convened experts across academia, government, the intelligence community, and the private sector to examine challenges and opportunities around research security. She currently serves as Study Director for a consensus study on corrections and retractions of scientific journal articles, which launched spring 2025. Before this, Karla led the science and innovation team at the British Embassy in Washington, where she worked on bilateral United States-United Kingdom policy and diplomacy across the science and innovation spectrum, including clean energy technologies like offshore wind and fusion; artificial intelligence; quantum science and technologies; and research security. Originally from the Space Coast of Florida, Karla earned her B.S. in Physics from Georgia Institute of Technology and her Ph.D. in experimental high energy physics from University of Virginia.

speakers.name: Jennifer Byrne
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Jennifer Byrne is Director of Biobanking- NSW Health, and Professor of Molecular Oncology, University of Sydney, where she leads the PRIMeR group (Publication and Research Integrity in Medical Research). Jennifer was one of Nature’s 10 people in 2017 for highlighting repetitive human gene research papers with critical reagent errors, and she was one of 3 experts who testified before US Congress at their 2022 congressional hearing into paper mills and research misconduct. Jennifer’s research team has continued to identify features of flawed human gene and cancer research, including descriptions of human cell line models that may not exist.

speakers.name: Roxana Lescano
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Ms. Lescano has worked at the U.S. Naval Medical Research Unit SOUTH (NAMRU SOUTH) for over 35 years and since 2005, as the Head of the Research Administration Program. Under this position, she has founded and later coordinated the Scientific Review Board, the Research Ethics Committee, and the Human Subjects Protection Training Program. More recently, her responsibilities have increased to include the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC), among other functions. Ms. Lescano is an attorney and holds a Masters in Bioethics, in addition to having completed the course work for the Masters in Epidemiological Research.  Her experience extends from IRB administration to ethical oversight of IRB approved studies, in addition to supporting NAMRU SOUTH and other Peruvian organizations to develop their institutional policies and procedures, and their education programs in the field of human subject protections, scientific integrity and animal welfare in research. Ms. Lescano has also focused on research integrity education programs for over 10 years, preparing modules for the original Peruvian course on Responsible Conduct of Research, coordinating curricula for local courses, as well as workshops for undergrad and post-graduate programs, and participating as a guest speaker in academic and research organizations in Peru and neighboring countries. Additionally, Ms. Lescano has led and supported several research projects in this field. From 2019 to date, she has been a member of the organizing committee of the World Conferences of Research Integrity conducted in Hong Kong in 2019, Cape Town, South Africa in 2022, and in Athens, Greece, this coming June 2024.

presentations.title: Corrections and Retractions: Upgrading the Scientific Record, a Consensus Study of the U.S. National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine

Originally from the Space Coast of Florida, Karla earned her B.S. in Physics from Georgia Institute of Technology and her Ph.D. in experimental high energy physics from University of Virginia.

speakers.bio: Ayu Putu Madri Dewi is a doctoral researcher at Amsterdam UMC, Department of Epidemiology and Data Science, within the OSIRIS project (Open Science to Improve Reproducibility in Science). Her research focuses on assessing how open science practices, such as data sharing, preregistration, and code sharing in peer review, can enhance reproducibility in biomedical research. She leads studies evaluating the use of open science checklists in journal peer review and in grant proposal assessment, combining quantitative analysis with meta-research methods. Trained as a veterinarian with a Master’s in Public Health majoring Biostatistics, Ayu brings an interdisciplinary background bridging public health, epidemiology, and data transparency. She is passionate about fostering a more open, ethical, and reproducible research culture through evidence-based policy and practical tools that support researchers, journals, and funders.

Her work aims to ensure that valuable and meaningful research findings are accessible, impactful, and trustworthy for the global research community.

sessions.name: Steneck-Mayer Lecture
sessions.details: The Steneck-Mayer lecture is named after Nick Steneck and Tony Mayer, the founders of the World Conferences on Research Integrity. The lecture aims to focus on one or more important issues that affect researchers, research institutions, and/or the research system at large and is intended to be an original, insightful, creative and inspiring contribution to the Conference.

speakers.name: Lex Bouter
speakers.title: Prof
speakers.position: Professor Emeritus of Methodology and Integrity
speakers.bio: Lex Bouter is Professor Emeritus of Methodology and Integrity at the Department of Epidemiology and Data Science of the Amsterdam University Medical Centers and the Department of Philosophy of the Faculty of Humanities of the Vrije Universiteit. He is involved research and teaching on research integrity and open science topics. He was appointed as tenured Professor of Epidemiology in 1992 and served his university as its rector between 2006 and 2013. Professor Bouter has supervised 81 PhD students, of whom to date 17 were appointed as professor. He is the founding chair of the World Conferences on Research Integrity Foundation.

speakers.name: Robert Dijkgraaf
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: The 2026 Steneck-Mayer lecturer is Robbert Dijkgraaf. He has been the president of the Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences, was the director of the Princeton Institute for Advanced Study and recently was Minister (Secretary of state) of Education, Culture and Science in the Netherlands. Currently Robbert Dijkgraaf is University Professor of Science and Society from an International Perspective at the University of Amsterdam and the President-Elect of the International Science Council. In his diverse roles Robbert Dijkgraaf has consistently taken a strong stance for academic freedom, research integrity and open science.

sessions.name: ECRPs Unite: Conversations by the Pacific
sessions.details:
This networking session is for all early to mid-career researchers and professionals. It is a free-of-charge event where you will meet others at a similar career stage. This session is sponsored by the World Conference on Research Integrity Foundation (WCRIF), and you will get to meet members of the WCRIF governing board and other experienced researchers and professionals.

Due to limited space, we kindly ask you to sign up during the registration process.

sessions.name: Plenary Session #1 - Misuse of AI by Paper Mills
sessions.details:
The use of AI is revolutionizing scientific research and scholarship. As AI use in research expands, concerns about AI misuse are also growing. This plenary session will invite 3 international experts to discuss the possible misuse of AI by research paper mills and how the research and publishing communities can best respond. 

The misuse of AI by paper mills is relevant to the WCRI2026 themes of AI and research security. While AI can valuably enhance research capacity, AI can also unethically scale manuscript production and render falsified and/or fabricated research more difficult to detect. Paper mills are likely to value AI to scale their operations and exploit new research fields that have been previously resistant to research fraud. These problems are likely to grow in magnitude in the immediate term, highlighting the importance of showcasing a diverse speaker group at the forefront of understanding and responding to AI misuse by paper mills. 

TIME SCHEDULE
8:30am - 8:57am: Misuse of AI in research- from data production to manuscripts - Dr Daniel Henriques Moreira, Loyola University Chicago
9:00am - 9:27am: AI misuse to scale production of human health research manuscripts - Prof Stefan Stender, Department of Clinical Biochemistry, Rigshospitalet, Copenhagen University Hospital, Copenhagen, Denmark
9:30 - 9:57am: Paper mills and AI- a publisher perspective - Dr Renee Hoch,  Public Library of Science (PLOS)

sessions.name: 4.B - Focus Track (Part 2)
sessions.details:
As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes increasingly integrated into research workflows, questions arise around ensuring honesty and transparency in AI use. There is a growing global consensus that AI disclosure is an integrity requirement for the use of (generative) AI in research, in publications and in other outputs. While some guidance exists regarding disclosure at the journal or editorial level, there is no global agreement on a reporting standard for disclosure of AI use.

This World Conference on Research Integrity Focus Track takes a participatory approach to the development of such an international reporting standard. The consultation would invite all relevant sector parties, of institutions, funders, publishers, governments, academies, regulators, libraries, AI industry, specialised research integrity bodies and specialised standardization bodies (like ISO and NISO) to contribute their perspective towards such a reporting standard with the Conference serving as a critical feedback mechanism to ensure a draft standard is meaningful and replicable across disciplines and contexts. 

After participatory consultation rounds, two sessions are held physically in Vancouver. The two sessions' topics:
A) Presentation of the drafted Vancouver Standard, discussion of important choices, collection of further comments
B) Futureproofing the Vancouver Standard: Accountability and responsibility of humans in a world of AI-first-science

After the WCRI, the standard will be published and all contributing actors will be invited to adopt it as their own policy.

speakers.name: Nancy Kwangwa
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Nancy Kwangwa

Dr. Nancy Kwangwa is a distinguished library and information professional committed to promoting digital inclusion, research integrity, AI  and media literacy and equitable access to information. She currently serves as Deputy Librarian and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Champion at the Women’s University in Africa, where she leads initiatives in research support, information and digital literacy, and scholarly communication. Dr. Kwangwa has developed and implemented programmes on information and digital literacy that have impacted over 10,000 community members through training and resources.
Dr. Kwangwa is an award-winning researcher, and her work concentrates on media and information literacy, research integrity, AI literacy, and community development, highlighting the essential role of library professionals in empowering communities to navigate the digital age and uphold information integrity. She advocates for inclusive, rights-based access to information, especially for marginalised groups.
She holds a PhD from the University of Cape Town, South Africa, and degrees from the National University of Science and Technology, Zimbabwe. In pursuit of ongoing professional development, she participated in the U.S. Department of State’s Community Solutions Programme. In recognition of her contributions to community development and academic librarianship, she was nominated as a specialist and fellow of the U.S. Department of State’s Community Engagement Exchange Programme and an International Visitor Leadership Program (IVLP), respectively.
She is an active member of several professional networks, including UNESCO Women 4 AI Ethics, the Zimbabwe Library Association, and IFLA Law Libraries Section. Dr. Kwangwa is a changemaker shaping the future of information professionals and advancing digital inclusion in Zimbabwe through her leadership, research and community engagement.

speakers.name: Sarah Jenkins
speakers.title: Miss
speakers.position: Senior Director, Research Integrity & Publishing Ethics
speakers.bio:
Sarah Jenkins is the Director for Research Integrity & Publishing Ethics at Elsevier.  Sarah and her team promote research integrity through policies, best practices, and education, support Elsevier’s Publishing Teams and Editors to investigate and resolve ethics cases and work with experts across Elsevier to build tools and develop processes to detect unethical practices during the manuscript submission and peer-review process. 

Sarah began her publishing career at Elsevier working with editors and learned societies in health and medical sciences.  Sarah later joined the Physical Sciences group as a Publishing Director where she focused on open access and open science through initiatives that promoted the deposition of code and software, and established links between original research articles and data journals.

speakers.name: Orkhon Gantogtokh
speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.bio: Orkhon Gantogtokh is a Ph.D. candidate in Education Studies at the University of British Columbia (UBC), a Public Scholar, and a Fellow of the Institute of Asian Research (IAR). She currently serves as the Executive Director of the Academy for Higher Education Development. Holding an MSc in Higher Education from the University of Oxford (2016), Orkhon actively contributes to higher education reform in Mongolia through civic engagement, research, and participation in national policy initiatives. She has chaired the Higher Education Subcommittee of the Education Reform Movement since 2019 to address education quality challenges. Her professional experience includes positions as a Higher Education Specialist at the Higher Education Reform Project, Researcher at the London School of Economics Enterprise, and Research and Partnership Manager at the Mongolian National Council for Education Accreditation. She has also consulted for the National University of Mongolia and the Mongolian University of Science and Technology.

speakers.name: Habtamu Hailu
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: I am a Lecturer at the Ethiopian Defense University, College of Health Sciences. I hold a Master of Public Health in Epidemiology and Biostatistics with a specialization in Health Research Ethics from Addis Ababa University, School of Public Health. My teaching and research focus on epidemiological methods, biostatistics, and ethical issues in health research. I have a strong interest in research integrity, particularly research misconduct and the responsible conduct of research in health-related studies. I have contributed to several community-based and institutional health projects and presented my work in academic and professional forums. As an early-career researcher from Africa, I am passionate about promoting ethical and transparent research practices through education, mentorship, and collaboration. My long-term goal is to advance research integrity and foster a culture of responsible science in health research across low- and middle-income countries.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.organization: Center for Science and Technology Studies (CWTS), Leiden University
speakers.bio: Joonha Jeon is a PhD candidate at the Centre for Science and Technology Studies (CWTS), Leiden University. He holds a Master’s degree from the Graduate School of Science and Technology Policy (STP) at KAIST. Prior to his doctoral studies, he worked in the IT industry in South Korea for several years, with a focus on data analytics and artificial intelligence. His research interests lie at the intersection of science and technology studies (STS) and valuation studies, particularly examining research systems and scholarly communication.

University of Athens (r-nano.gr/personnel/eleni-spyrakou-2/ ). She has studied Philosophy
(BA Hons, University of Patras), and she holds an MPhil in Philosophy focusing on Ethics and Political
philosophy (King’s College London, UK) and a PhD (Hons) in Political Philosophy (University of
Ioannina, Greece). Her main research interests include research ethics and research integrity, applied ethics (bioethics, environmental
ethics, business ethics), technology assessment,  the ethics-politics relation and its
epistemological problems, the methodology/ies of social sciences. During the last 7 years her
research has been focused on research ethics and research integrity through her participation in a number of Horizon 2020 and Horizon Europe “Science with and for Society” projects. She was a member of the local organising committee of the 8th WCRI in Athens, Greece in June 2024.

speakers.name: Prakash Hande
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Dr. M. Prakash Hande is a researcher and educator specialising in radiation biology, telomere biology, and genomic instability. He has authored over 180 peer-reviewed articles in leading journals such as Nature Genetics, Cell, Science, Genes and Development, and PNAS, with a Scopus H-index of 56 (~15,000 citations). At NUS, he teaches Cancer Biology and Ageing, and has conducted interdisciplinary and STS-based courses (e.g., Biomedicine and Singapore Society, Radiation and Society). In addition, he coordinated workshops and gave lectures on Responsible Conduct of Research for graduate students for more than 15  years and delivering educational lectures on research integrity. He currently serves on Committee 1 of the International Commission on Radiological Protection (ICRP), co-chairs Task Group 121, contributes to Task Group 118, and is the recipient of the AARR Senior Level Scientist Award 2025 from the Asian Association for Radiation Research.

speakers.name: Tim Kersjes
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Tim Kersjes is the Head of Research Integrity, Resolutions at Springer Nature. The Resolutions team consists of highly committed research integrity expects who support in-house editors and Editors-in-Chief in handling and resolving all types of research integrity and publication ethics issues. The cases vary from plagiarism and authorship disputes to peer review manipulation, medical-ethical concerns, data fabrication and image manipulation in submitted manuscripts and published articles. Tim has worked in publishing for over 10 years, almost 9 of which in research integrity. He has advised on thousands of research integrity cases small and large, and has written hundreds of retraction notices. He is also proud to serve as an elected trustee at the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE).

speakers.name: Gilles Hubert
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Gilles Hubert is an Associate Professor in Computer Science at the University of Toulouse, France. He holds a PhD (2010) and a habilitation degree (2010) from the University of Toulouse. He is at the head of the Information Retrieval and Information Synthesis (IRIS) research team at the IRIT laboratory, France.
His research interests includes Information Retrieval, automatic summarization and more globally text analysis.

speakers.name: Mark Hooper
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr Mark Hooper is the Founder and Director of Tricky Goose Training. He has some twenty years' experience designing and delivering effective education programs, managing people and projects, and facilitating change across the academic, industry, and government sectors. Mark is also a Council Member for the Committee on Publication Ethics.

speakers.name: Alex Holcombe
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: In his lab, Alex conducts attention and perception experiments on topics such as how humans keep track of moving objects. Outside the lab, Alex has been involved in improving researcher practices in science through initiatives such as the metascience publishing platform MetaROR, the Association for Interdisciplinary Meta-research and Open Science, the Free Journal Network, Registered Replication Reports, the WikiJournal of Science, and the creation of the journal Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science.

speakers.name: Fahad Mahmood
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: I am associated with Department of Digital media, School of Communication Studies, University of the Punjab, Lahore, Pakistan as Assistant Professor. I was a resident scholar at Center for the Study of Religion and Conflict and Walter Cronkite School of Journalism and Mass Communication, Arizona State University, USA in 2017. I have a background of working in print and electronic media, and the development sector. I have published my research and have presented it at many forums including IAMCR. I am founding general secretary of the Association of Media and Communication Academic Professional (AMCAP) established in 2017 in Pakistan that represents the pioneer media association for the academics in the country.

speakers.name: Karen Wallace
speakers.title: Ms
speakers.organization: Secretariat on Responsible Conduct of Research
speakers.bio:
Karen Wallace is the Executive Director for the Secretariat on Responsible Conduct of Research. The Secretariat is responsible for the implementation of the Tri-Agency Framework: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR Framework) on behalf of Canada’s three research funding Agencies (the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR), the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC) and the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC)).  
She has been working in the research integrity and responsible conduct of research (RCR) space since joining CIHR more than 20 years ago. Karen played an integral role in the development of the first Canadian Framework on RCR. Since 2021, Karen has been the Executive Director of the Secretariat where she leads a dedicated team responsible for developing and interpreting guidance on responsible conduct of research and the ethical conduct for research involving humans in Canada.

speakers.name: Malgorzata Lagisz
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
I am a biologist with interdisciplinary expertise in ecology, evolution, environmental science, conservation, and biomedical and social research. My work centers on research synthesis methodologies for systematic reviews, evidence maps, and meta-analyses. I also develop methods addressing challenges such as data non-independence, heterogeneity, scaling, and reporting biases.
Alongside evidence syntheses, I conduct meta-research on research transparency, equity, diversity, and inclusion. This includes evaluating research practices, advancing reporting guidelines, and assessing recognition systems (e.g., awards and authorship). My goal is to foster rigorous, open, and socially inclusive science.
Through local and international collaborations, I aim to improve how research is designed, conducted, and disseminated, helping build a more transparent, equitable, and resilient scientific community.
Lab webpage: www.i-deel.org/

presentations.title: Implications for Genuineness of Indigenous Knowledge Streams in AI‑Driven Contexts: A Perspective From the Global South

speakers.name: Fahad Mahmood
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: I am associated with Department of Digital media, School of Communication Studies, University of the Punjab, Lahore, Pakistan as Assistant Professor. I was a resident scholar at Center for the Study of Religion and Conflict and Walter Cronkite School of Journalism and Mass Communication, Arizona State University, USA in 2017. I have a background of working in print and electronic media, and the development sector. I have published my research and have presented it at many forums including IAMCR. I am founding general secretary of the Association of Media and Communication Academic Professional (AMCAP) established in 2017 in Pakistan that represents the pioneer media association for the academics in the country.

affiliations: Department Of Digital Media Scs, University Of The Punjab, Lahore Pakistan

presentations.title: Uncritical Reliance on Retraction Watch Database undermines Research on Paper Mills as Limitations are rarely sufficiently addressed

speakers.name: Tim Kersjes
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Tim Kersjes is the Head of Research Integrity, Resolutions at Springer Nature. The Resolutions team consists of highly committed research integrity expects who support in-house editors and Editors-in-Chief in handling and resolving all types of research integrity and publication ethics issues. The cases vary from plagiarism and authorship disputes to peer review manipulation, medical-ethical concerns, data fabrication and image manipulation in submitted manuscripts and published articles. Tim has worked in publishing for over 10 years, almost 9 of which in research integrity. He has advised on thousands of research integrity cases small and large, and has written hundreds of retraction notices. He is also proud to serve as an elected trustee at the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE).

presentations.title: Dynamics of Text Reuse in MDPI Peer Review Reports

speakers.name: Gilles Hubert
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Gilles Hubert is an Associate Professor in Computer Science at the University of Toulouse, France. He holds a PhD (2010) and a habilitation degree (2010) from the University of Toulouse. He is at the head of the Information Retrieval and Information Synthesis (IRIS) research team at the IRIT laboratory, France.
His research interests includes Information Retrieval, automatic summarization and more globally text analysis.

affiliations: Univ. Grenoble Alpes, CNRS, Grenoble INP, LIG

presentations.title: Beyond Ghosts: Ethical Dilemmas When Research Contributors Decline Authorship

speakers.name: Mark Hooper
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr Mark Hooper is the Founder and Director of Tricky Goose Training. He has some twenty years' experience designing and delivering effective education programs, managing people and projects, and facilitating change across the academic, industry, and government sectors. Mark is also a Council Member for the Committee on Publication Ethics.

presentations.title: Selective Highlighting in Scholarly Communication: An Emerging Concern for Research Integrity?

speakers.name: Prakash Hande
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Dr. M. Prakash Hande is a researcher and educator specialising in radiation biology, telomere biology, and genomic instability. He has authored over 180 peer-reviewed articles in leading journals such as Nature Genetics, Cell, Science, Genes and Development, and PNAS, with a Scopus H-index of 56 (~15,000 citations). At NUS, he teaches Cancer Biology and Ageing, and has conducted interdisciplinary and STS-based courses (e.g., Biomedicine and Singapore Society, Radiation and Society). In addition, he coordinated workshops and gave lectures on Responsible Conduct of Research for graduate students for more than 15  years and delivering educational lectures on research integrity. He currently serves on Committee 1 of the International Commission on Radiological Protection (ICRP), co-chairs Task Group 121, contributes to Task Group 118, and is the recipient of the AARR Senior Level Scientist Award 2025 from the Asian Association for Radiation Research.

presentations.title: Improving Acknowledgment Sections to Better Credit Research Contributors

speakers.name: Alex Holcombe
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: In his lab, Alex conducts attention and perception experiments on topics such as how humans keep track of moving objects. Outside the lab, Alex has been involved in improving researcher practices in science through initiatives such as the metascience publishing platform MetaROR, the Association for Interdisciplinary Meta-research and Open Science, the Free Journal Network, Registered Replication Reports, the WikiJournal of Science, and the creation of the journal Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science.

speakers.name: Victoria Chepkorir Soi
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
Victoria is a Research Scientist at the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI), working within the Scientific and Ethics Review Unit (SERU). With a focus on ethical research practices and scientific integrity, she contributes to the review and oversight of research protocols, ensuring that studies meet both ethical standards and scientific rigor. 
 She holds a Master degree in Global Bioethics from Anahuac University, Mexico and a Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology from Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (2010). She also holds a Certificate in Clinical Ethics from Centre for Bioethics and Culture, Sindh Institute of Urology and Transplantation (CBEC-SIUT) Pakistan (2014). 
Victoria is dedicated to advancing health research in Kenya, fostering ethical research environments, and promoting public trust in scientific endeavors. Outside of her professional work, Victoria enjoys hiking, reading, and exploring new cultures through travel, which helps her stay inspired and connected to diverse perspectives.

speakers.name: Thea Marie Drachen
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Thea Marie Drachen is a research librarian at the University Library of Southern Denmark. She has more than a decade of experience in the field of research integrity, with a particular focus on Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR). Her work encompasses both teaching and practice, and she has played a key role in developing and delivering RCR training for researchers at various career stages. Thea has contributed to an international research project on RCR and Questionable Research Practices (QRP), and along with colleagues she was instrumental in founding the Danish RCR Network, which fosters collaboration and knowledge exchange among professionals working with research integrity. In addition to her work in research integrity, Thea has worked in systematic reviews and bibliometric analyses, which she has applied in support of evidence-based research and strategic research evaluation, and along with colleagues she was instrumental in founding the Danish RCR Network, which fosters collaboration and knowledge exchange among professionals working with research integrity.

speakers.name: Gustaaf Cornelis
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Born in Belgium (1966), he studied philosophy of science in Brussles, Ghent and Athens. PhD  in 1995 on the philosophy of relativistic cosmology. Nowadays as professor teaching scientific integrity at the universities of Brussels and Antwerp and performing research on ethics and generative AI. Member of different ethical committees.

speakers.name: Adira Daniel
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
Adira Daniel is a PhD Candidate in Social, Personality, and Developmental Psychology at the University of Western Ontario, Canada. Her research explores how intercultural and diverse couples navigate relational identity, disclosure, and resilience while facing stigma and marginalization. Guided by feminist and intersectional perspectives, she is especially interested in how processes such as “we-ness” and cognitive interdependence shape relationship well-being across cultural contexts.

Alongside her dissertation, Adira contributes to applied projects on newcomer integration, disability and inclusion, and the hidden curriculum in research methods. She is deeply engaged in metaresearch, open science, and methodological innovation, with a focus on strengthening rigour in mixed-methods psychology.

Adira’s work is driven by a commitment to inclusive, community-focused research that connects theory with practice. She is active in knowledge mobilization networks and regularly develops accessible resources, teaching materials, and collaborative tools that foster equity, transparency, and meaningful participation in research.

speakers.name: Vidar Enebakk
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: The National Committees for Research Ethics, Norway
speakers.bio: Vidar Enebakk is Director of The National Committee for Research Ethics in Social Sciences and Humanities (NESH) since 2014. He has a PhD in science studies from 2005 and was both postdoc and researcher in history of science at the University of Oslo until 2012. During this period, he was also visiting student and visiting scholar at the Department of History and Philosophy of Science (HPS), University of Cambridge. In 2012, he started as senior curator at the Norwegian Museum of Science and Technology, and from 2016 to 2018 he was editor of the Norwegian academic journal Nytt Norsk Tidsskrift. Enebakk has co-edited two books on research ethics (in Norwegian), he has served on the programme committee for the ENRIO Congress in 2021, 2023 and 2025, and since 2023 he is also a member of the Commission for Research Integrity (CRI) within The Luxembourg Agency for Research Integrity (LARI).

speakers.name: Muhammet Demirbilek
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Prof. Dr. Muhammet Demirbilek is a distinguished academic with expertise in educational technology. He currently serves as a professor in the Department of Educational Sciences within the Faculty of Education at Suleyman Demirel University. Prof. Demirbilek holds a Ph.D. in Curriculum & Instruction with a specialization in Educational Technology, and a Master’s in the same field from the School of Education at the University of Florida. Additionally, he earned both B.S. and M.S. degrees in Electronics Engineering from Istanbul University.
Prof. Demirbilek's career is marked by significant contributions to the field of educational technology and his interdisciplinary approach spans artificial intelligence, machine learning, educational game design, gamification, mobile media, social networks, and virtual worlds. His global perspective is enriched by his work as a Post-Doctoral Researcher with the Games, Learning, and Society (GLS) research group at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, supported by The MacArthur Foundation.

speakers.name: Kari Steen-Johnsen
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Chair of NESH, Research Professor at ISF

speakers.name: Mette Eriksen
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Mette Brandt Eriksen is a research librarian and associate professor at the University Library of Southern Denmark. In addition to her work on systematic reviews and literature search, she has more than a decade of experience in the field of research integrity, with a particular focus on Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR). Her work spans both teaching and practice, and she has played a role in developing and delivering RCR training for researchers at various career stages. Mette has contributed to national and international research projects on RCR and Questionable Research Practices (QRP), and along with colleagues she was instrumental in founding the Danish RCR Network, which fosters collaboration and knowledge exchange among professionals working with research integrity. In addition, she serves as a member of the Research Ethics Committee at the University of Southern Denmark, where she supports ethical review processes and promotes best practices in research.

speakers.name: Marijke Van Vlasselaer
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
Marijke Van Vlasselaer (marijke.van.vlasselaer@vub.be; ORCID: 0009-0001-2434-3695) is a 
doctoral researcher at the Data Analytics Lab, Vrije Universiteit Brussel. Her research focuses on 
academic integrity, generative AI in higher education, and policy reform in higher education. She also 
works on moral emotions, the philosophy of (alternative) education, and the didactics of philosophy.

sessions.name: 4.A - Symposium: Towards an Indigenous African Code of Conduct for Research Integrity
sessions.details:
Integrity is the cornerstone of research. In Africa, where research has been associated with systemic injustices, atrocities and power imbalances, research integrity can strengthen public trust through enhanced transparency, honesty and accountability. Other than the Ubuntu philosophy which embodies humanity, interconnectedness and collective responsibility, little is known regarding the systems, structures and procedures used to promote research integrity and deal with research misconduct and questionable research practices. ARIN is a network of like-minded individuals aiming at advancing, nurturing, and sustaining a culture of research integrity in African scholars. Although the Cape Town Statement on Research Integrity produced after the 7th WCRI exists, the extent to which it has been institutionalised across Africa remains unclear. The proposed symposium aims to engage diverse key stakeholders and experts in discussing the development of an indigenous African code of conduct for research integrity. Experts will present the state of affairs regarding research integrity in Africa, including the contribution of the Ubuntu philosophy and critical steps for developing a comprehensive African code of conduct. Speakers from diverse backgrounds will discuss challenges and opportunities associated with creating a code of conduct for research integrity, with a special focus on diverse African cultural and research practices.

Published literature suggests an increase in the volume of research being conducted in Africa. Furthermore, research output from Africa is consumed globally. Despite these trends, important systems for documenting the everyday practices of researchers and nurturing responsible conduct of research practices among researchers in Africa are limited and fragmented. This lack of structures and systems to document and monitor the everyday practices of researchers, especially research misconduct, raises concerns about the trustworthiness and accountability of African research output. We suspect that the lack of an indigenous  African code of conduct for research integrity could exacerbate this situation. The proposed symposium will lay the ground for developing a code of conduct for research integrity in Africa, and similar settings. The session will bring speakers from diverse African backgrounds to discuss the state of affairs in their various regions and the role Indigenous values and philosophies such as Ubuntu can play in this discourse. External experts will also be invited to share their experiences of developing various codes of conduct, the processes they used and the challenges they faced. Through this diverse engagement, the plenary will contribute to developing a blueprint for creating a comprehensive indigenous African code of conduct for research integrity. Ultimately, such a code will provide a much-needed standard and framework for responsible conduct of research, including management of research misconduct for institutions, scholars and researchers in Africa and other similar settings. 

TIME SCHEDULE
10:30am - 10:35am: Africa’s Research Integrity landscape-Key considerations for an indigenous African code of conduct for research integrity - Francis Kombe 
10:35am - 10:50am: From Compliance to Coherence - Eleni Flack-Davison
10:50am - 11:05am: Developing a code of conduct for research integrity-The case of the Netherlands - Prof Jonathan Soeharno 
11:05am - 11:20am: Peru and its Code of Research Integrity - Ms Roxana Lescano 
10:20am - 11:35am: The Asia Pacific Economic Cooperation’s Guiding Principles for Research Integrity - Dr Daniel Barr 
11:35am - 12:00pm: Q&A

speakers.name: Jonathan Soeharno
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio:
Prof. dr. mr. Jonathan Soeharno is full professor of administration of justice and legal philosophy at the University of Amsterdam.

He holds degrees in law (PhD, LLM, LLB), theology (MA, BA) and philosophy (MA, BA) and has noted expertise on the rule of law, ethics and integrity. Prof. Soeharno is also practicing lawyer (Of Counsel), deputy judge at the Court of Appeal ‘s-Hertogenbosch (commerce chamber) and a former Senator of the State of The Netherlands. Among others, he is the author of The Integrity of the Judge (Ashgate 2009), Introduction to Logic for Lawyers (2021) and The Value of the Oath (2020).

Prof. Soeharno has worked on rule of law projects involving the judiciary and lawyers in, among others, Ukraine, Russia, Suriname, Curacao and Indonesia.

speakers.name: Francis Kombe
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Francis Kombe is the current Chief Executive Officer, EthiXPERT, South Africa and a Public Health practitioner and bioethicist, with a passion in Research Integrity. He has a wealth of experience working in international health research institutions, where held various leadership positions. Kombe holds an MSocSc (research ethics) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal (UKZN), South Africa; Master of Public Health (MPH) and Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health (PGDPH) from London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine-UK (LSHTM) and is currently awaiting to graduate from a Wellcome Trust funded PhD at UKZN focusing on the implications of fieldworkers’ institutional support systems for research integrity practices in Africa.

He is a co-founder and chairperson of the African Research Integrity Network (ARIN), a member of the USA National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine (NASEM) and the Strategic Advisory Board for the EU’s AI ethics in human cognition and behaviour project. He served as a member of the programme planning committee during the 6th and 7th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong and Cape Town respectively. He has published widely in the field of research ethics, community engagement, frontline staff and research integrity among others.

speakers.name: Eleni Flack-Davison
speakers.title: Ms
speakers.position: Head: Research Legal, Compliance, Ethics And Integrity
speakers.bio:
Eleni Flack-Davison, an admitted attorney of South Africa, Legal Adviser, Research Compliance Manager, Head: Office of Research Integrity and the Research Data Protection Officer at the University of the Witwatersrand, Johannesburg. She is one of the founding fellows of the Wits MIND institute. Eleni is part of the SARIMA Research Management Portfolio Committee.

Eleni was a special adviser to the LOC for the 7th World Conference on Research Integrity in Cape Town. She is part of the REASA Executive Committee and heads up the Marketing and Communications sub-Committee. She has been part of the ASSAf drafting Committee in relation to the POPIA Code of Conduct for Research in South Africa.

Eleni co-founded and co-Chairs the Northern Regions Research Ethics and Integrity Community of Practice (CoP) under SARIMA and co-Chairs the Steering Committee for the CoPs for Research Ethics and Integrity. The CoP has drafted ethics guidelines and toolkit for the SADC Region in terms of the Nagoya Protocol. Eleni is part of the SARIMA Research Management Portfolio Committee. Eleni is a Certified VIRT2UE Research Integrity Trainer with the Embassy of Good science under the EU and Amsterdam UMC.

speakers.name: Roxana Lescano
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Ms. Lescano has worked at the U.S. Naval Medical Research Unit SOUTH (NAMRU SOUTH) for over 35 years and since 2005, as the Head of the Research Administration Program. Under this position, she has founded and later coordinated the Scientific Review Board, the Research Ethics Committee, and the Human Subjects Protection Training Program. More recently, her responsibilities have increased to include the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC), among other functions. Ms. Lescano is an attorney and holds a Masters in Bioethics, in addition to having completed the course work for the Masters in Epidemiological Research. Her experience extends from IRB administration to ethical oversight of IRB approved studies, in addition to supporting NAMRU SOUTH and other Peruvian organizations to develop their institutional policies and procedures, and their education programs in the field of human subject protections, scientific integrity and animal welfare in research. Ms. Lescano has also focused on research integrity education programs for over 10 years, preparing modules for the original Peruvian course on Responsible Conduct of Research, coordinating curricula for local courses, as well as workshops for undergrad and post-graduate programs, and participating as a guest speaker in academic and research organizations in Peru and neighboring countries. Additionally, Ms. Lescano has led and supported several research projects in this field. From 2019 to date, she has been a member of the organizing committee of the World Conferences of Research Integrity conducted in Hong Kong in 2019, Cape Town, South Africa in 2022, and in Athens, Greece, this coming June 2024.

speakers.name: Daniel Barr
speakers.title: Dr
speakers.bio: Dr Daniel Barr is the Principal Research Integrity Advisor at RMIT University, Australia, specialising in responsible research governance, management, and teaching. He contributes globally to research integrity through roles with the World Conferences on Research Integrity Foundation, the Asia-Pacific Research Integrity Network, and the Australian Academy of Science. Dr Barr co-authored the APEC Guiding Principles for Research Integrity and co-chaired the 6th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong. In 2021, Dr Barr was awarded the Paul Taylor Medal for Excellence in Research Integrity by the Australasian Research Management Society.

speakers.name: Limbanazo Matandika
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Limbanazo Mindiera Matandika, PhD, is a newly elected Advisory Member of the African Research Integrity Network. She has served as an Interim Steering Committee member since 2015 and co-founded Africa’s first network dedicated to promoting responsible research conduct in Africa. A bioethicist and social scientist by training, Dr. Matandika obtained her PhD in bioethics (2023) from the University of Malawi, College of Medicine. She holds a Master of Social Science degree in Health Research Ethics (2017) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal in South Africa and a master’s in public health degree (2014) from the University of Malawi, College of Medicine. Dr. Matandika currently works as a Research Ethics Committee Administrator at the Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences. She is active in bioethics and research integrity leadership both in Malawi and globally, having developed an online module on Research Integrity and currently serving as a Research Integrity Ambassador for Malawi. Currently she’s serving as a guest editor with the Journal of Clinical Epidemiology under the methodological aspects of research integrity culture series. This series aims to cover a broad range of methodological issues of relevance to research integrity, scientific misconduct, and questionable research practices.She has received various travel grants to present as a keynote speaker and panellist at international conferences, reflecting on research ethics and integrity from a low- and middle-income setting. Her research interests include conflict of interest, authorship, decolonization of global health, embedded ethics, and clinical ethics.

speakers.name: Francis Kombe
speakers.title: Mr
speakers.bio: Limbanazo Mindiera Matandika, PhD, is a newly elected Advisory Member of the African Research Integrity Network. She has served as an Interim Steering Committee member since 2015 and co-founded Africa’s first network dedicated to promoting responsible research conduct in Africa. A bioethicist and social scientist by training, Dr. Matandika obtained her PhD in bioethics (2023) from the University of Malawi, College of Medicine. She holds a Master of Social Science degree in Health Research Ethics (2017) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal in South Africa and a master’s in public health degree (2014) from the University of Malawi, College of Medicine. Dr. Matandika currently works as a Research Ethics Committee Administrator at the Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences. She is active in bioethics and research integrity leadership both in Malawi and globally, having developed an online module on Research Integrity and currently serving as a Research Integrity Ambassador for Malawi. Currently she’s serving as a guest editor with the Journal of Clinical Epidemiology under the methodological aspects of research integrity culture series. This series aims to cover a broad range of methodological issues of relevance to research integrity, scientific misconduct, and questionable research practices.She has received various travel grants to present as a keynote speaker and panellist at international conferences, reflecting on research ethics and integrity from a low- and middle-income setting. Her research interests include conflict of interest, authorship, decolonization of global health, embedded ethics, and clinical ethics.

presentations.title: Africa’s Research Integrity landscape-Key Considerations for an Indigenous African Code of Conduct for Research Integrity

speakers.name: Francis Kombe
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Francis Kombe is the current Chief Executive Officer, EthiXPERT, South Africa and a Public Health practitioner and bioethicist, with a passion in Research Integrity. He has a wealth of experience working in international health research institutions, where held various leadership positions. Kombe holds an MSocSc (research ethics) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal (UKZN), South Africa; Master of Public Health (MPH) and Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health (PGDPH) from London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine-UK (LSHTM) and is currently awaiting to graduate from a Wellcome Trust funded PhD at UKZN focusing on the implications of fieldworkers’ institutional support systems for research integrity practices in Africa.

He is a co-founder and chairperson of the African Research Integrity Network (ARIN), a member of the USA National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine (NASEM) and the Strategic Advisory Board for the EU’s AI ethics in human cognition and behaviour project. He served as a member of the programme planning committee during the 6th and 7th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong and Cape Town respectively. He has published widely in the field of research ethics, community engagement, frontline staff and research integrity among others.

presentations.title: From Compliance to Coherence

speakers.name: Eleni Flack-Davison
speakers.title: Ms
speakers.position: Head: Research Legal, Compliance, Ethics And Integrity
speakers.bio:
Eleni Flack-Davison, an admitted attorney of South Africa, Legal Adviser, Research Compliance Manager, Head: Office of Research Integrity and the Research Data Protection Officer at the University of the Witwatersrand, Johannesburg. She is one of the founding fellows of the Wits MIND institute. Eleni is part of the SARIMA Research Management Portfolio Committee.

Eleni was a special adviser to the LOC for the 7th World Conference on Research Integrity in Cape Town. She is part of the REASA Executive Committee and heads up the Marketing and Communications sub-Committee. She has been part of the ASSAf drafting Committee in relation to the POPIA Code of Conduct for Research in South Africa.

Eleni co-founded and co-Chairs the Northern Regions Research Ethics and Integrity Community of Practice (CoP) under SARIMA and co-Chairs the Steering Committee for the CoPs for Research Ethics and Integrity. The CoP has drafted ethics guidelines and toolkit for the SADC Region in terms of the Nagoya Protocol. Eleni is part of the SARIMA Research Management Portfolio Committee. Eleni is a Certified VIRT2UE Research Integrity Trainer with the Embassy of Good science under the EU and Amsterdam UMC.

presentations.title: Developing a Code of Conduct for Research Integrity - The Case of the Netherlands

speakers.name: Jonathan Soeharno
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio:
Prof. dr. mr. Jonathan Soeharno is full professor of administration of justice and legal philosophy at the University of Amsterdam.

He holds degrees in law (PhD, LLM, LLB), theology (MA, BA) and philosophy (MA, BA) and has noted expertise on the rule of law, ethics and integrity. Prof. Soeharno is also practicing lawyer (Of Counsel), deputy judge at the Court of Appeal ‘s-Hertogenbosch (commerce chamber) and a former Senator of the State of The Netherlands. Among others, he is the author of The Integrity of the Judge (Ashgate 2009), Introduction to Logic for Lawyers (2021) and The Value of the Oath (2020).

Prof. Soeharno has worked on rule of law projects involving the judiciary and lawyers in, among others, Ukraine, Russia, Suriname, Curacao and Indonesia.

presentations.title: Peru and its Code of Research Integrity

speakers.name: Roxana Lescano
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Ms. Lescano has worked at the U.S. Naval Medical Research Unit SOUTH (NAMRU SOUTH) for over 35 years and since 2005, as the Head of the Research Administration Program. Under this position, she has founded and later coordinated the Scientific Review Board, the Research Ethics Committee, and the Human Subjects Protection Training Program. More recently, her responsibilities have increased to include the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC), among other functions. Ms. Lescano is an attorney and holds a Masters in Bioethics, in addition to having completed the course work for the Masters in Epidemiological Research. Her experience extends from IRB administration to ethical oversight of IRB approved studies, in addition to supporting NAMRU SOUTH and other Peruvian organizations to develop their institutional policies and procedures, and their education programs in the field of human subject protections, scientific integrity and animal welfare in research. Ms. Lescano has also focused on research integrity education programs for over 10 years, preparing modules for the original Peruvian course on Responsible Conduct of Research, coordinating curricula for local courses, as well as workshops for undergrad and post-graduate programs, and participating as a guest speaker in academic and research organizations in Peru and neighboring countries. Additionally, Ms. Lescano has led and supported several research projects in this field. From 2019 to date, she has been a member of the organizing committee of the World Conferences of Research Integrity conducted in Hong Kong in 2019, Cape Town, South Africa in 2022, and in Athens, Greece, this coming June 2024.

presentations.title: The Asia Pacific Economic Cooperation’s Guiding Principles for Research Integrity

speakers.name: Daniel Barr
speakers.title: Dr
speakers.bio: Dr Daniel Barr is the Principal Research Integrity Advisor at RMIT University, Australia, specialising in responsible research governance, management, and teaching. He contributes globally to research integrity through roles with the World Conferences on Research Integrity Foundation, the Asia-Pacific Research Integrity Network, and the Australian Academy of Science. Dr Barr co-authored the APEC Guiding Principles for Research Integrity and co-chaired the 6th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong. In 2021, Dr Barr was awarded the Paul Taylor Medal for Excellence in Research Integrity by the Australasian Research Management Society.

speakers.name: Francis Kombe
speakers.title: Mr.

speakers.name: Seán Lacey
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr Seán Lacey received his BSc in Mathematical Sciences and Computing from the University of Limerick in 2003, followed by a PhD in Applied Mathematics in 2007. He has lectured at numerous Higher Education Institutions in Ireland. In March 2011, he joined the Department of Mathematics at Cork Institute of Technology, where he served as Chair of the Research & Innovation Committee (2016–2020) and Chair of the Department of Mathematics Research Committee (2016–2021). He was also a member of the National Forum for the Enhancement of Teaching and Learning in Higher Education Research Ethics Committee (2019–2021) and the National Research Ethics Committee (2023–2025). In 2022, he completed an MBA in Educational Leadership (International) at the Institute of Education, University College London.

In June 2021, Seán was promoted to Senior Lecturer at Munster Technological University (MTU, formerly Cork Institute of Technology). In October that year, he was elected the inaugural Chair of MTU’s Research Council. In January 2022, he was appointed as the University’s Research Integrity & Compliance Officer (RICO). In this role, Seán is responsible for fostering a research environment that upholds the highest standards of integrity, leading the development, implementation, and review of research-related policies and procedures. Working with relevant offices and postholders, he ensures that the University’s research training emphasises the importance of research integrity, promotes responsible conduct of research, discourages research misconduct, and raises awareness of the consequences of same.

Seán has played a pivotal role in shaping MTU’s research governance. He oversaw the creation of its Human Research Ethics Committee in 2022, and the Animal Research and Education Ethics Committee in 2025. He also served as co-lead for the European Commission-supported Network for Education and Research Quality, with a focus on Research Supervision and Leadership. 

With over 50 peer-reviewed publications in partnership with local, national, and international collaborators, Seán is an active researcher and mentor. He is currently supervising two PhD students and has successfully guided six MSc students to completion. His expertise in statistical analysis has made him the lead statistician on more than 40 industry collaboration studies, and he has designed and delivered over 70 Continuing Professional Development (CPD) workshops for industry professionals.

speakers.name: Kaixiang Kang
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Kaixiang Kang is a Specially Appointed Assistant Professor at the Higher Education Promotion Organization of the Prefectural University of Hiroshima. His research interests focus on higher education, and he is dedicated to using simulation methods such as Agent-Based Modeling (ABM) to explain various phenomena in the field.
He is currently a doctoral candidate in Higher Education at Hiroshima University's Graduate School of Humanities and Social Sciences, where he also earned his master's degree. Since April 2024, he has held his current position at the Prefectural University of Hiroshima.

speakers.name: Simon Fuglsang
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Simon Fuglsang studies the relationship between science and society. His primary focus is the public's relationship to science, attitudes towards science, knowledge of science, and the relationships between these aspects.

speakers.name: Signe Mežinska
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Signe Mežinska is a bioethicist and sociologist with an MSc in Bioethics (Union Graduate College, US) and PhD in Sociology of Health and Illness (Riga Stradins University, Latvia). She teaches at the University of Latvia, Faculty of Medicine and Life Sciences, with over 25 years of experience in medical ethics and sociology of health and illness. Her research focuses on ethics in biobanking, psychiatry, reproductive health, and rational use of medicines. She has led research ethics training courses internationally, serves for several research ethics committees and  international bodies, including UNESCO’s International Bioethics Committee (Vice-Chair 2017–2021), Council of Europe’s CDBIO, and WHO’s COVID-19 Ethics and Governance group. She currently leads work packages in Horizon Europe projects BEYOND and FAMILY and has contributed to VIRT2UE, ROSiE, B1MG, EDITH projects and several COST actions.

speakers.name: Malcolm Macleod
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.position: Professor Of Neurology And Translational Neurosciences
speakers.bio: Prof Malcolm Macleod co-founded the CAMARADES collaboration in 2005 and is now at the University of Edinburgh as Professor of Neurology and Translational Neuroscience and co-Director of Edinbrugh Neuroscience. With Saran, he was co-Chair or the ESIC Working Group on Safe and Responsible Use of AI in Evidence Synthesis.

speakers.name: Lodewijk Pet
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
I am a PhD candidate in the Department of Clinical Epidemiology at Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC), the Netherlands. My research focuses on the Quality and Integrity of Biomedical Sciences.

Alongside my research, I teach epidemiological methods to students and PhD candidates, and I develop large language model (LLM) tools to support research practices.

Trained as a medical doctor with a passion for coding, I have built numerous websites and tools over the past decade using HTML, JavaScript, CSS, and Python for both professional and personal use.

I combine my expertise in medicine, coding, and epidemiology to enhance scientific quality throughout the entire research process from idea generation to post-publication evaluation. My goal is to enhance the trustworthiness and efficiency of science for all stakeholders.

speakers.name: Irene Pasquetto
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: I’m a scholar in the field of information and communication studies. I research contemporary knowledge production practices, with a focus on how digital technologies shape and mediate them. My most recent research work centers on knowledge production in the context of open science and public participation in science. I’m an Assistant Professor at the University of Maryland College of Information Studies. Previously, I was an Assistant Professor at the University of Michigan School of Information, where I taught ethics of information technologies and digital curation. I’m also a Senior Research Fellow at the Shorenstein Center on Media, Politics, and Public Policy, at the Harvard Kennedy School, where I co-founded the Harvard Kennedy School Misinformation Review in 2019.

speakers.name: Victor Benjamin Oshodi
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.organization: Association For The Advancement Of Artificial Intelligence
speakers.bio:
Victor Oshodi is an accomplished policy and technology strategist, business consultant, and AI governance advocate with over a decade of experience advancing innovation, education, and international development across Africa. He serves as the Country Director of the Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI) – Nigeria, where he leads initiatives on AI safety, ethics, and governance, bridging research, policy, and community engagement.He has represented Nigeria at international conferences hosted by The Alan Turing Institute (UK), the University of Leeds, and other global research institutions, where he has presented research on AI governance, trustworthy digital infrastructure, and sustainable economic transformation.

Passionate about inclusive innovation, Victor is dedicated to shaping Africa’s contribution to global AI policy and governance, promoting ethical adoption of emerging technologies, and building collaborative frameworks that connect education, innovation, and economic growth.

speakers.name: Teresa Ottinger
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: I am head of the grant application unit at the Swedish Research Council, with long experience in research grant funding and peer-review processes. I hold a special interest in research integrity issues and am part of the working group on research ethics and integrity at the Council.

speakers.name: Sue Russell
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
Sue Russell is Senior Funding Policy Advisor in Funding Operations and Governance at Wellcome based in the UK, having joined in 2025. Wellcome is a global charitable foundation that works towards everyone benefitting from science’s potential to improve health and save lives. 
Sue’s role at Wellcome spans a number of funding policy areas including promoting research integrity. She sits on the signatories group for the UK Concordat for Research Integrity and has been involved in rewriting the Concordat and drafting annual statement templates required under the Concordat.
Sue joined Wellcome in 2025, following 10 years in different roles leading funding policies and in grant management roles at Cancer Research UK (CRUK). Before that, Sue spent a further 10 years at various international development funders and non-governmental organisations in programmatic and funding roles, focusing on improving health and disaster relief across many low- and middle-income countries.

sessions.name: Tuesday Lunch and Poster Meet and Greet
Plenary Session #3 - Championing Research Integrity: Insights from the Einstein Foundation Award Winners
sessions.details:
The Einstein Foundation Award shares with the WCRI the common goal of enhancing the integrity and openness of research. Winners of the Einstein Foundation Award for Promoting Quality in Research will present their impactful work advancing research integrity. This plenary session will offer a unique opportunity to hear from three prestigious award winners focused on improving the scientific knowledge base through open science, post-publication peer review and error detection. The Center for Open Science (COS), recipient of the inaugural Institutional Award in 2021, will be represented by Timothy Errington, Senior Director of Research. He will share insights on how COS is working to make research processes, content, and outcomes more openly accessible.
Brandon Stell, co-founder of the PubPeer platform—the winner of the 2024 Institutional Award—will present on how it elevates scientific standards through community-driven assessment. Finally, Elisabeth Bik, honored with the 2024 Individual Award for her pioneering work in image forensics, will discuss the prevalence of errors and misconduct in biomedical research. The €350,000 Einstein Foundation Award for Promoting Quality in Research (https://award.einsteinfoundation.de/) honors individuals, institutions, and early career researchers worldwide who have made significant contributions to advancing the rigor, transparency, and reproducibility of science. This plenary session directly addresses the conference theme of "Research Security & Artificial Intelligence" by exploring the significant work the three past winners do to safeguard research integrity. In particular, Elisabeth Bik who has analyzed thousands of scientific images will discuss the risks posed by AI-generated images. PubPeer plays a pivotal role in strengthening the scientific knowledge base by enabling scholarly discourse on published research. Through post-publication peer review, it helps detect errors, correct the literature, and uphold research integrity. Finally, the Center for Open Science empowers the global research community with tools to make the research process more transparent, accessible, collaborative, and verifiable, enhancing the overall security and trustworthiness of scientific work. These presentations will highlight the intersection of research integrity, AI, and the ongoing need for secure, transparent, and reproducible science. Moreover, all three awardees serve as powerful role models, exemplifying how dedication to responsible research practices can drive positive change. Their work highlights the importance of engaging with and championing research integrity, motivating attendees to continue their efforts in fostering transparency, accountability, and reproducibility in their own research. By sharing their experiences, the awardees will inspire others to follow their lead, fostering a global dialogue that transcends disciplinary and cultural boundaries. speakers.name: Timothy Errington
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Tim Errington is the Senior Director of Research at the Center for Open Science (COS) that aims to increase openness, integrity, and reproducibility of scientific research. In that position he conducts and collaborates with researchers and stakeholders across scientific disciplines and organizations on metascience projects aimed to understand the current research process and evaluate initiatives designed to increase reproducibility and openness of scientific research. These include large scale replication and reproducibility projects such as the Reproducibility Project: Cancer Biology and the DARPA supported Systematizing Confidence in Open Research and Evidence (SCORE), and evaluation projects of new initiatives such as open science badges, Registered Reports, automated confidence assessment, and pilot programs to increase rigor and openness of research.

speakers.name: Mai Har Sham
speakers.title: Prof
speakers.organization: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
speakers.bio: Professor Sham is a Pro-Vice-Chancellor of The Chinese University of Hong Kong and Choh-Ming Li Professor of Biomedical Sciences. Professor Sham has delivered plenary and keynote lectures on the roles of universities on research integrity issues in different continents. She was an organizer of the Asia-Pacific Research Integrity Network meeting in Hong Kong in 2017, the Co-Chair for the 6th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong in 2019. Professor Sham obtained her PhD in Biochemistry from the University of Cambridge. She received her postdoctoral training in Developmental Genetics in the National Institute for Medical Research in London, U.K. She had previously served as the Associate Vice-President (Research); Assistant Dean (Research), Head of the Department of Biochemistry, Director of the Centre for Reproduction Development and Growth in the Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine, the University of Hong Kong.

speakers.name: Brandon Stell
speakers.title: Dr
speakers.bio:
Brandon Stell is a neuroscientist with the CNRS in Paris, France. His research explores how the brain coordinates and adapts movement in response to a changing environment. He earned his undergraduate degree in biology from the University of Colorado and completed his PhD in 2005 at UCLA, where he trained with Istvan Mody. After his doctoral work, he moved to Paris as a postdoctoral researcher and has held a permanent position with the CNRS since 2014.

In 2012, in his spare time, he co-founded PubPeer, a website dedicated to the community-based evaluation of scientific publications, which he now runs with fellow CNRS researcher Boris Barbour. Together, they also lead the nonprofit PubPeer Foundation, which supports the platform. This work is carried out independently of their CNRS roles.

speakers.name: Elisabeth Bik
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Elisabeth Bik, PhD, is a Dutch-American microbiologist who has worked for 15 years at Stanford University and 2 years in industry. Since 2019, she is a science integrity volunteer and consultant who scans the biomedical literature for images or other data of concern. She has found over 8,000 problematic scientific papers. Her work has resulted in over 1,300 retractions and another 1,000 corrections. For her work on exposing threats to research integrity, she received the 2021 John Maddox Prize and the 2024 Einstein Foundation Award.
