Furthermore, she is a committed Non-Executive Member of various advisory and supervisory boards across industries and countries. Here, she contributes especially with legal, operational and risk management experience and in the fields ESG, Women Rights and Innovation & Technology worldwide.

Her professional activities are strongly related to the implementation of the Agenda 2030, because for her it's of utmost importance to find solutions for our living together in the "The Fourth Industrial Revolution" by implementing the 17 Sustainable Development Goals of the United Nations (SGDs).

speakers.name: Gengyan Tang
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Gengyan Tang, MA, is a PhD student in the Werklund School of Education at the University of Calgary. His research interests include research integrity, academic integrity, scholarly publishing, and higher education. Prior to his studies at the UCalgary, Gengyan lived in Mainland China, where he was concerned about the increasingly serious issue of research integrity, especially in the field of medicine, and has published some related papers. Gengyan currently serves as a peer reviewer for academic journals such as Accountability in Research, Journal of Scholarly Publishing, Journal of Librarianship an

speakers.name: Tanvi Singh
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Tanvi Singh holds a  M.Sc. in Biotechnology. She began her research career at the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) Headquarters, contributing to COVID-19 clinical data management, and later worked as a Scientist at ICMR–National Institute of Malaria Research, focusing on mosquito omics datasets and data organization systems. Currently, she serves as the Research Integrity Officer at the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS), where she supports institutional data governance, integrity compliance, and reproducibility initiatives. Her background spans molecular biology, biostatistics, and entomology, with ongoing research on the behavioral biology of Anopheles mosquitoes. She is also one of two Research Integrity professionals currently serving within the Government of India, contributing to national efforts to strengthen research transparency and data stewardship.

speakers.name: Baptiste Vendé
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: After earning a Master’s degree in Philosophy of Science from Leibniz University Hannover, Baptiste Vendé continued his studies as a PhD candidate. His thesis focuses on the intersection of scientific integrity, evidence-based policy, and causality. Funded by a COFRA grant, he is a student at Sorbonne Université while primarily working at his administrative employer, the Ofis (French office for research integrity).

speakers.name: COLBY VORLAND
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: Indiana University School of Public Health-Bloomington
speakers.bio: Colby Vorland is an assistant research scientist at the Indiana University School of Public Health-Bloomington. His research focuses on error detection and correction, semi-automated methods to improve research quality, reproducibility in science, reporting quality, and other meta-research primarily related to nutrition, obesity, and aging. He is committed to enhancing the transparency and integrity of scientific research. He contributes to initiatives aimed at improving methodological standards through statistical support, education, and technological solutions.

speakers.name: Amanda Sulicz
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Amanda Sulicz is the Associate Program Manager, Publishing Ethics at IEEE. In this role, Amanda is responsible for upholding the integrity of the scholarly record by assisting with investigations and working with IEEE’s volunteers to resolve ethics and plagiarism allegations.

Prior to joining IEEE in 2023, Amanda started her publishing career in 2019 at AIP Publishing. Initially, she was as an Associate Editor where she made first editorial decisions and handled the peer review process for manuscripts submitted to AIPP’s flagship journal, Applied Physics Reviews. While serving as an Associate Editor, Amanda was involved in expanding the journal portfolio through the launch of two new titles, Biophysics Reviews and Chemical Physics Reviews. In 2021, Amanda transitioned into the role of Journal Manager for AIP Advances where she was responsible for the journal’s strategic and financial success. Simultaneously, Amanda worked to support the expansion of AIPP’s Open Access portfolio by helping to launch 3 new Gold Open Access journals.

Before starting her career in publishing, Amanda worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher in the lab of Dr. Kirk S. Schanze upon the completion of her Ph.D. in Chemistry from Case Western Reserve University in 2017.

speakers.name: Michael Streeter
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Mike Streeter is Director of Research Integrity Strategy & Policy at Wiley, leading a cross-functional team representing a range of publishing services that support publication ethics and integrity.  He champions strategic alignment for research integrity and proactive policy development across our Research Publishing organization and with our external partners. Mike is a member of COPE Council, the STM Integrity Hub Governance Board, United2Act, and the STM Research Integrity Committee. He amplifies Wiley’s mission through frequent scholarly communications partnerships and events.

speakers.name: Giulia Inguaggiato
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Giulia Inguaggiato, is Assistant professor at the department of Ethics Law and Humanities of Amsterdam University Medical Centre (NL). Her expertise lies in ethics, and research integrity with a focus on education, clinical ethics support and training.

speakers.name: Lex Bouter
speakers.title: Prof
speakers.position: Professor Emeritus of Methodology and Integrity
speakers.bio:
Lex Bouter is Professor Emeritus of Methodology and Integrity at the Department of Epidemiology 
and Data Science of the Amsterdam University Medical Centers and the Department of Philosophy 
of the Faculty of Humanities of the Vrije Universiteit. He is involved research and teaching on 
research integrity and open science topics. He was appointed as tenured Professor of Epidemiology
in 1992 and served his university as its rector between 2006 and 2013. Professor Bouter has 
supervised 81 PhD students, of whom to date 17 were appointed as professor. He is the founding 
chair of the World Conferences on Research Integrity Foundation.

speakers.name: Clement Adebamowo
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.position: Professor And Director Of Division Of Cancer Epidemiology & Associate Director, Pop. Science
speakers.organization: University Of Maryland School Of Medicine
speakers.bio:
I direct the Cancer Epidemiology Division at University of Maryland School of Medicine, Baltimore and several NIH funded international bioethics training programs. I am also Associate Director of the Population Sciences Program in our Cancer Center.
My research focuses the epidemiological and genomic risks, biomarkers discovery, early detection and prevention of breast, lung, and cervical cancers. I also conduct research on research ethics including comprehension of consent, consent in AD/ADRD, and the intersection between research and data science.

speakers.name: Tetsuji Iseda
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Graduated from Kyoto University in 1991, and received Ph. D. in philosophy from University of Maryland in 2001. Employed at Nagoya University in 1999, and have been teaching at Kyoto University since 2008.

speakers.name: Kalypso Iordanou
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Kalypso Iordanou is Professor of Psychology at UCLan Cyprus and Director of theThinkingLab. She holds a PhD in Psychology from Columbia University, New York. Her research focuses on argumentation skills, reasoning, my-side bias, epistemic cognition, metacognition, and particularly in using technology to support the development of critical thinking skills. She is currently part of the Horizon-Europe projects VERITY, SMIDGE and BEYOND, for promoting public trust in science, research integrity and tackle conspiratorial thinking in social media. . She has held sponsored visiting research positions at Columbia University (2017) as a Fulbright award recipient, and the University of Geneva (2025). She serves as Associate Editor of the Journal of Educational Psychology. She received the Cyprus Research Award-Young Researcher in Social Sciences and Humanities from the Research and Innovation Foundation in 2015 for her work on argumentation skills.

speakers.name: Barna Ganguly
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. (Mrs) BARNA GANGULY, joined in the year 1999 as Assistant Professor, has been working as Professor in the Department of Pharmacology, Pramukh Swami Medical College (PSMC), Karamsad, Gujarat since 2006. She passed M.B;B.S, from Calcutta University, M.D (Pharmacology) from Aligarh Muslim University,  PG Dip Bioethics from IGNOU supported by ICMR &NIH, Ph.D (Bioethics) from Faculty of Medicine, University of Porto, Portugal. She. She has got more than 32 years of teaching experience in medical colleges like J.N.Medical College, AMU, Manipal College of Medical Sciences, Pokhara, Nepal including that of current institution. As a post graduate teacher, she has guided 14 candidates for M.D. Pharmacology who have successfully completed the course. She has also guided six M.Pharm candidates, four candidates who were enrolled under her for Ph.D in Pharmacology. She underwent training on “Problem based Pharmacotherapy teaching – WHO model of P drug Concept” in Groningen, The Netherlands in 1999 and thereafter introduced the model for teaching Pharmacology for UG students in the department in 2000 which was the first of its kind in India. She was also trained in Clinical Pharmacology from PGI, Chandigarh in 1999. In the year 2015, PSMC got approval for establishment of UNESCO Bioethics Chair Unit (presently named as WMA support Centre of Bioethics Chair Program) with an objective of strengthening bioethics teaching and Dr. Ganguly is heading the same. She has been continuously engaged in ethics committee related  activities  within and outside the institution since 2003. She is a dedicated and recognized GCP trainer.
Her area of interest is Clinical Pharmacology and Bioethics. She has 52 publications (10 of them are on Bioethics) in various national and international journal of repute (including Q1) and 6 chapters in 3 books by national and international repute.

speakers.bio: Will White is the Scholarly Communications Librarian at the University of New Mexico's Health Sciences Library and Informatics Center. He was previously the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee's Health Sciences Librarian. Will co-presented with Dr. Leslie McIntosh on "A taxonomy of scholarly disinformation to enhance research and integrity" at the 8th WCRI in Athens.

speakers.name: Vincent Yuan
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: My name is Vincent and I am a medical student from Australia. My research interests are in spin and publication bias.

speakers.name: Sarah Patricia Wendt
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
As a researcher at the Robert K. Merton Center for Science Studies, Sarah examines social configurations and practices in research assessment, especially in the academic publishing system and peer review processes. Her focus is on qualitative social research, epistemic diversity and (historically grown) power relations within academia.

Sarah studied Global Studies at Humboldt University Berlin, University of Pretoria, Jawaharlal Nehru University New Delhi and FLACSO Argentina. The various places, institutions and local and global collectives in which she was and is involved, especially with regard to the decolonization of knowledge, shape her understanding of concepts of epistemic justice and epistemic diversity. From 2022 to 2024 she worked at the BIH QUEST Center for Responsible Research at the Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin on the evaluation of programs for the transformation of biomedical research and global health, as well as on topics related to open science and reproducibility of research.

speakers.name: Claudia Pallise Perello
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Claudia Pallise Perello (she/her) is a PhD candidate at the Amsterdam UMC with a background in biomedicine, public health, and bioethics. In her PhD thesis, she explores the role of epistemic injustice in the fields of research ethics and research integrity, combining empirical and theoretical approaches to identify and address these injustices. Claudia also teaches in the research integrity course at Amsterdam UMC and in the Master's program in Philosophy, Bioethics, and Health at the VU Amsterdam.

speakers.name: Anthony Oko-Isu
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Oko-Isu holds a Ph.D. in Agribusiness and Entrepreneurship Development, and he is currently pursuing an M.Sc. in Bioethics at the University of Ibadan. He is a distinguished scholar, policy advisor, and development consultant with over 13 years of experience at the intersection of agriculture, entrepreneurship, public policy, and data-driven development. He currently serves as Senior Lecturer and Head of the Department of Agribusiness and Management at Alex Ekwueme Federal University, Ndufu-Alike (AE-FUNAI), Ebonyi State, Nigeria.
He is a Postdoctoral Research Fellow of the Nigerian Institute of Social and Economic Research (NISER) and a globally recognized voice on food systems and data governance, having served as a member of the Technical Advisory Board of the Research Data Alliance (RDA-TAB) for Agriculture and Nutrition, representing Europe and Africa. Dr. Oko-Isu has participated in high-level research collaborations, trainings, and academic fellowships in Germany, Romania, India, and at Harvard Law School, USA.
He has made significant contributions to the domestication of the National Food and Nutrition Policy in Ebonyi State, led the development of the State's Costed Multi-Sectoral Plan of Action for Food and Nutrition, and facilitated capacity strengthening for the State Committee on Food and Nutrition. His teaching and policy advisory efforts are grounded in evidence-based approaches that emphasize the strategic use of quality data for transformative development.
Dr. Oko-Isu is a respected researcher and consultant with a rich portfolio of national and international projects supported by organizations such as UNICEF, USAID, and the European Union. His work spans agricultural value chain analysis, food and nutrition policy, and data-driven development planning.

speakers.name: Bernhard Sabel

speakers.organization: Otto-v.-Guericke University of Magdeburg & Sciii Foundation
speakers.bio:
Bernhard A. Sabel, PhD

Present position: Full-Professor of Medical Psychology, Medical Faculty, Otto-v.-Guericke University of Magdeburg, Germany (since 1992); med. director of SAVIR-Center

Main domain of research: Brain plasticity after cerebral lesions with focus on visual system damage, vision training and non-invasive microcurrent stimulation for vision restoration; visual system functional imaging; quality of life research in low vision and prediction models of vision restoration; Fake-publishing in science

2008-2010 Vice President for Research, Univ. Magdeburg (Germany)/ 
1998-99 Visiting Research Fellow, Dept. of Psychology, Princeton University, USA) / 
1991 Visiting Neuroscientist, Mass Gen Hospital, Harvard Med. School, Boston, USA/ 
1986-92 Research Scientist, Univ. Munich Med. School/

International Council of Ophthalmology (ICO), voting member (since 2023)

 
Selected publications: 

>270 SCI publications; 10 books; 19 patents; 14,000+ Citations, H-index: 67

Scientific achievements
•	Increasing our understanding mechanisms of recovery of function after eye and brain damage tby reactivation of neurons in partially damaged tissue.
•	Developed new therapies for blindness using vision training and neuromodulation.  It overcomes the long-held paradigm that blindness is irreversible. 
•	Discovered brain network reorganization processing speed at the millisecond scale which correlates with recovery because synchrony (“time”) in different brain regions (“space”) are tightly intertwined (“Spacetime of the brain”).

speakers.bio: Associate Prof Jodi Schneider is at the Information School, University of Wisconsin-Madison US, where her research explores information quality, with the ultimate goal of ensuring public policy is informed by the best available scientific evidence. Recently, she has studied how unreliable research lingers in science, often through accidental citations to retracted research.

Will co-presented with Dr. Leslie McIntosh on "A taxonomy of scholarly disinformation to enhance research and integrity" at the 8th WCRI in Athens.

speakers.bio: Associate Prof Jodi Schneider is at the Information School, University of Wisconsin-Madison US, where her research explores information quality, with the ultimate goal of ensuring public policy is informed by the best available scientific evidence. Recently, she has studied how unreliable research lingers in science, often through accidental citations to retracted research.

speakers.name: Sarah Patricia Wendt
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
As a researcher at the Robert K. Merton Center for Science Studies, Sarah examines social configurations and practices in research assessment, especially in the academic publishing system and peer review processes. Her focus is on qualitative social research, epistemic diversity and (historically grown) power relations within academia.

Sarah studied Global Studies at Humboldt University Berlin, University of Pretoria, Jawaharlal Nehru University New Delhi and FLACSO Argentina. The various places, institutions and local and global collectives in which she was and is involved, especially with regard to the decolonization of knowledge, shape her understanding of concepts of epistemic justice and epistemic diversity. From 2022 to 2024 she worked at the BIH QUEST Center for Responsible Research at the Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin on the evaluation of programs for the transformation of biomedical research and global health, as well as on topics related to open science and reproducibility of research.

presentations.title: The Stockholm Declaration for a Reformation of Science Publishing

speakers.name: Claudia Pallise Perello
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Claudia Pallise Perello (she/her) is a PhD candidate at the Amsterdam UMC with a background in biomedicine, public health, and bioethics. In her PhD thesis, she explores the role of epistemic injustice in the fields of research ethics and research integrity, combining empirical and theoretical approaches to identify and address these injustices. Claudia also teaches in the research integrity course at Amsterdam UMC and in the Master's program in Philosophy, Bioethics, and Health at the VU Amsterdam.

presentations.title: How Peer Pressure Influences Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) in Local and International Collaborative Research in Nigeria

speakers.name: Anthony Oko-Isu
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Oko-Isu holds a Ph.D. in Agribusiness and Entrepreneurship Development, and he is currently pursuing an M.Sc. in Bioethics at the University of Ibadan. He is a distinguished scholar, policy advisor, and development consultant with over 13 years of experience at the intersection of agriculture, entrepreneurship, public policy, and data-driven development. He currently serves as Senior Lecturer and Head of the Department of Agribusiness and Management at Alex Ekwueme Federal University, Ndufu-Alike (AE-FUNAI), Ebonyi State, Nigeria.
He is a Postdoctoral Research Fellow of the Nigerian Institute of Social and Economic Research (NISER) and a globally recognized voice on food systems and data governance, having served as a member of the Technical Advisory Board of the Research Data Alliance (RDA-TAB) for Agriculture and Nutrition, representing Europe and Africa. Dr. Oko-Isu has participated in high-level research collaborations, trainings, and academic fellowships in Germany, Romania, India, and at Harvard Law School, USA.
He has made significant contributions to the domestication of the National Food and Nutrition Policy in Ebonyi State, led the development of the State's Costed Multi-Sectoral Plan of Action for Food and Nutrition, and facilitated capacity strengthening for the State Committee on Food and Nutrition. His teaching and policy advisory efforts are grounded in evidence-based approaches that emphasize the strategic use of quality data for transformative development.
Dr. Oko-Isu is a respected researcher and consultant with a rich portfolio of national and international projects supported by organizations such as UNICEF, USAID, and the European Union. His work spans agricultural value chain analysis, food and nutrition policy, and data-driven development planning.

sessions.name: 7.A - Symposium: Balancing Editorial Gate-keeping and Open Research Approaches to Strengthen Research Integrity
sessions.details:
This panel discussion examines the evolving relationship between traditional editorial ‘gatekeeping’ mechanisms and emerging open research practices in protecting research integrity. There is an inherent tension between these two models with the former typically relying on editorial selection to filter out flawed or unethical research before it is published or indexed and the later favouring a more inclusive approach that relies on post-publication review to identity untrustworthy content. 

While editorial selection has long functioned as a quality control filter, it can face criticism for opacity and reinforcing the status quo. In contrast, open research promotes transparency and accessibility, but this approach can increase the risk of unverified or fraudulent findings entering the scholarly record. 

The session brings together stakeholders across the research ecosystem to explore the benefits and limitations of both models and address how we can best support trustworthy, equitable scholarship in a rapidly changing research landscape. The discussion aims to foster a critical yet constructive dialogue on how the principles of gatekeeping and openness can be balanced to strengthen research integrity. 

The integrity of the scholarly record is under threat as increasing volumes of compromised content is published, often via fraudulent entities such as paper mills. 

This session explores the strengths and weaknesses of traditional editorial gate-keeping practices and emerging open research approaches in protecting the integrity of the scholarly record. 

The session will commence with opening remarks from the chair to set the scene following by presentations from three panellists: a proponent of pre-publication editorial selection; a proponent of replacing upstream selection by open research approaches such as post-publication review; a neutral user perspective from a librarian. The presentations will be followed by a panel discussion to conclude the session. 

TIME SCHEDULE
1:30pm - 1:40pm: Opening Remarks - Dr Nandita Quaderi 
1:40pm - 2:00pm: Perspective of a proponent of editorial selection - Dr Deborah Sweet 
2:00pm - 2:20pm: Perspective of a proponent of open research practices - Perspective of a proponent of open research practices - Dr Katie Corker 
2:20pm - 2:40pm: A broad, end-user perspective of traditional and new forms of research output - Leo Lo 
2:40pm - 3:00pm: Q&A

speakers.name: Nandita Quaderi
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Nandita Quaderi is a Senior Vice President and the Editor-in-Chief for Web of Science at Clarivate. Nandita has oversight of the Web of Science editorial division, the Institute for Scientific Information and the Research Professional News team. She has overall responsibility for editorial strategy, selection of Web of Science content and inclusion in Journal Citation Reports. Nandita’s focus is on protecting the integrity of the scholarly record, promoting the responsible use of bibliometrics and developing a responsible framework for evaluating the societal impact of research.

Nandita joined Clarivate as an accomplished senior publishing executive with a strong research background. Previously, she worked in leadership roles at BMC and Springer Nature and was a Principal Investigator at the MRC Centre for Developmental Neurobiology, King’s College London. Nandita has a BA in Chemistry from the University of Oxford and a PhD in Molecular Genetics from Imperial College London.

Nandita is the incoming co-chair of United2Act, an international collaboration committed to addressing the challenge of paper mills in scholarly publishing, and currently sits on the Board of STM.

speakers.name: Deborah Sweet
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Executive Vice President, Journals, Nature Portfolio
speakers.bio: Deborah Sweet, PhD, Executive Vice President, Journals, Nature Portfolio has broad experience in high-quality journal publishing, including previous roles as the launch Editor-in-Chief of Cell Stem Cell and the VP of Editorial at Cell Press. She joined Springer Nature in 2022 as Vice President, Journals for the Nature Journals and then in 2024 became Executive Vice President for Nature Portfolio. In this role she is responsible for leading the editorial teams working on Nature, the Nature Research journals, the Nature Reviews journals, Nature Communications, and the Communications and npj series journals, and guiding the strategic direction of the Nature Portfolio overall.

speakers.name: Katie Corker
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Katie is executive director of ASAPbio. She earned a PhD in psychology from Michigan State University in 2012. Her interest in preprints was kindled in her role as executive officer of SIPS (the Society for the Improvement of Psychological Science), which launched and continues to steward PsyArXiv, the community-run preprint server for psychology research. Prior to joining ASAPbio, she was a faculty member at Kenyon College and Grand Valley State University, as well as a community engagement advisor for the Center for Open Science. She lives and works in Grand Rapids, MI, USA.

speakers.name: Leo Lo
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Leo S. Lo is University Librarian and Dean of Libraries, Advisor to the Provost for AI Literacy, and professor of education at the University of Virginia. He is internationally recognized for advancing AI literacy across higher education and workforce development, consulting and publishing widely on AI literacy, workforce development, and higher education innovation. As President (2024–2025) of the Association of College and Research Libraries, he established the ACRL AI Competencies Task Force and leads national efforts to develop AI competencies for library professionals. His initiatives include creating national AI literacy frameworks and leading large scale studies on how academic libraries adapt to AI. Lo holds a Doctor of Education from the University of Pennsylvania, an MS in Information Studies from Florida State University, and a Postgraduate Diploma in AI for Business from the University of Oxford. Guided by a human centered vision of technology, he challenges institutions to reimagine learning as an act of curiosity, creativity, and courage in the AI era.

speakers.name: Jason Pither
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: University Of British Columbia, Okanagan Campus
speakers.bio: I am an ecologist with broad interests spanning community ecology, biogeography, and landscape ecology.  For almost a decade I have worked with faculty and staff at UBC to raise awareness about Open Science (OS) principles and practices, and have delivered OS seminars and instructional sessions to students, faculty, and staff.  I continue to advocate for integrating Open Science principles and practices into university policies.  More recently, I have collaborated with Indigenous communities in the region to help build capacity around Indigenous Data Sovereignty.

speakers.name: Jos Kole
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Associate Professor Professional and Research Integrity Education
speakers.bio: Associate professor in professional and research integrity education, specialised in ethics and moral philosophy, stationed at the Ethics of Healthcare Department of the Radboud university medical Center, Nijmegen, The Netherlands.

speakers.name: Rosemary Kamuyu
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Rosemary Kamuyu is a health economist and a monitoring and evaluation officer at the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI) where she has been working for the last 7 years. Rose has been involved in a long-term bioethics training project funded by the National Institute of Health (NIH) and research regulatory and oversight projects funded by EDCTP II including SERCEA. She is involved in supporting ethics committees and regulators build capacity for their members especially with several new committees constituted. There are several new research projects that go through the KEMRI ethics committee so constantly supporting capacity strengthening is key. Rose is currently working on a new proposal to support establishment of a research integrity office in KEMRI. She is skilled in conducting qualitative and quantitative data analysis, generating data reports in compliance with procedures, agreements, and evaluation systems.

speakers.name: Francis Kombe
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Francis Kombe is the current Chief Executive Officer, EthiXPERT, South Africa (www.ethixpert.org.za/board-of-directors) and a Public Health practitioner and bioethicist, with a passion in Research Integrity. He has a wealth of experience working in international health research institutions, where held various leadership positions. Kombe holds an MSocSc (research ethics) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal (UKZN), South Africa; Master of Public Health (MPH) and Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health (PGDPH) from London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine-UK (LSHTM) and is currently awaiting to graduate from a Wellcome Trust funded PhD at UKZN focusing on the implications of fieldworkers’ institutional support systems for research integrity practices in Africa. He is a co-founder and chairperson of the African Research Integrity Network (ARIN), a member of the USA National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine (NASEM) and the Strategic Advisory Board for the EU’s AI ethics in human cognition and behaviour project. He served as a member of the programme planning committee during the 6th and 7th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong and Cape Town respectively. He has published widely in the field of research ethics, community engagement, frontline staff and research integrity among others.

speakers.name: Charis Wong
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Charis is a neurologist and clinical fellow based at the University of Edinburgh. She is interested in developing and applying AI-enabled evidence synthesis methodology to inform drug prioritisation for clinical trials in neurodegenerative diseases. She was part of the trial development group for MND-SMART (mnd-smart.org), an adaptive platform trial in motor neuron disease (MND). For her PhD, supervised by Professor Malcolm Macleod, Professor Siddharthan Chandran, and Professor Neil Carragher, she developed methods to identify, evaluate, and prioritise candidate drugs for clinical trials in MND. She is continuing to develop this work with the aim of improving the framework and expanding its applications to other disease areas. She is interested in the responsible use of AI in evidence synthesis and evaluation of AI-enabled digital evidence synthesis tools.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Anna Catharina (PhD, MSc, DMD) is a postdoctoral fellow in the Metaresearch and Open Science Program at the University of Ottawa Heart Institute. She holds a Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD) degree and a PhD in Health Sciences. She has contributed to multiple European and North American projects on metaresearch, research ethics, and research integrity. Her research explores various aspects of metaresearch, with a current focus on data management and sharing, open science practices, and best practices in research reporting.

speakers.name: Dimity Stephen
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: German Centre for Higher Education Research and Science Studies
speakers.bio: Dimity Stephen is a post-doctoral researcher at the German Centre for Higher Education Research and Science Studies (DZHW) in Berlin, Germany. She holds a bachelor’s in psychology and a PhD in public health from Queensland University of Technology in Brisbane, Australia. Dimity's main research focus lies in applying scientometric methods to investigate questions pertaining to questionable publishing practices, peer review, and the quality of research outputs. She is currently leading a four-year, internationally collaborative project funded by the Volkswagen Foundation that examines the effect of research evaluation systems on researchers' publishing practices in six countries in Europe, Asia, and Africa

speakers.name: Navin Kumar Verma
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.position: Director, Research Integrity and Ethics Office (RIEO)
speakers.bio: Dr Navin Kumar Verma is an Associate Professor of Immunology and Cell Biology at Lee Kong Chan School of Medicine (LKCMedicine), Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU). In addition, he serves as a head of Good Research Practice Office at LKCMedicine and Deputy Research Integrity Officer at NTU. He is an expert T-cell immunologist, internationally recognized for his work on the T-cell integrin lymphocyte function-associated antigen-1, has published over 80 papers, filed 5 patents, and won several national and international awards. He is also an elected Fellow of the Royal Society of Biology, UK.

She holds a Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD) degree and a PhD in Health Sciences. She has contributed to multiple European and North American projects on metaresearch, research ethics, and research integrity. Her research explores various aspects of metaresearch, with a current focus on data management and sharing, open science practices, and best practices in research reporting.

speakers.organization: German Centre for Higher Education Research and Science Studies
speakers.bio: Dimity Stephen is a post-doctoral researcher at the German Centre for Higher Education Research and Science Studies (DZHW) in Berlin, Germany. She holds a bachelor’s in psychology and a PhD in public health from Queensland University of Technology in Brisbane, Australia. Dimity's main research focus lies in applying scientometric methods to investigate questions pertaining to questionable publishing practices, peer review, and the quality of research outputs. She is currently leading a four-year, internationally collaborative project funded by the Volkswagen Foundation that examines the effect of research evaluation systems on researchers' publishing practices in six countries in Europe, Asia, and Africa

speakers.name: John Willinsky
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: John Willinsky is Professor at Simon Fraser University, as well as Khosla Family Professor Emeritus at Stanford University. He is founder and Co-Scientific Director of the Public Knowledge Project, which conducts research and develops the world’s most widely used open source scholarly publishing platform in support of greater access to knowledge. His dozen books include Empire of Words: The Reign of the OED (Princeton, 1994); Learning to Divide the World: Education at Empire’s End (Minnesota, 1998); The Access Principle: The Case for Open Access to Research and Scholarship (MIT, 2006), The Intellectual Properties of Learning: A Prehistory from Saint Jerome to John Locke (Chicago, 2017) and Copyright’s Broken Promise: How to Restore the Law’s Ability to Promote the Progress of Science (MIT, 2023).

speakers.name: Miro Vukovic
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: University Of Split School Of Medicine
speakers.bio: Miro Vuković, MD, is a Research and Teaching Assistant at the University of Split School of Medicine, Department for Research in Biomedicine and Health. He earned his Doctor of Medicine degree from the same institution in 2022 and gained clinical experience as a General Medicine practitioner in Split–Dalmatia County. Miro completed two research internships at Harvard Medical School’s Department of Neurobiology, focusing on neuroscience and sleep research. His scientific contributions include publications in Scientific Reports, BMJ Open, and Therapeutic Apheresis and Dialysis. As an active member of the academic community, he has held leadership roles in non-profit organization called Med&X where he still serves as a vice-president. His research interests span health technology assessment and public health.

speakers.position: Head Of Publication Ethics And Research Integrity

speakers.name: Patrick Chun Man Wong
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.organization: The Chinese University Of Hong Kong
speakers.bio: Patrick C. M. Wong is Professor of Linguistics and Cognitive Neuroscience and Founding Director of the Brain and Mind Institute at The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK). Wong’s research covers a wide range of basic and translational issues concerning the neural basis and disorders of language and music. In 2021, he was named a Guggenheim Fellow for Humanities. A versatile and effective teacher, research mentor, and clinical educator, Wong is a four-time recipient of the Faculty Outstanding Teaching Award at CUHK. He actively seeks to translate his research into clinical and educational solutions. One of his patented inventions, Precision Listening®, was awarded the Gold Medal with Congratulations of the Jury at the 2023 International Exhibition of Inventions Geneva. Wong has been Associate Vice-President (Research) at CUHK since 2023.

speakers.name: Josephine Woodhams
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr Josephine Woodhams has a Ph.D. in Medicine (Clinical Studies). She previously worked as a Senior Research Associate with over 10 years’ experience of designing and performing experiments in photobiology and nanomedicine at London’s Global University – UCL. She has contributed to collaborative cancer research projects with partners within the EU and USA.
Josephine’s role as Senior Programmes Manager is to support the delivery of UKRIO’s programme of work and assist with the development of new activities, in accordance with the objectives of the charity. A key element of her role is liaising with UKRIO’s many subscriber institutions to ensure proactive support of the needs of their researchers.
Josephine collaborates closely with the Chief Executive, Trustees and Advisory Board, Expert Community, and the representatives and members of UKRIO subscriber institutions.

speakers.name: Susanne van den Hooff
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Susanne van den Hooff is an assistant professor at the University of Humanistic Studies, Utrecht, The Netherlands. She is a qualitative social scientist with a PhD in Care Ethics and a Master's degree in Psychology and Health Law. Her research focus is on qualitative, interdisciplinary research. Among other things, she conducted research on good care for people with Korsakoff syndrome; she contributed to a juridical study concerning euthanasia carried out on dementia patients with an advance euthanasia directive. She is involved as a researcher in the Horizon Europe project, "Beyond Bad Apples: Towards a Behavioral and Evidence-Based Approach to Promote Research Ethics and Research Integrity in Europe” (BEYOND), a Horizon Europe project that investigates the root causes of research misconduct (see: beyondbadapples.eu/ ).
She is an active member of the CoARA working group ERIP: Ethics @ Research Integrity Policy in Responsible Research Assessment for Data and Artificial Intelligence (see: coara.org/working-groups/wg-erip/).

speakers.name: Namol Vorapreeda
speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.organization: King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi (KMUTT)
speakers.bio:
My name is Namol Vorapreeda , now I serve as an Educational Development Officer at the Office of Research, Innovation, and Partnerships, King Mongkut’s University of Technology Thonburi (KMUTT). I hold a Master of Science in Biotechnology from KMUTT and a Bachelor of Science in Agro-Industrial Technology from King Mongkut’s University of Technology North Bangkok.
My professional journey has focused on advancing research integrity, biosafety, human and animal ethical oversight. I am certified in Biorisk Management by the International Federation of Biosafety Associations (IFBA), and I have served as Secretary of KMUTT’s Institutional Biosafety Committee since 2011. I also support the Human Research Ethics Committee and the Animal Care and Use Committee as Assistant Secretary, and since 2019, I have contributed to KMUTT’s Working Group on Research Integrity Promotion.

speakers.name: Kathryn Weber-Boer
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Originally an anthropological archaeologist, Kathryn Weber-Boer transitioned from research (as a doctoral candidate at Cornell University) to research support, when she began a research intelligence project at the University of Groningen. She joined Digital Science when she saw the opportunity to make a broader impact in the world of responsible research evaluation. As a computational social scientist, she uses Altmetric and Dimensions data to explore non-traditional metrics, understand the use and perception of research, and improve research integrity–in a systemic way. Her recent work considers the importance of collaboration between social sciences and STEM, the role of mentorship on integrity practices, and the use and reuse of research data. As Director Scientometrics, Kathryn is responsible for engagement with scientometric researchers, managing access to Digital Science data for research and journalistic purposes, and collaborating with partners to improve and innovate Digital Science data and insights.

speakers.name: Maria Zalm
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Senior Editor, Team Manager Publication Ethics

speakers.name: Kimberley Wever
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.organization: Radboud university medical center, Nijmegen, The Netherlands
speakers.bio: I am a meta-researcher specializing in preclinical research integrity and reproducibility. Based in Nijmegen, The Netherlands, I am part of the internationally recognized meta-research team at the Radboudumc, and the CAMARADES collaboration. My work focuses on improving research reproducibility, study validity, and best practices in preclinical research, often using systematic review and meta-analysis as key methodology. I lead and contribute to major EU consortia, including iRISE, EQIPD and ChampAIons, and have co-developed pioneering resources such as Preclinicaltrials.eu and PROSPERO4animals. As an educator, I design and coordinate courses on research methodology and evidence synthesis for students and professionals at Radboud University and beyond. Through collaborations with organizations like EFSA and ZonMW, I strive to advance rigorous and transparent scientific practices on an international scale.

speakers.name: Kelebogile Kenalemang
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Kelebogile Kenalemang is a Senior Lecturer and Programme Leader for International Finance and Banking at the Botswana Accountancy College (BAC). She is an early-career researcher whose work focuses on advancing ethical, inclusive, and technology-driven research and learning systems in higher education. Her research examines how emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence can be responsibly adopted within teaching, research, and entrepreneurship development across the Global South.

Dr. Kenalemang has published on knowledge-based economies, innovation in SMEs, and sustainable education practices. She contributes to the British Council–funded multi-country project on gender and sustainability careers and authored internationally published case studies with the UK Quality Assurance Agency (QAA) on inclusive student engagement. At BAC, she mentors student societies, leads programme development, and drives academic–industry collaboration that strengthens integrity, employability, and research impact. A regular contributor to national media and policy dialogues, she champions ethical innovation and the democratization of knowledge in African higher education.

speakers.name: Heidi Lamb
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio:
Heidi Lamb (PhD, Peace and Conflict Studies; MA, Peace and Conflict Studies, 2020, University of Waterloo; BA[H], Drama and Communications, Media and Film, 2014, University of Windsor). Heidi is a PhD candidate in Peace and Conflict Studies at the University of Manitoba. Her SSHRC-funded research examines how post-secondary students experience digital misogyny and gender-based sexual violence on Canadian campuses, exploring how online narratives intersect with campus culture to shape attitudes and responses.
In addition, Heidi is a graduate student researcher on the We Interrupt This Programming project (Dr. Shannon Moore & Dr. Jennifer Watt, University of Manitoba), which investigates how K–12 educators perceive and respond to GBSV and consent, and how media can be used pedagogically to challenge rape culture. Beyond academia, she is actively involved in her local community through the Tavistock Agricultural Society and she enjoys spending time with her two shiba inus, Chance and Lucy.

speakers.name: Timothy Kiplagat
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio:
Mr. Timothy Kipkosgei is a Research Scientist at the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI). He is currently the SERU Compliance officer for CGHR and CIPDCR and a member of committee A at SERU. Timothy obtained his Master of Science degree in International Health from JKUAT and a Bachelor of Science degree in Biochemistry from Egerton University.
He also holds a Post Graduate Diploma in Bioethics from the Centre of Biomedical Ethics and Culture (CBEC), SIUT, Pakistan. Timothy’s research interest focuses on Bioethics, Public Health and Health Systems research. He is part of the KEMRI SERU training team and a member of Bioethics society of Kenya

speakers.name: Lene Os Johannessen
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Advisor and Director of the National Committee for Research Ethics on Human Remains
speakers.bio: Lene Os Johannessen is a Senior Advisor at the National Research Ethics Committees (NREC) in Norway and serves as Director of the National Committee for Research Ethics on Human Remains. She also leads NREC’s project on the development of training resources in research integrity and research ethics. Johannessen holds a PhD in Classical Archaeology from the University of Oslo, awarded in 2015. She has previously held positions as a lecturer in archaeology and as a research advisor at the University of Oslo.

speakers.name: Cynthia Komolafe
speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.bio: Komolafe Cynthia is a researcher and holds a master’s degree in Bioethics with a focus on the Responsible Conduct of Research and a Bachelor’s degree in Microbiology. My research interests lie at the intersection of research integrity, scientific ethics, and biomedical science. I have conducted empirical studies on mechanisms for reporting and responding to research misconduct in Nigerian universities. I also explore issues in microbiology, antibiotic resistance, and the sustainable use of biological resources. My ongoing work seeks to strengthen institutional frameworks that foster transparency, accountability, and trust in research.

speakers.name: Ana Marušić
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.organization: University Of Split School Of Medicine
speakers.bio: Ana Marušić is Professor of Anatomy, Chair of the Department of Research in Biomedicine and Health, and Head of the Center for Evidence-based Medicine at the University of Split School of Medicine. Her research interests include evidence-based medicine and research integrity. She has published over 400 articles in peer-reviewed journals and was heavily involved with creating the policy of mandatory registration of clinical trials in public registries which helped change the legal regulation of clinical trials worldwide. Prof. Marušić is the founder of Cochrane Croatia and also serves on the Advisory Board the EQUATOR Network. She was the editor in chief of the Croatian Medical Journal and the Journal of Global Health. She is currently the Senior Editor-in-Chief of ST-OPEN, an overlay journal of the University of Split. She serves on the Council of the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE), and as the president of The Embassy Foundation. She is the founding member of the Croatian Reproducibility and Integrity Network, CroRIN.

speakers.name: Nihar Shah
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Nihar B. Shah is an Associate Professor in the Machine Learning and Computer Science departments at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU). His research is on the evaluation of science and the science of evaluation. His group develops computational tools with strong mathematical guarantees, and also designs and conducts controlled experiments for evidence-based policy design. His work has been used in the review of well over hundred thousand papers and thousands of proposals, in over two hundred venues. He is a recipient of the Young Alumnus Medal from the Indian Institute of Science, a JP Morgan faculty research award, Google Research Scholar Award, an NSF CAREER Award 2020-25, the 2017 David J. Sakrison memorial prize from EECS Berkeley for a "truly outstanding and innovative PhD thesis", the Microsoft Research PhD Fellowship 2014-16, the Berkeley Fellowship 2011-13, and several Best Paper Awards.

speakers.name: Louisa Savereide
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio:
Dr. Savereide is a consultant with Maidstone Consulting Group LLC.  With over ten years of experience in heterogeneous catalysis and chemical engineering research, her technical expertise includes reactor design and construction, catalyst synthesis (including air-free techniques), imaging (STEM-EDX, TEM), spectroscopy (XAS, DRUV-vis, XPS, etc.), and additional catalyst characterization techniques (TPR, TGA, BET, XRD, etc.).  She is also interested in promoting international scientific collaboration and has worked as an intern at the National Academy of Sciences of Costa Rica and volunteered at an NGO in Bolivia. 
Louisa is currently based at the École polytechnique fédérale de Lausanne, where she serves as the research coordinator at the EPFL Energy Center, guiding the formation of projects in the field of energy. She has in-depth knowledge of the grant writing and submission process for small- and large-scale collaborative projects. She received her PhD in Chemical Engineering from Northwestern University and her BS in Chemical Engineering from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.name: Thembeka Sdinane
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Thembeka Sdinane is a Lecturer in the Faculty of Health Sciences Research Directorate, Nelson Mandela University, South Africa.  She holds a PhD in Psychology, which focused on Religious Rituals and Psychological Well-being in Rural Communities. In her current role, she oversees research support, ethics and engagement in the faculty of health sciences.   Her research interests are: Indigenous Knowledge systems, Culture and Mental Health and  Research ethics

speakers.name: Inge Stegeman
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Inge Stegeman is epidemiologist in the UMC Utrecht in the Netherlands. She combines clinical research with meta-science.

speakers.name: Gowri Krishna
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Dr. Gowri Gopalakrishna is an epidemiologist and public health scientist with expertise in research integrity, open science, and responsible conduct of research. She led the Dutch National Survey on Research Integrity, one of the most comprehensive studies globally on research misconduct and questionable research practices. Currently, she coordinates and oversees efforts around research integrity and certification of journals in Elsevier with a research affiliation at Maastricht University, where she conducts empirical research on issues of research integrity. Prior to her current positions, she made notable contributions to the control and prevention of the SARS epidemic in 2003 and later in the Covid-19 pandemic. She also actively contributes to national and international advisory bodies, funding agencies and is a well-known public speaker on the topic of research integrity.

presentations.title: Open Science to Increase Reproducibility in Science: The OSIRIS Project

speakers.name: Inge Stegeman
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Inge Stegeman is epidemiologist in the UMC Utrecht in the Netherlands. She combines clinical research with meta-science.

affiliations: Czech University of Life Sciences Prague

presentations.title: Disciplinary Differences Matter

speakers.name: Mads Sørensen
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Mads P. Sørensen is a Professor at the Danish Centre for Studies in Research and Research Policy at Aarhus University, Denmark. His current research focuses on Social Theory and Research on Research, including Research Integrity, Research Ethics, and Research Policy: pure.au.dk/portal/en/persons/mps@ps.au.dk

presentations.title: The More You Automate, the Less You See: Hidden Pitfalls of AI Scientist Systems

speakers.name: Nihar Shah
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Nihar B. Shah is an Associate Professor in the Machine Learning and Computer Science departments at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU). His research is on the evaluation of science and the science of evaluation. His group develops computational tools with strong mathematical guarantees, and also designs and conducts controlled experiments for evidence-based policy design. His work has been used in the review of well over hundred thousand papers and thousands of proposals, in over two hundred venues. He is a recipient of the Young Alumnus Medal from the Indian Institute of Science, a JP Morgan faculty research award, Google Research Scholar Award, an NSF CAREER Award 2020-25, the 2017 David J. Sakrison memorial prize from EECS Berkeley for a "truly outstanding and innovative PhD thesis", the Microsoft Research PhD Fellowship 2014-16, the Berkeley Fellowship 2011-13, and several Best Paper Awards.

presentations.title: Ethical Challenges in an Observational Study with Adolescents in Bangladesh: Researchers' Reflexive Insights on Participant Wellbeing, Integrity, and Responsibility
affiliations: International Centre For Diarrhoeal Disease Research Bangladesh (icddr,b)

presentations.title: Generative AI in Research and Data Integrity

Dr. Savereide is a consultant with Maidstone Consulting Group LLC.  With over ten years of experience in heterogeneous catalysis and chemical engineering research, her technical expertise includes reactor design and construction, catalyst synthesis (including air-free techniques), imaging (STEM-EDX, TEM), spectroscopy (XAS, DRUV-vis, XPS, etc.), and additional catalyst characterization techniques (TPR, TGA, BET, XRD, etc.). sessions.name: Closing Ceremony and Awards
sessions.details: Join us as we wrap up the 9th World Conference on Research Integrity with a celebratory closing ceremony. This session will recognize outstanding contributions to research integrity through the presentation of several prestigious awards. We will also express our gratitude to all who made the event possible. Be sure not to miss the exciting announcement of the host location for the 10th WCRI—a moment that sets the stage for the next chapter in advancing global research integrity.

speakers.name: Christopher Graf
speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.bio: Chris Graf is the Research Integrity Director at Springer Nature, in charge of leading the continued development and implementation of Springer Nature’s research integrity strategy, leading a team accountable for assuring the highest standards for research integrity in all of Springer Nature’s publications, working with many good colleagues inside Springer Nature and with many good academic partners outside Springer Nature. Chris also serves the publishing sector with a voluntary role at the STM Association, the global trade association for academic and professional publishers, where he chairs a committee of senior publishing industry executives overseeing governance of the STM Integrity Hub, and the STM Research Integrity Committee. In 2023 Chris was appointed to a position as committee member of the UK Committee on Research Integrity, a new organisation hosted by UKRI, that promotes research integrity across the UK and internationally.

presentations.title: Chair: Closing Ceremony

In 2023 Chris was appointed to a position as committee member of the UK Committee on Research Integrity, a new organisation hosted by UKRI, that promotes research integrity across the UK and internationally.

sessions.name: Plenary Session #4 - Research Security and Integrity
sessions.details:
The shifting geopolitical climate is increasingly shaping discussions around research integrity, open science, and security. This panel explores the evolving relationship between politics and research, focusing on how global uncertainty impacts research integrity and Open Science.
As research security becomes more prominent globally, it is often linked to protecting research from harmful influences, including the misuse of technology that could threaten security or violate ethical standards. While some security measures reinforce ethics and integrity, others may restrict the freedom of scientific research, openness, and collaboration. Security and openness, therefore, are not always aligned—they often present competing priorities that must be carefully balanced. 
This plenary will spark a nuanced dialogue on how to foster trusted global research collaboration while upholding ethical standards in an era of heightened security concerns. Speakers will examine the tensions and synergies between open science, security, and integrity, highlighting the responsibilities of researchers, institutions, and governments in navigating these challenges. 
By addressing both the conceptual foundations and practical implications of research security, this session aims to build shared understanding and support the development of sustainable research governance models—ones that protect integrity and ethics while encouraging global cooperation amid geopolitical uncertainty. 

SPEAKERS:
Carthage Smith, Head of the Global Science Forum, OECD 
Smith will provide an overview of what research security entails, outlining its key components and relevance for research integrity and in today’s global context. He will also elaborate on how the OECD offers a broader framework for assessing both research integrity and security and propose a governance model designed to address emerging challenges in the research landscape. 

Sakine Weikert, Head of Section, Center for International Academic Cooperation (KIWi), DAAD – German Academic Exchange Service 
Weikert will contribute practical insights by highlighting recent developments in research security in Germany. She will present examples from KIWi, which serves as a central contact point for German universities and discuss its role in developing guidelines and tools for systematic chance-risk analysis and information exchange related to research integrity and security. 

Richard Gold (McGill University, Canada), Leveraging Open Science to Enhance Global Security 
This talk will delve into the implications of open science on research security. Richard Gold will elucidate how open data and know-how protect national security by ensuring that no country is excluded from utilising datasets and knowledge in the future and how open international collaborations can provide insights into global research efforts. Highlighting the health sector, the talk will explore the WHO's experience in South Africa with mRNA vaccine development to underscore the relevance of open science in mitigating delays and enhancing security. The session will examine the interplay between intellectual property rights and open environments for collaborative research. 

Relevance: This talk will illustrate how open science can serve as a catalyst for global security, offering solutions for timely access to critical technologies and data. It will empower policy-makers and researchers to balance exclusivity rights with the principles of transparency and collaboration.

speakers.name: Joeri Tijdink
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.bio: Joeri Tijdink is an associate professor and principal investigator at Amsterdam UMC, location VUmc, and is affiliated with VU University in Amsterdam, the Netherlands. After studying medicine at Utrecht University (1999–2006) and specializing as a clinical psychiatrist in Amsterdam (2007–2012), he completed his PhD (2012–2015), entitled Publish & Perish: Research on Research and Researchers. His thesis focused on the impact of publication pressure on research quality and the mental health of researchers. His current research focuses on research integrity, reproducibility, research quality, mental well-being in academia, and research culture. He is involved in several national and international research projects, including the TIER2 project (www.tier2-project.eu), which explores the future(s) of reproducibility and develops tools to support reproducibility practices. He is also a principal investigator in the EU-funded TRUSTparency project, which implements promotion plans for different stakeholders to foster reproducibility practices. In addition, he leads several projects aimed at supporting a responsible research culture in diverse academic settings and studies how early career researchers can be empowered to speak up. He initiated the national Akademiethermometer survey, which investigates the mental health of academics in the Netherlands. Joeri is also the author of the book The Happy Academic – How to Thrive and Survive in Academia (2023), which offers guidance to early career researchers navigating the challenges of academic life. In his work, he consistently focuses on individual, cultural, and systemic factors that can help improve academia, with a strong emphasis on promoting mental health among researchers. Alongside his research, he continues to work as a clinical psychiatrist. In 2022, he was appointed as a member of The Young Academy of the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences, highlighting his commitment to improving research quality, strengthening the societal relevance of research, and fostering a more supportive academic environment for early career researchers.

speakers.name: Carthage Smith
speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.position: Head of the Global Science Forum
speakers.bio: Carthage Smith joined the OECD as head of the Global Science Forum (GSF) Secretariat in 2014. He is responsible for working with countries to define the overall strategy and priorities for the Forum. This includes policy work on research infrastructures, Open Science, research funding mechanisms, science advisory processes and research careers. He has been leading the GSF team working on Research Security since 2021. Carthage was originally trained as a biochemist, with a PhD in neuroscience. Prior to joining the GSF secretariat, he was Deputy Executive Director of the International Council for Science (ICSU, Paris) for twelve years. Before moving to France, he spent six years at the UK Medical Research Council, where he was Head of International Cooperation.

speakers.name: Richard Gold
speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.bio: Richard Gold brings 35 years of academic and private-sector experience to his policy work on innovation, economic growth, and open science. A former technology lawyer at a leading international firm, he currently holds the title of Distinguished James McGill Professor in Innovation and Intellectual Property, Director of McGill’s Centre for Intellectual Property Policy (CIPP), and Chief Policy and Partnerships Officer at Conscience. With training in law (LL.B [Toronto], LL.M and S.J.D [Michigan]) and computer science (B.Sc. [McGill]), he integrates technical, business, and legal expertise to investigate how law interacts with other social systems shaping innovation outcomes.

speakers.name: Sakine Weikert
speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.position: Head of Section, Center for International Academic Cooperation (KIWi)
speakers.bio: Sakine Weikert is head of the Centre for International Academic Cooperation (KIWi) at the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD). KIWi offers German universities individual advice on the strategic development and expansion of international cooperation under ever more complex framework conditions. Focus topics are research security, science diplomacy or legal frameworks, which are combined with DAAD’s worldwide regional expertise.

presentations.title: Chair: Research Security and Integrity

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Anna Abalkina
speakers.bio: Dr. Anna Abalkina is a research fellow at Freie Universität Berlin (Germany). She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Perugia (Italy) with a background in international economics. However, she later shifted her research focus to corruption in higher education, academic misconduct, plagiarism, paper mills, and predatory and hijacked journals. Her research not only involves detecting and analyzing scientific misconduct but also explaining its costs and consequences. Since 2013, Anna Abalkina has been actively serving as an expert for Dissernet, a grassroots initiative aimed at detecting plagiarism in Ph.D. theses and scientific papers in the Russian language. Since 2021, she has successfully detected and investigated several paper mills, identifying more than 2300 papers associated with paper mills. In 2022, Anna Abalkina in collaboration with Retraction Watch created “The Retraction Watch Hijacked Journal Checker,” a regularly updated list of hijacked journals. She received Nature’s 10 award in 2024 for her work on exposing fraud.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Amani Abu-Shaheen
speakers.bio: Amani is a Senior Researcher with extensive experience in clinical research and medical writing. She is interested in research and academic integrity, and predatory scientific journals. She has many publications in international journals and more than ten years of lecturing experience. She has published several articles in international peer-reviewed journals and has participated in several national and international conferences and workshops as a speaker. Ms. Amani also serves as a reviewer for a number of well-known journals.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Daniel Acuna
speakers.bio: Daniel Acuna, Ph.D. is the founder of ReviewerZero AI, an AI-driven platform that identifies issues like image tampering, citation manipulation, and statistical anomalies to protect research integrity. He is on entreprenurial leave from his role as Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of Colorado Boulder. In academia, Acuna has conducted extensive research in research integrity, publishing over 70 peer-reviewed papers. His work has been supported by the U.S. Office of Research Integrity, the National Science Foundation, the Sloan Foundation, and DARPA’s SCORE initiative. In 2021, he organized Computational Research Integrity Conference (cri-conf.org), which united researchers, ethicists, and tech experts to address real-world issues in integrity.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Clement Adebamowo
speakers.position: Professor And Director Of Division Of Cancer Epidemiology & Associate Director, Pop. Science
speakers.organization: University Of Maryland School Of Medicine
speakers.bio:
I direct the Cancer Epidemiology Division at University of Maryland School of Medicine, Baltimore and several NIH funded international bioethics training programs. I am also Associate Director of the Population Sciences Program in our Cancer Center.
My research focuses the epidemiological and genomic risks, biomarkers discovery, early detection and prevention of breast, lung, and cervical cancers. I also conduct research on research ethics including comprehension of consent, consent in AD/ADRD, and the intersection between research and data science.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Tayewo Adewumi
speakers.bio: Dr. Tayewo Adewumi is a Senior Lecturer and Head of Department of Private and Property Law at the Faculty of Law, Elizade University, Ilara-Mokin, Ondo State, Nigeria

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Achal Agrawal
speakers.bio: Dr. Achal Agrawal is the founder of India Research Watch and PostPub. India Research Watch has the objective to improve Research Integrity in India as well as the world.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Abiodun Akindele
speakers.bio: Abiodun Tunde AKINDELE is a seasoned generalist university administrator with 27 years of experience, including 15 years in Research Management and Administration (RMA) at the University of Ibadan, Nigeria. His expertise spans research ethics, compliance, and integrity. He has presented on RMA topics at notable conferences such as the Conference of Rectors, Vice Chancellors, and Presidents of African Universities, the World Conference on Research Integrity, the EARIMA Conference, and NCURA’s annual meeting. Akindele has also trained researchers and administrators at institutions in Nigeria and the United States, including Northwestern University and Obafemi Awolowo University. His publications focus on RMA practices in Nigeria and Africa, with comparative indices in a global context. He is an alumnus Fellow of the International Research and Exchanges Board, supported by the Carnegie Corporation of New York and a member of the  African Research Integrity Network (ARIN).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Sabina Alam
speakers.position: VP of Publishing Ethics And Integrity
speakers.bio: Sabina Alam is the Director of Publishing Ethics and Integrity at Taylor and Francis Group. After a research career in neuroscience, Sabina moved into scientific publishing in 2008 at the open access publisher BioMed Central where she served as Chief Editor of BMC Medicine from 2011-2016, and then to F1000 in 2017 where she was the Editorial Director of F1000 Research. Sabina joined Taylor and Francis Group in 2018 where she leads the Publishing Ethics and Research Integrity team on providing investigative support and expert guidance on editorial policies, publishing ethics and research integrity, and ensures our global outreach on research integrity issues with key researcher communities fosters trust and knowledge exchange. Sabina also serves as an Advisor for Committee of Publication Ethics (COPE), and is a member of the Expert committee on STM Integrity Hub.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Marie Alavi
speakers.bio:
Marie Alavi is a research associate at Kiel University. Her research focus is the intersection of RCR, Open Science, Intellectual Property and AI. She is the co-developer of the GATE and member of NERQ. 

Marie is currently involved in the IP4OS project and GATE initiative. Prior to that, she was involved with Path2Integrity and HAnS, conducting research on research integrity and the role of AI in higher education.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Isamme Al-Fayyad
speakers.bio:
Isamme AlFayyad is a Senior Research Specialist and Chairperson of the Clinical Research Performance Assurance Department at King Fahad Medical City, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. He is also an external PhD candidate at the Department of Epidemiology, Care and Public Health Research Institute (CAPHRI), Maastricht University, The Netherlands, where his research focuses on the use of Artificial Intelligence in biomedical research.
He has extensive expertise in regulatory compliance and clinical research governance. Mr. AlFayyad has published widely and actively contributes to initiatives that promote research integrity and the responsible use of AI in healthcare research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Eby Aluckal
speakers.bio:
Dr. Eby Aluckal is a Professor in the Department of Public Health Dentistry at Mar Baselios Dental College, Kothamangalam, affiliated with the Kerala University of Health Sciences, India. He completed his Master’s in Public Health Dentistry from KLE University, Belgaum, after graduating in dentistry, and went on to pursue his PhD at Annamalai University, Chidambaram, with his research focusing on smokeless tobacco use among youth.

Dr. Aluckal further enhanced his expertise in global tobacco control by completing a fellowship in the Tobacco Control Program at Johns Hopkins University, Baltimore, USA, under the prestigious WHO–Bloomberg Initiative Scholarship.

With a strong academic and research background, Dr. Aluckal has authored and co-authored several books and numerous peer-reviewed journal articles. His contributions extend beyond academia, as he actively holds key positions in various national organizations connected to dentistry and public health.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Olavo Amaral
speakers.organization: Federal University Of Rio De Janeiro
speakers.bio: Olavo Amaral is an associate professor at the Institute of Medical Biochemistry Leopoldo de Meis, at the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro since 2009. He graduated as a medical doctor (2002) and PhD in Biochemistry (2007) in the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul, is a Radiology and Diagnostic Imaging Specialist (2006). After 15 years of research in neuroscience, with a focus on the neurobiology of memory, he now dedicates himself to meta-research applied to research reproducibility, as well as to activism and training of researchers in open and reproducible science. He is the founder of multiple initiatives to promote this goal in Brazil, including the Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative, the Brazilian Reproducibility Network, and the Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative in Systematic review and meta-Analysis (BRISA).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Emily Anderson
speakers.bio: Emily E. Anderson, PhD, MPH, is professor of bioethics at Loyola University Chicago. She teaches research ethics and responsible conduct of research to graduate and medical students as well as courses in empirical and global bioethics. Her areas of interest and expertise include researcher and physician professionalism and misconduct; ethical issues in research with vulnerable populations; informed consent; and institutional review board (IRB) decision-making and quality.  A mixed-methods but primarily qualitative researcher, she serves as associate editor for Narrative Inquiry in Bioethics. She also has over 20 years of experience serving on six different IRBs and has been PI or Co-I on several federally-funded research and educational projects. She is currently PI of the Loyola University Chicago-Ukrainian Catholic University (LUC-UCU) International Bioethics Training Program, which provides advanced training in research ethics in English to scholars and professionals from Ukraine.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Christian Anyiador
speakers.bio: Christian Anyiador is a final-year medical student at Ambrose Alli University, Nigeria, and the founder of the Nigeria Association of Undergraduate Medical Researchers (NAUMR). He is committed to promoting research integrity, digital health literacy, and mentorship for medical undergraduates. His work focuses on innovative approaches to medical education and research capacity building in Nigeria. Beyond research, Christian is passionate about applying medical knowledge to community development and inspiring the next generation of healthcare professionals.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Rene Aquarius
speakers.bio:
Rene Aquarius is a biomedical scientist working at the Neurosurgery department of the Radboud university medical center in Nijmegen, The Netherlands. 

Besides his regular work - which focuses mainly on systematic reviews, as well as vivo, in vitro, and clinical research in the field of vascular neurosurgery -, he has also been active as a so-called ‘science sleuth’ for the last two years. 

As a science sleuth, he is focusing mostly on finding image-related issues in scientific articles.

speakers.title: Miss
speakers.name: Nicole Au
speakers.bio: Nicole is a PhD student and part-time research fellow in the Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at Monash University. Her research centres on evidence-based approaches to women's healthcare, with a focus on randomised controlled trials, individual participant data meta-analysis, and research integrity. She holds a Bachelor of Biomedical Science from Monash University and a Master of Biostatistics from the University of Sydney.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Samson Mabuchi Banda
speakers.bio:
Mabuchi Samson Banda is a seasoned public health professional and research ethics expert with over seven years of experience in health research, data analysis, and project management. Holding a Master of Public Health from the University of Zambia and an Advanced Diploma in Clinical Research from the Vienna School of Public Health, Mabuchi specializes in research protocol development, ethical oversight, and monitoring & evaluation.  

Currently serving as a Senior Research Promotions Officer at Zambia’s National Health Research Authority, he has previously contributed to institutions like the University of Maryland Baltimore and the Centre for Infectious Disease Research in Zambia (CIDRZ). His expertise spans randomized evaluations, IRB submissions, and fieldwork coordination, supported by proficiency in STATA, SPSS, and multilingual communication (English and Zambian languages).  

Mabuchi is certified in human subjects protection (CITI), epidemiology, and health research ethics. His work aligns with global health priorities, particularly bioethics and infectious disease research. Referees highlight his analytical rigor and leadership in regulatory compliance.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Suresh Baral
speakers.organization: School of Engineering, Pokhara University, Nepal

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Rebecca Barber
speakers.position: Ethics And Integrity Analyst Team Lead

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Daniel Barr
speakers.bio: Dr Daniel Barr is the Principal Research Integrity Advisor at RMIT University, Australia, specialising in responsible research governance, management, and teaching. He contributes globally to research integrity through roles with the World Conferences on Research Integrity Foundation, the Asia-Pacific Research Integrity Network, and the Australian Academy of Science. Dr Barr co-authored the APEC Guiding Principles for Research Integrity and co-chaired the 6th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong. In 2021, Dr Barr was awarded the Paul Taylor Medal for Excellence in Research Integrity by the Australasian Research Management Society.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: John Barugahare
speakers.bio: John Barughare, Ph.D., is a Senior Lecturer of Philosophy and Bioethics, and heads the Department of Philosophy at Makerere University. After a BA Honors (Philosophy) from Makerere University, he received his MPhil and Ph.D. in philosophy from the University of Bergen, Norway. He is a former Fogarty Post-doc fellow at the Johns Hopkins Berman Institute of Bioethics, and a CAPREx visiting Scholar at the University of Cambridge.
