He teaches African philosophy and ethics, philosophy of method, research ethics, among others. His research interests are on conceptual, analytic, normative, and methodological aspects in ethics in international collaborative health research, and public health. With a goal of fostering health justice within and between countries. He is a member of UNESCO’s International Bioethics Committee (IBC); Africa CDC’s Ethics Technical Working Group; National Bioethics Committee, and the Mulago National Referral Hospital, and the Makerere University School of Health Sciences Research Ethics Committees.”

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Janina Bau
speakers.organization: European Association of Research Managers and Administrators
speakers.bio: Janina is an EU Projects Officer and Research Analyst at EARMA. She contributes to several EU-funded projects focused on ethics and research integrity, sustainability, as well as the evolving role of research management within the European Research Area. She is also part of the Community of Early career Researchers working within Research Ethics as part of the irecs project. Janina holds academic qualifications in European Studies (Germany and Sweden), Political Science (Belgium), and Cultural Studies (United States).

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Rosemarie De La Cruz Bernabe
speakers.bio: Rosemarie Bernabe is professor of research ethics and research integrity at the Centre for Medical Ethics of the University of Oslo. She leads/has led several European and national projects addressing responsible open science, reviewing research misconduct from a complexity perspective, ethical issues in the development of immersive technologies, ethical issues in late-phase clinical trials, research ethics capacity-building, equitable access to care, and responsible innovation in crisis-affected regions, among others. Key projects include TRUSTparency, AccessAfrica 1 and 2, ROSiE, XR4Human, Pandemic Ethics, and BEYOND.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Ann Beynon
speakers.bio: Ann Beynon has held various roles at Clarivate for 19 years, specializing in bibliometrics, research evaluation, and research information management. She currently runs customer success operations for the Research and Analytics business.  She has also managed partnerships in the Institute for Scientific Information (ISI), led the North American solutions consulting team, and managed global commercial training. Her current focus areas are research integrity, research security, open science, and research evaluation reform.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Sabuj Bhattacharyya
speakers.position: Scientist And Research Ethics And Integrity Officer
speakers.organization: Institute For Stem Cell Science And Regenerative Medicine
speakers.bio: Dr. Sabuj Bhattacharyya is currently working as a Scientist and Research Ethics and Integrity Officer at BRIC-inStem, Bangalore. He received training in ecology, population genomics, and research ethics and integrity during his Ph.D. and postdoctoral tenure. He has established India’s first dedicated Research Ethics and Integrity Office and is actively engaged in teaching research ethics, conducting research on retractions, and promoting integrity in scientific practice. Dr. Bhattacharyya is an alumnus of the Fulbright Fellowship (USA) and the Commonwealth Fellowship (UK). He also serves as a member of the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission Alumni Advisory Panel and the European Network for Academic Integrity. He has published more than 25 articles in international peer-reviewed journals and has presented his research at several international conferences.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Elisabeth Bik
speakers.bio: Elisabeth Bik, PhD, is a Dutch-American microbiologist who has worked for 15 years at Stanford University and 2 years in industry. Since 2019, she is a science integrity volunteer and consultant who scans the biomedical literature for images or other data of concern. She has found over 8,000 problematic scientific papers. Her work has resulted in over 1,300 retractions and another 1,000 corrections. For her work on exposing threats to research integrity, she received the 2021 John Maddox Prize and the 2024 Einstein Foundation Award.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Margaret Blakeney
speakers.organization: Secretariat On Responsible Conduct Of Research
speakers.bio: Margaret Blakeney is a Senior Policy Analyst with the Canadian Secretariat on Responsible Conduct of Research

speakers.title: Prof
speakers.name: Lex Bouter
speakers.position: Professor Emeritus of Methodology and Integrity
speakers.bio: Lex Bouter is Professor Emeritus of Methodology and Integrity at the Department of Epidemiology and Data Science of the Amsterdam University Medical Centers and the Department of Philosophy of the Faculty of Humanities of the Vrije Universiteit. He is involved research and teaching on research integrity and open science topics. He was appointed as tenured Professor of Epidemiology in 1992 and served his university as its rector between 2006 and 2013. Professor Bouter has supervised 81 PhD students, of whom to date 17 were appointed as professor. He is the founding chair of the World Conferences on Research Integrity Foundation.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Jennifer Byrne
speakers.bio: Jennifer Byrne is Director of Biobanking- NSW Health, and Professor of Molecular Oncology, University of Sydney, where she leads the PRIMeR group (Publication and Research Integrity in Medical Research). Jennifer was one of Nature’s 10 people in 2017 for highlighting repetitive human gene research papers with critical reagent errors, and she was one of 3 experts who testified before US Congress at their 2022 congressional hearing into paper mills and research misconduct. Jennifer’s research team has continued to identify features of flawed human gene and cancer research, including descriptions of human cell line models that may not exist.

speakers.title: Miss
speakers.name: Barbara Ćaćić
speakers.organization: Faculty Of Humanities And Social Sciences, Split
speakers.bio: I am a Master’s student in Psychology at the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences at the University of Split, Croatia, with a strong interest in improving research quality and promoting data sharing practices. My work focuses on understanding the factors that influence open data behavior among researchers and on developing ways to enhance transparency and reproducibility in science. I am passionate about advancing research integrity and fostering a culture of openness that supports better scientific outcomes. I have presented my work at several conferences and recently published my first peer-reviewed article.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Laura Caquelin
speakers.bio: Postdoctoral researcher at Karolinska Institutet, Sweden. Current work focuses on testing an intervention aimed at improving research reproducibility. Holds a PhD in Clinical Research from the University of Rennes, France, with a specialization in metascience. Background in biomedical sciences and clinical research methodology.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Minal Caron
speakers.bio: Minal Caron is an attorney at Ropes & Gray LLP, focusing on research misconduct, research ethics, and other research-related compliance issues.  Minal speaks and publishes frequently on research ethics issues.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Eduarda Centeno
speakers.bio: Eduarda Centeno is a Brazilian scientist with a BSc in Biotechnology from the Federal University of Pelotas (Brazil), which included a one-year academic exchange at the University of Reading (UK). She holds a double MSc degree in Neuroscience from the Neurasmus program, awarded jointly by the University of Bordeaux (France) and the University of Amsterdam (Netherlands), and earned her PhD in Neuroscience from the University of Bordeaux. Eduarda currently works as Executive Director of the Brazilian Reproducibility Network (BrRN), where she leads efforts to promote transparency and rigour in scientific research. Over the past eight years, she has actively advocated for open and reproducible science across Europe and now in Brazil, working at the intersection of community engagement, policy advocacy, and research integrity.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Sabine Chai
speakers.bio: Sabine Chai is the managing director of the Austrian Agency for Research Integrity (OeAWI). As such, she collaborates with national and international  stakeholders to foster an environment conducive to improving and protecting research integrity, conducts trainings on research integrity and related issues, and offers advising in the context of alleged research misconduct. She is a social scientist by training (PhD from University of Maryland) and has extensive experience in teaching intercultural communication, research methods, scientific writing and dissemination skills.

speakers.title: Mr
speakers.name: Wei Mun Chan
speakers.bio: Research Integrity Manager at eLife. Overseeing research integrity and editorial ethics at the journal.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: De Ming Chau
speakers.bio: Dr. De Ming Chau is a molecular biologist with experience in cancer stem cell research. He is the co-founder of the Young Scientists Network-Academy of Sciences Malaysia (YSN-ASM) Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) Programme and he has been chairing the YSN-ASM Science Integrity Working Group since 2017. Dr. Chau is the co-editor of the Malaysian Educational Module on RCR and a contributor of the Malaysian Code of Responsible Conduct in Research. Currently, Dr. Chau is a committee member of the National Committee on Research Integrity (Malaysia) and the Co-Chair of the Association of Southeast Nations (ASEAN) RCR Project.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Tiffany Chenneville
speakers.position: Professor Of Psychology And Marie E. And E. Leslie Cole Endowed Chair In Ethics
speakers.bio: Dr. Tiffany Chenneville is the Marie E. and E. Leslie Cole Endowed Chair in Ethics and Professor of Psychology and Pediatrics at the University of South Florida (USF). She also holds an Honorary Professorship in the Perinatal HIV Research Unit at the University of the Witwatersrand (WITS) in Johannesburg, South Africa. Her scholarship focuses on ethics and sexual health, with recognized contributions to professional, research, and treatment ethics, particularly within psychological and social-behavioral health contexts. Her research addresses psychosocial issues affecting youth living with or at risk for HIV and broader issues of sexual health among marginalized communities. Committed to global health, Dr. Chenneville collaborates with colleagues in Kenya, South Africa, India, England, and Canada. She has produced nearly 100 publications and 250 presentations and is a former chair of the APA Ethics Committee. She recently completed APA Congressional and AAAS Executive Branch Fellowships.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Victoria Chepkorir Soi
speakers.bio:
Victoria is a Research Scientist at the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI), working within the Scientific and Ethics Review Unit (SERU). With a focus on ethical research practices and scientific integrity, she contributes to the review and oversight of research protocols, ensuring that studies meet both ethical standards and scientific rigor. 
 She holds a Master degree in Global Bioethics from Anahuac University, Mexico and a Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology from Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (2010). She also holds a Certificate in Clinical Ethics from Centre for Bioethics and Culture, Sindh Institute of Urology and Transplantation (CBEC-SIUT) Pakistan (2014). 
Victoria is dedicated to advancing health research in Kenya, fostering ethical research environments, and promoting public trust in scientific endeavors. Outside of her professional work, Victoria enjoys hiking, reading, and exploring new cultures through travel, which helps her stay inspired and connected to diverse perspectives.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Kevin Chong
speakers.bio: Dr Chong is a senior research administrator with expertise in research strategy, policy development, and governance. Trained in biomedical sciences, he brings over a decade of experience across academic and industry research, research management, integrity, and ethics. He has led initiatives in research output and data governance, digital transformation, and research integrity across multiple research domains, integrating foresight-led planning, impact measurement, and governance frameworks to enhance institutional accountability and trust in research.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Chien Chou
speakers.organization: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Jana Christopher
speakers.bio:
Jana is the Image Data Integrity Analyst at the Federation of European Biochemical Societies (FEBS Press). She has also been a freelance consultant for The Royal Society since 2019. 
Jana chairs the STM Working Group on Image Integrity.
She is an experienced trainer who has worked with Integrity teams at eLife, Elsevier, PNAS and others, and frequently speaks to early-career scientist audiences.
Jana obtained an MA in Linguistics from Westminster University, London in 2002 and has worked in Scientific Publishing for well over 20 years.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Ilaria Cianchetta
speakers.bio: Ilaria Cianchetta leads the Research Integrity and Trust Innovation Program at Elsevier, aimed at developing solutions to improve the credibility and reliability of scholarly publishing. With a PhD in chemistry and ten years of experience in the field, Ilaria has a strong background in scientific communication and publishing. She started her career as a , scientific editor before advancing to the role of Editor-in-Chief, gaining valuable insights into the needs of researchers to ensure the dissemination of high-quality scientific knowledge. Ilaria is currently dedicated to creating innovative strategies and tools that foster trust, transparency, and clarity in scholarly publishing. Her work aims to ensure that valuable and meaningful research findings are accessible, impactful, and trustworthy for the global research community.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Reda Cimmperman
speakers.organization: Office of the Ombudsperson for Academic Ethics and Procedures of the Republic of Lithuania
speakers.bio:
I am the Ombudsperson for Academic Ethics and Procedures. I hold a PhD in Biomedical Sciences from Vilnius University (VU), a Master’s degree in Management from ISM, a Master’s degree in Microbiology, and a Bachelor's degree in Biochemistry from VU. My career of more than 15 years at the Research Council of Lithuania (RCL) includes a variety of positions. At the RCL, I have served as Scientific (General) Secretary, Board Member, and Head of the Science Fund Secretariat. I have led projects funded by the European Commission on academic ethics (Pro-Ethics and IANUS), open science (OPUS), and enhancing gender equality in science and innovation (GENDERACTIONplus). I am an active member of various working groups and councils with important roles in science, policy, and academic ethics, e.g., I am a member of the project board of the European Commission’s iRECS: Improving Research Ethics Expertise and Competences to Ensure Reliability and OPUS: Open Universal Science, and a member of the European Network of Research Integrity Offices (ENRIO). I have been Vice-Chair of the Lithuanian National Commission for UNESCO, member of the working group on the European Commission ERA Policy 'Inclusive Gender Equality in the European Research Area’, Council Representative in the CoARA Coalition, member of the Lithuanian National Committee on Climate Change, member of the Steering and Coordination Committees of the Biodiversa, BiodivClim and BiodivRestore programmes of the ERA-NET Cofund H2020 scheme, etc.
I often host workshops on the future challenges of academic ethics (artificial intelligence). In this capacity, I am continuously studying the legal framework of the scientific system and I have published more than 10 scientific articles. 
In order to improve my professional performance, I try to actively present at conferences and continuously improve my knowledge during various trainings, including the two-year Mutual Learning Exchange Training ‘Research Ethics’ of the H2020 Policy Support Facility and the training ‘Academic Ethics in a Changing Society’ of the Mykolas Romeris University Centre for Academic Ethics.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Ilinca Ciubotariu
speakers.bio:
Dr. Ilinca Ciubotariu is an infectious disease scientist by training and currently an interdisciplinary faculty member at Johns Hopkins University, specializing in communication and research methods. Currently, Dr. Ciubotariu serves as the Assistant Director of the R³ Center for Innovation in Science Education (R³ISE). In this role, she is dedicated to training the next generation of STEM professionals, focusing on rigorous methods, data science, and ethics.
Her research encompasses genomic epidemiology, particularly in the context of malaria transmission dynamics and vaccine development, and SARS-CoV-2 variant tracking. Beyond her research, Dr. Ciubotariu is actively involved in enhancing science education. She has developed curricula for multiple graduate courses at JHSPH, integrating elements of philosophy to foster critical thinking in scientific training. Originally from Bucharest, Romania, she holds a PhD in Biological Sciences from Purdue University, an MHS in Molecular Microbiology & Immunology from JHSPH, and a BA in Biology from Columbia University.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Eileen Clancy
speakers.bio: Certified medical publication professional (CMPP) with proven track record of success over 8 years in managing and leading teams, developing journal policies, and executing tactical plans to deliver robust publication pipelines while enforcing best practices and ethics standards in publishing and research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Katie Corker
speakers.bio: Katie is executive director of ASAPbio. She earned a PhD in psychology from Michigan State University in 2012. Her interest in preprints was kindled in her role as executive officer of SIPS (the Society for the Improvement of Psychological Science), which launched and continues to steward PsyArXiv, the community-run preprint server for psychology research. Prior to joining ASAPbio, she was a faculty member at Kenyon College and Grand Valley State University, as well as a community engagement advisor for the Center for Open Science. She lives and works in Grand Rapids, MI, USA.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Gustaaf Cornelis
speakers.bio: Born in Belgium (1966), he studied philosophy of science in Brussles, Ghent and Athens. PhD  in 1995 on the philosophy of relativistic cosmology. Nowadays as professor teaching scientific integrity at the universities of Brussels and Antwerp and performing research on ethics and generative AI. Member of different ethical committees.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Ioana Cristea
speakers.bio: Ioana Alina Cristea is Associate Professor at the Department of General Psychology, University of Padova, Italy and a Research Affiliate at the Meta-Research Innovation Center at Stanford University, USA (METRICS). She is trained as a clinical psychologist and cognitive behavioral psychotherapist and has previously worked at Babes-Bolyai University, Romania, the Universities of Pisa and Pavia, Italy, Stanford University, USA and Vrije Universiteit, Amsterdam, the Netherlands. Between 2016 and 2017, she was a Fulbright Visiting Senior Scholar at Stanford University. Her field is best defined as meta-research (“research on research”), taking a bird’s eye view on how research is planned, conducted, reported, and used within and across different disciplines. She applies meta-research methods, such as meta-analyses, to clinically important questions, as for example how to best treat or prevent mental disorders in adults and children or how to improve psychotherapies.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Bibek Dahal
speakers.bio: Bibek Dahal, a PhD candidate in educational research at the University of Calgary, specializing in higher education leadership, policy and governance. Bringing Southern epistemologies, his research scholarship focuses on academic and research integrity in global higher education.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Adira Daniel
speakers.bio:
Adira Daniel is a PhD Candidate in Social, Personality, and Developmental Psychology at the University of Western Ontario, Canada. Her research explores how intercultural and diverse couples navigate relational identity, disclosure, and resilience while facing stigma and marginalization. Guided by feminist and intersectional perspectives, she is especially interested in how processes such as “we-ness” and cognitive interdependence shape relationship well-being across cultural contexts.

Alongside her dissertation, Adira contributes to applied projects on newcomer integration, disability and inclusion, and the hidden curriculum in research methods. She is deeply engaged in metaresearch, open science, and methodological innovation, with a focus on strengthening rigour in mixed-methods psychology.

Adira’s work is driven by a commitment to inclusive, community-focused research that connects theory with practice. She is active in knowledge mobilization networks and regularly develops accessible resources, teaching materials, and collaborative tools that foster equity, transparency, and meaningful participation in research.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Ilse De Jong
speakers.bio: Ilse de Jong is a PhD candidate at the Department of Ethics, Law, and Humanities at Amsterdam UMC. Her research addresses the quality and reproducibility of research. As part of her PhD research, she is involved in the TRUSTparency project, which is a three-year EU-funded project aiming to improve reproducibility through the co-creation of interventions that support a transparent and trustworthy research ecosystem. Before starting her PhD, Ilse completed a BA in Philosophy, a BSc in Pedagogical Sciences, and a MSc in Behavioural Science at Radboud University. During her Research Master, Ilse worked as a research data manager for the Behavioural Science Institute at Radboud University.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Muhammet Demirbilek
speakers.bio:
Prof. Dr. Muhammet Demirbilek is a distinguished academic with expertise in educational technology. He currently serves as a professor in the Department of Educational Sciences within the Faculty of Education at Suleyman Demirel University. Prof. Demirbilek holds a Ph.D. in Curriculum & Instruction with a specialization in Educational Technology, and a Master’s in the same field from the School of Education at the University of Florida. Additionally, he earned both B.S. and M.S. degrees in Electronics Engineering from Istanbul University.
Prof. Demirbilek's career is marked by significant contributions to the field of educational technology and his interdisciplinary approach spans artificial intelligence, machine learning, educational game design, gamification, mobile media, social networks, and virtual worlds. His global perspective is enriched by his work as a Post-Doctoral Researcher with the Games, Learning, and Society (GLS) research group at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, supported by The MacArthur Foundation.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nicholas DeVito
speakers.bio: Dr. Nicholas DeVito is a postdoctoral researcher and research integrity lead at the Bennett Institute for Applied Data Science at the University of Oxford. His research focuses on topics in metascience, specifically around how to improve the transparency, rigour, and reproducibility of research.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Ayu Putu Madri Dewi
speakers.bio: Ayu Putu Madri Dewi is a doctoral researcher at Amsterdam UMC, Department of Epidemiology and Data Science, within the OSIRIS project (Open Science to Improve Reproducibility in Science). Her research focuses on assessing how open science practices, such as data sharing, preregistration, and code sharing in peer review, can enhance reproducibility in biomedical research. She leads studies evaluating the use of open science checklists in journal peer review and in grant proposal assessment, combining quantitative analysis with meta-research methods. Trained as a veterinarian with a Master’s in Public Health majoring Biostatistics, Ayu brings an interdisciplinary background bridging public health, epidemiology, and data transparency. She is passionate about fostering a more open, ethical, and reproducible research culture through evidence-based policy and practical tools that support researchers, journals, and funders.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Jules Di Scala
speakers.bio: 2nd year PhD student.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Kris Dierickx
speakers.bio: Kris Dierickx is professor at the School of Medicine at KU Leuven  (Belgium).  Besides a research line in medical and research ethics, he is also very active in (national and European projects) the field of research integrity, focusing codes, training, supervision and values.  Kris was for 2 terms member of the Committee on Scientific Integrity of his university.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Robert Dijkgraaf
speakers.bio: The 2026 Steneck-Mayer lecturer is Robbert Dijkgraaf. He has been the president of the Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences, was the director of the Princeton Institute for Advanced Study and recently was Minister (Secretary of state) of Education, Culture and Science in the Netherlands. Currently Robbert Dijkgraaf is University Professor of Science and Society from an International Perspective at the University of Amsterdam and the President-Elect of the International Science Council. In his diverse roles Robbert Dijkgraaf has consistently taken a strong stance for academic freedom, research integrity and open science.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Thea Marie Drachen
speakers.bio: Thea Marie Drachen is a research librarian at the University Library of Southern Denmark. She has more than a decade of experience in the field of research integrity, with a particular focus on Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR). Her work encompasses both teaching and practice, and she has played a key role in developing and delivering RCR training for researchers at various career stages. Thea has contributed to an international research project on RCR and Questionable Research Practices (QRP), and along with colleagues she was instrumental in founding the Danish RCR Network, which fosters collaboration and knowledge exchange among professionals working with research integrity. In addition to her work in research integrity, Thea has worked in systematic reviews and bibliometric analyses, which she has applied in support of evidence-based research and strategic research evaluation, and along with colleagues she was instrumental in founding the Danish RCR Network, which fosters collaboration and knowledge exchange among professionals working with research integrity.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Haihong E
speakers.organization: Beijing University of Post and Telecommunication
speakers.bio: Haihong E is a Professor at the School of Computer Science (National School of Software), Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications. She also serves as Deputy Director of the Office of Cyber Information Affairs and Deputy Director of the Ministry of Education Engineering Research Center for Information Networks.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Sarah Elaine Eaton
speakers.bio:
Dr. Sarah Elaine Eaton, PhD, is a Professor and Research Chair in the Werklund School of Education and Chair, Leadership, Policy, and Governance specialization area. She holds a concurrent appointment as an Honorary Associate Professor, Deakin Learning Futures, Deakin University, Australia. 

Professor Eaton’s research focuses on academic ethics in higher education. Her work can be found in the Canadian Journal of Higher Education, the British Educational Research Journal, Educational Policy, and the Journal of Academic Ethics, among other places.

Dr. Eaton is the Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal for Educational Integrity (Springer Nature), a Q1 (Education) journal published by BMC Springer. Dr. Eaton was the co-founder (2018) and co-editor (2018-2020) of Canadian Perspectives on Academic Integrity. 

Dr. Eaton is an internationally recognized authority in the field of academic ethics. In 2020 she received the National Research and Scholarship award from the Canadian Society for the Study of Higher Education (CSSHE) for her contributions to research on academic integrity in Canadian higher education.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Vidar Enebakk
speakers.organization: The National Committees for Research Ethics, Norway
speakers.bio: Vidar Enebakk is Director of The National Committee for Research Ethics in Social Sciences and Humanities (NESH) since 2014. He has a PhD in science studies from 2005 and was both postdoc and researcher in history of science at the University of Oslo until 2012. During this period, he was also visiting student and visiting scholar at the Department of History and Philosophy of Science (HPS), University of Cambridge. In 2012, he started as senior curator at the Norwegian Museum of Science and Technology, and from 2016 to 2018 he was editor of the Norwegian academic journal Nytt Norsk Tidsskrift. Enebakk has co-edited two books on research ethics (in Norwegian), he has served on the programme committee for the ENRIO Congress in 2021, 2023 and 2025, and since 2023 he is also a member of the Commission for Research Integrity (CRI) within The Luxembourg Agency for Research Integrity (LARI).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Timothy Errington
speakers.bio: Tim Errington is the Senior Director of Research at the Center for Open Science (COS) that aims to increase openness, integrity, and reproducibility of scientific research. In that position he conducts and collaborates with researchers and stakeholders across scientific disciplines and organizations on metascience projects aimed to understand the current research process and evaluate initiatives designed to increase reproducibility and openness of scientific research. These include large scale replication and reproducibility projects such as the Reproducibility Project: Cancer Biology and the DARPA supported Systematizing Confidence in Open Research and Evidence (SCORE), and evaluation projects of new initiatives such as open science badges, Registered Reports, automated confidence assessment, and pilot programs to increase rigor and openness of research.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Natalie Evans
speakers.bio: Natalie Evans is an Associate Professor in the Department of Ethics, Law and Humanities at Amsterdam UMC. Her work explores how responsible research practices are conceptualized and embedded within academic communities, with a focus on virtue ethics, fairness, equity, and environmental responsibility in research conduct. She has played a leading role in major European research integrity projects, including RE4GREEN, PREPARED, AIOLIA, VIRT2UE, and EnTIRE. As Scientific Coordinator of VIRT2UE, she oversaw the development of a virtue ethics–based training program that prepared nearly 500 trainers and reached over 4,000 researchers across Europe. Through this work, she has also trained research integrity trainers from Europe, Africa, and North America. Natalie leads the Research Integrity Programme at Amsterdam UMC and is co-founder of the Embassy of Good Science, an international platform for sharing resources and fostering dialogue on research ethics and integrity.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Petra Falin
speakers.organization: Finnish National Board On Research Integrity Tenk
speakers.bio: Petra Falin is a senior adviser at the Finnish National Board on Research Integrity TENK. In this role, she monitors and coordinates the work of the ethics committees in human and social sciences at the national level. She also participates in the other tasks of TENK in promoting and monitoring research integrity.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Daniele Fanelli
speakers.bio: Daniele Fanelli is Assistant Professor in Social Research Methods at Heriot-Watt University and visiting Fellow in Quantitative Methodology at the London School of Economics. He has published numerous influential studies assessing the prevalence and causes of research misconduct and bias, and has proposed novel methodologies to forecast and quantify scientific progress and the level of scientific consensus. He is also member of the Committee for Ethics and Bioethics of Italy's CNR and of the Luxembourg Agency for Research Integrity, for which he advises and investigates allegations of research misconduct, and the creator of comCensus.org, a platform that aims to promote an inclusive approach to policy making.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Tom Farrelly
speakers.bio: Tom is an academic developer, particularly in eLearning, and a lecturer at Munster Technological University in Ireland where he teaches in the School of Health and Social Sciences, and is co-chair of the institutional Human Research Ethics Committee. He has a long history of working in e-learning and is particularly committed to supporting the transition of non-traditional learners to higher education (he does amazing work with the Irish Traveller communities), and international students. He’s also an open access advocate, and the Gasta Master (if you don’t know what Gasta is, check it out!). Tom has an academic background in social sciences and education.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Misty Fish
speakers.bio:
Dr Misty Fish is a lawyer with experience in research integrity, administrative and public interest disclosure investigations. Misty is the Manager of the Research Integrity Office at Curtin University and has previously worked as the Senior Manager Research Integrity at the University of Queensland. Misty is responsible for managing Research Integrity complaint matters and manages the Human and Animal Ethics teams. Misty’s role also involves oversight of Curtin’s Animal Ethics and Human Research Ethics Committees. 
With a PhD in law focussing on wild animal welfare and environmental law, Misty is well versed in animal welfare and conservation protections. Misty also has significant experience in external administrative review, having worked at the Queensland Ombudsman which included review of government agencies, correctional facilities, and universities, to ensure proper, transparent and accountable administrative process and governance.

speakers.title: Ms
speakers.name: Eleni Flack-Davison
speakers.position: Head: Research Legal, Compliance, Ethics And Integrity
speakers.bio:
Eleni Flack-Davison, an admitted attorney of South Africa, Legal Adviser, Research Compliance Manager, Head: Office of Research Integrity and the Research Data Protection Officer at the University of the Witwatersrand, Johannesburg. She is one of the founding fellows of the Wits MIND institute. Eleni is part of the SARIMA Research Management Portfolio Committee. 

Eleni was a special adviser to the LOC for the 7th World Conference on Research Integrity in Cape Town. She is part of the REASA Executive Committee and heads up the Marketing and Communications sub-Committee. She has been part of the ASSAf drafting Committee in relation to the POPIA Code of Conduct for Research in South Africa.

Eleni co-founded and co-Chairs the Northern Regions Research Ethics and Integrity Community of Practice (CoP) under SARIMA and co-Chairs the Steering Committee for the CoPs for Research Ethics and Integrity.  The CoP has drafted ethics guidelines and toolkit for the SADC Region in terms of the Nagoya Protocol. Eleni is part of the SARIMA Research Management Portfolio Committee.  Eleni is a Certified VIRT2UE Research Integrity Trainer with the Embassy of Good science under the EU and Amsterdam UMC.

speakers.title: Miss
speakers.name: Lauren Flintoft
speakers.bio:
Lauren Flintoft, Research Integrity Manager, IOP Publishing

Lauren manages the Research Integrity Team at IOP Publishing, which is responsible for investigating ethical cases and developing policies and processes to uphold research integrity. After completing her Master’s degree in Modern History in 2017, she has worked in research integrity focused roles at both Emerald Publishing and IOP Publishing. She is currently involved in several initiatives, including the NISO CRediT taxonomy group and an STM Integrity Hub working group. Her particular areas of interest include special issue ethics and papermill prevention.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Delwen Franzen
speakers.organization: BIH-QUEST Center For Responsible Research at Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin
speakers.bio: I am a postdoctoral researcher at the BIH-QUEST Center for Responsible Research at Charité - Universitätsmedizin in Berlin. After studying Neuroscience at the University of Edinburgh, I completed a PhD in Systemic Neuroscience at LMU Munich, specializing in auditory neurophysiology and sound localization. My current research explores ways to support the uptake of responsible research practices, with a focus on improving transparency in clinical trials. I conduct meta-research to understand current practice and pilot communication strategies to support improvement. My broader interests include open research, scholarly communication, research assessment and culture.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Jun Fudano
speakers.bio: Jun Fudano was Professor at the Center for Higher Education Studies, Waseda University, until March, 2026.  He is Professor Emeritus of the Kanazawa Institute of Technology.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Simon Fuglsang
speakers.bio: Simon Fuglsang studies the relationship between science and society. His primary focus is the public's relationship to science, attitudes towards science, knowledge of science, and the relationships between these aspects.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Ann Gaeth
speakers.bio:
For more than 15 years, Dr Gaeth has worked in research administration at Swinburne University of Technology, in roles that supported human and animal ethics committees to her current role as the Research Integrity Coordinator. She manages the research integrity complaints process along with delivering an education program to support researchers’ knowledge of best practice in ethics and integrity. She focuses on encouraging research integrity as one of the pillars of research excellence.

Dr Gaeth has a BSc(Hon) and PhD in evolutionary biology from the University of Melbourne and has worked in biomedical research in Australia and the USA. She combines her practical experience with detailed knowledge of the complexities of ethics, compliance, and best practice with the aim of ensuring that investigations are fair and reasonable.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Wei Lin Gan
speakers.bio: Wei Lin leads the Research Integrity, Compliance, Ethics and Support Unit at A*STAR’s Research Office, where she provides oversight for research integrity and ethics frameworks, and manages the review and investigation of potential research conduct concerns. In this role, she also oversees scientific reviews and initiatives that underpin the organisation’s commitment to scientific excellence. She has held roles at the A*STAR Graduate Academy and A*ccelerate Technologies Pte Ltd, A*STAR’s commercialisation arm (presently known as A*STAR’s Innovation & Enterprise Division).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Barna Ganguly
speakers.bio:
Dr. (Mrs) BARNA GANGULY, joined in the year 1999 as Assistant Professor, has been working as Professor in the Department of Pharmacology, Pramukh Swami Medical College (PSMC), Karamsad, Gujarat since 2006. She passed M.B;B.S, from Calcutta University, M.D (Pharmacology) from Aligarh Muslim University,  PG Dip Bioethics from IGNOU supported by ICMR &NIH, Ph.D (Bioethics) from Faculty of Medicine, University of Porto, Portugal. She. She has got more than 32 years of teaching experience in medical colleges like J.N.Medical College, AMU, Manipal College of Medical Sciences, Pokhara, Nepal including that of current institution. As a post graduate teacher, she has guided 14 candidates for M.D. Pharmacology who have successfully completed the course. She has also guided six M.Pharm candidates, four candidates who were enrolled under her for Ph.D in Pharmacology. She underwent training on “Problem based Pharmacotherapy teaching – WHO model of P drug Concept” in Groningen, The Netherlands in 1999 and thereafter introduced the model for teaching Pharmacology for UG students in the department in 2000 which was the first of its kind in India. She was also trained in Clinical Pharmacology from PGI, Chandigarh in 1999. In the year 2015, PSMC got approval for establishment of UNESCO Bioethics Chair Unit (presently named as WMA support Centre of Bioethics Chair Program) with an objective of strengthening bioethics teaching and Dr. Ganguly is heading the same. She has been continuously engaged in ethics committee related  activities  within and outside the institution since 2003. She is a dedicated and recognized GCP trainer.
Her area of interest is Clinical Pharmacology and Bioethics. She has 52 publications (10 of them are on Bioethics) in various national and international journal of repute (including Q1) and 6 chapters in 3 books by national and international repute.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Orkhon Gantogtokh
speakers.bio: Orkhon Gantogtokh is a Ph.D. candidate in Education Studies at the University of British Columbia (UBC), a Public Scholar, and a Fellow of the Institute of Asian Research (IAR). She currently serves as the Executive Director of the Academy for Higher Education Development. Holding an MSc in Higher Education from the University of Oxford (2016), Orkhon actively contributes to higher education reform in Mongolia through civic engagement, research, and participation in national policy initiatives. She has chaired the Higher Education Subcommittee of the Education Reform Movement since 2019 to address education quality challenges. Her professional experience includes positions as a Higher Education Specialist at the Higher Education Reform Project, Researcher at the London School of Economics Enterprise, and Research and Partnership Manager at the Mongolian National Council for Education Accreditation. She has also consulted for the National University of Mongolia and the Mongolian University of Science and Technology.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nathalie Gaudreault
speakers.position: Sr Advisor for Microscopy, Dissemination, and Alliances
speakers.bio: Nathalie Gaudreault is an accomplished scientist with a rich academic and professional background. She earned her Master's degree in Endocrinology at Laval University and her PhD in Physiology at the University of British Columbia. Following her PhD, Nathalie completed post-doctoral research in vascular biology at UC Davis and UCSF. She then joined the Allen Institute for Brain Science and later transitioned to the Allen Institute for Cell Science, where she led the Microscopy Team. In this role, she was responsible for designing standardized pipelines for handling large image datasets, with an emphasis on quality control and calibration. Nathalie is currently serving as an Advisor and is responsible for Dissemination and Alliances at the Allen Institute for Cell Science.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Fred Yao Gbagbo
speakers.bio: I am a Public Health Consultant with specialization in Population and Health, Sexual Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR), Bioethics, Health Policy Analysis, and Reviews. I have 25 years of cumulative work experience in public, private and NGO Health sectors in Ghana and internationally. I have served on several boards/committees in various capacities and collaborated with multiple institutions/teams on SRHR projects. My research outputs have contributed significantly to informing SRHR policy and program development in Ghana and beyond. I am currently an Associate Professor at the University of Education, Winneba, Ghana.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Björn Gerlach
speakers.bio:
Björn Gerlach is co-founder of PAASP GmbH and co-founder and treasurer of the non-profit Guarantors of EQIPD e.V. (GoEQIPD), both based in Heidelberg, Germany. He also serves as Quality Manager at the Central Institute of Mental Health (CIMH) in Mannheim and, since 2024, as researcher at GoEQIPD, contributing to projects under this initiative.
He studied biology and earned his PhD at Imperial College London, focusing on immune cell signaling. After several years at Abbott/AbbVie studying microglia signaling in multiple sclerosis, he co-founded PAASP in 2015, the first full-service consultancy assessing risks in biomedical research. He supported several publicly funded projects, including the EU funded project EQIPD, and worked as an auditor for preclinical data quality.
In 2021, he co-founded GoEQIPD to ensure continued governance of the EQIPD Quality System. He is a regular invited speaker on research quality, data integrity, and best research practices.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Richard Gold
speakers.bio: Richard Gold brings 35 years of academic and private-sector experience to his policy work on innovation, economic growth, and open science. A former technology lawyer at a leading international firm, he currently holds the title of Distinguished James McGill Professor in Innovation and Intellectual Property, Director of McGill’s Centre for Intellectual Property Policy (CIPP), and Chief Policy and Partnerships Officer at Conscience. With training in law (LL.B [Toronto], LL.M and S.J.D [Michigan]) and computer science (B.Sc. [McGill]), he integrates technical, business, and legal expertise to investigate how law interacts with other social systems shaping innovation outcomes.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Cory Goldstein
speakers.bio: Cory E. Goldstein is a Postdoctoral Fellow with the Methodological and Implementation Research Program at the Ottawa Hospital Research Institute and the School of Epidemiology and Public Health at the University of Ottawa. His research interests lie at the intersection of ethics and clinical trial design, with a focus on ethical issues raised by pragmatic randomized controlled trials that use innovative designs and electronic health records. Dr. Goldstein is currently working an international team of ethicists, healthcare providers, scientists, trialists, patient partners, and members of the public to co-develop and disseminate guidance for the ethical design and conduct of cluster randomized trials. Dr. Goldstein received a B.A. in philosophy from McGill University, and an M.A. and Ph.D. in philosophy from Western University.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Rachael Gooberman-Hill
speakers.position: Co-chair, UK Committee on Research Integrity
speakers.bio: Rachael is Co-chair of the UK Committee on Research Integrity, and is Professor of Health and Anthropology at the University of Bristol. She has spent most of her career to date working in interdisciplinary research. Rachael has served on research advisory groups, steering groups, funding panels, and as a trustee and committee member of scholarly associations.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Christopher Graf
speakers.bio: Chris Graf is the Research Integrity Director at Springer Nature, in charge of leading the continued development and implementation of Springer Nature’s research integrity strategy, leading a team accountable for assuring the highest standards for research integrity in all of Springer Nature’s publications, working with many good colleagues inside Springer Nature and with many good academic partners outside Springer Nature. Chris also serves the publishing sector with a voluntary role at the STM Association, the global trade association for academic and professional publishers, where he chairs a committee of senior publishing industry executives overseeing governance of the STM Integrity Hub, and the STM Research Integrity Committee. In 2023 Chris was appointed to a position as committee member of the UK Committee on Research Integrity, a new organisation hosted by UKRI, that promotes research integrity across the UK and internationally.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Julia Gunn
speakers.bio: Amy Flanders Milligan earned her PhD studying publishing history at the University of Oxford before taking up postdoctoral fellowships at New York University and the University of London. She then moved into scholarly publishing with roles at Oxford University Press and Taylor & Francis in digital archives and open research. She now works as the Associate Director for Research and Impact at the Said Business School, University of Oxford. She also serves the Association of Research Managers & Administrators (ARMA) UK as a champion of the Metaresearch Special Interest Group and on the editorial board of ARMA Insights magazine.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Lisa Häberlein
speakers.bio: Lisa Häberlein is a Research Project Manager and Co-Team Leader at the European Network of Research Ethics Committees (EUREC). As a workpackage leader, she is involved in various EU-funded projects focused on Responsible Research and Innovation (RRI), Research Ethics (RE), and Research Integrity (RI), including VERITY, STRATEGIC, ROSiE, ETHNA System, and Path2Integrity. With a strong academic background in Philosophy, Interdisciplinary Ethics, and Education, Lisa has a deep research interest in moral concerns. She is actively involved in the Department of Medical Ethics and Human Rights at FAU Erlangen-Nuremberg, the Council for Research in Values and Philosophy (CRVP), and the Network of Young Ethics Experts of Medical Ethics Committees in Germany (AKEK).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Karla Hagan
speakers.position: Study Director, Consensus Study On Corrections And Retractions
speakers.bio: Karla Hagan is a Senior Program Officer at the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine. In this role, she served as Staff Director of the National Science, Technology, and Security Roundtable, a Congressionally-mandated activity that convened experts across academia, government, the intelligence community, and the private sector to examine challenges and opportunities around research security. She currently serves as Study Director for a consensus study on corrections and retractions of scientific journal articles, which launched spring 2025. Before this, Karla led the science and innovation team at the British Embassy in Washington, where she worked on bilateral United States-United Kingdom policy and diplomacy across the science and innovation spectrum, including clean energy technologies like offshore wind and fusion; artificial intelligence; quantum science and technologies; and research security. Originally from the Space Coast of Florida, Karla earned her B.S. in Physics from Georgia Institute of Technology and her Ph.D. in experimental high energy physics from University of Virginia.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Habtamu Hailu
speakers.bio: I am a Lecturer at the Ethiopian Defense University, College of Health Sciences. I hold a Master of Public Health in Epidemiology and Biostatistics with a specialization in Health Research Ethics from Addis Ababa University, School of Public Health. My teaching and research focus on epidemiological methods, biostatistics, and ethical issues in health research. I have a strong interest in research integrity, particularly research misconduct and the responsible conduct of research in health-related studies. I have contributed to several community-based and institutional health projects and presented my work in academic and professional forums. As an early-career researcher from Africa, I am passionate about promoting ethical and transparent research practices through education, mentorship, and collaboration. My long-term goal is to advance research integrity and foster a culture of responsible science in health research across low- and middle-income countries.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Prakash Hande
speakers.bio: Dr. M. Prakash Hande is a researcher and educator specialising in radiation biology, telomere biology, and genomic instability. He has authored over 180 peer-reviewed articles in leading journals such as Nature Genetics, Cell, Science, Genes and Development, and PNAS, with a Scopus H-index of 56 (~15,000 citations). At NUS, he teaches Cancer Biology and Ageing, and has conducted interdisciplinary and STS-based courses (e.g., Biomedicine and Singapore Society, Radiation and Society). In addition, he coordinated workshops and gave lectures on Responsible Conduct of Research for graduate students for more than 15  years and delivering educational lectures on research integrity. He currently serves on Committee 1 of the International Commission on Radiological Protection (ICRP), co-chairs Task Group 121, contributes to Task Group 118, and is the recipient of the AARR Senior Level Scientist Award 2025 from the Asian Association for Radiation Research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Tamarinde Haven
speakers.bio: Tamarinde Haven is an Assistant Professor in Research Methodology for the Social Behavioural Sciences. She has a background in psychology, philosophy and epidemiology. Her PhD focused on fostering a responsible research climate for integrity. After that, she worked as a postdoc at the Charité UMC in Berlin where she started her research into responsible supervision. This led to a Rubicon grant from NWO to stay at Aarhus University to further investigate the concept of responsible supervision. She recently obtained a VENI grant to strengthen rigour in mixed methods research. Tamarinde’s research interests include research integrity, methodology, open science, and qualitative and mixed methods research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Emily Hazlett
speakers.bio: Dr. Emily Hazlett has been with the Duke University Office of Scientific Integrity since 2022. She manages the Duke Faculty and Staff Responsible Conduct of Research training program. Prior to joining DOSI, Dr. Hazlett received her PhD in Biomedical Sciences studying neuroethology and then spent several years working as a social researcher for the North Carolina state government.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Calvin Heal
speakers.bio: Calvin Heal is a UK-based Research Fellow in Biostatistics, currently developing a tool (INSPECT-IPD) to identify problematic randomised controlled trials when individual participant data are available.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: James Heathers
speakers.bio: I am the director of The Medical Evidence Project, a project of The Center for Scientific Integrity. The MEP aims to reduce harm to patients by finding and making known serious errors in the medical literature. The MEP is especially interested in detecting and shining light on errors arising from low-quality science and fraudulent work in areas that involve large numbers of patients. I have worked in forensic metascience for ten years. My  PhD in Computational Psychophysiology (Usyd)

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Maura Hiney
speakers.organization: University College Dublin Institute for Discovery

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Renee Hoch
speakers.bio: Dr Renee Hoch is Head of Publishing Ethics at PLOS. After a research career in developmental neurobiology, Dr Hoch entered scientific publishing in 2013 as an associate editor at PLOS One. She joined the PLOS Publication Ethics team in 2018 and is now Head of Publication Ethics.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Alex Holcombe
speakers.bio: In his lab, Alex conducts attention and perception experiments on topics such as how humans keep track of moving objects. Outside the lab, Alex has been involved in improving researcher practices in science through initiatives such as the metascience publishing platform MetaROR, the Association for Interdisciplinary Meta-research and Open Science, the Free Journal Network, Registered Replication Reports, the WikiJournal of Science, and the creation of the journal Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Krista Holmes
speakers.bio: Dr. Krista Holmes is passionate in her commitment to advancing research and innovation through academic, industry and community partnerships and collaboration. With extensive expertise in research ethics, she is actively engaged in policy and advocacy initiatives to establish and maintain high quality and standards for the responsible conduct of research provincially, nationally and internationally.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Mark Hooper
speakers.bio: Dr Mark Hooper is the Founder and Director of Tricky Goose Training. He has some twenty years' experience designing and delivering effective education programs, managing people and projects, and facilitating change across the academic, industry, and government sectors. Mark is also a Council Member for the Committee on Publication Ethics.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Lyn Horn
speakers.position: Director Offfice of Research Integrity retired
speakers.bio: Lyn Horn is currently the Director of the Office of Research Integrity at the University of Cape Town, South Africa and an Extraordinary [Honorary] Associate Professor in the Centre for Applied Ethics at Stellenbosch University. She is a medical doctor with a PhD in Bioethics and worked as a clinician in the public health sector for the first twenty years of her career before transitioning to an academic work environment in 2004. She has been working in the fields of research ethics and integrity for the last almost twenty years and served as the Research Integrity Officer (RIO) at Stellenbosch University from 2011 to 2016. In her current role she is responsible for the institutional-wide promotion of responsible research practice, including RCR education and training. She was the South African Co-Chair for the 7th WCRI 2022.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Mohammad Hosseini
speakers.bio: Dr. Mohammad Hosseini is an assistant professor at Northwestern University Feinberg School of Medicine. His research is focused on ethical implications of using artificial intelligence (AI) in research and publications, research ethics and integrity and open science. He is involved in and leads various ethics projects in national and international organizations (e.g., Association of Professional and Practical Ethics, National Information Standards Organization, National Academy of Sciences, and Global Young Academy). He teaches research ethics and integrity and responsible conduct of research at Northwestern University and various international institutions.

speakers.title: Mr
speakers.name: Jason Hu
speakers.bio:
Jason Hu is the Director of Research Integrity Engagement at Taylor & Francis Publishing Group. Prior to this role, he has held several key positions at ORCID and Wiley. He has been leading strategic partnership, journal management, business development, editorial outreach, market insights and strategy, with extensive industry experience and deep insights of the global academic publishing landscape.
Mr. Hu has been serving as a Council Member of the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE) since 2016, actively participating in the development of key ethical guidelines and discussions of challenging cases. In parallel, he also serves as the Chair of United2Act Education Working Group, Trustee of the Publishing Training Centre (UK), Advisor of the Asian Council of Science Editors and Advisor of the Publishing Journal.
Mr. Hu graduated from the University of Chicago with a Master of Business Administration (MBA) degree.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Gilles Hubert
speakers.bio:
Gilles Hubert is an Associate Professor in Computer Science at the University of Toulouse, France. He holds a PhD (2010) and a habilitation degree (2010) from the University of Toulouse. He is at the head of the Information Retrieval and Information Synthesis (IRIS) research team at the IRIT laboratory, France.
His research interests includes Information Retrieval, automatic summarization and more globally text analysis.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Giulia Inguaggiato
speakers.bio: Giulia Inguaggiato, is Assistant professor at the department of Ethics Law and Humanities of Amsterdam University Medical Centre (NL). Her expertise lies in ethics, and research integrity with a focus on education, clinical ethics support and training.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Kalypso Iordanou
speakers.bio: Kalypso Iordanou is Professor of Psychology at UCLan Cyprus and Director of theThinkingLab. She holds a PhD in Psychology from Columbia University, New York. Her research focuses on argumentation skills, reasoning, my-side bias, epistemic cognition, metacognition, and particularly in using technology to support the development of critical thinking skills. She is currently part of the Horizon-Europe projects VERITY, SMIDGE and BEYOND, for promoting public trust in science, research integrity and tackle conspiratorial thinking in social media. . She has held sponsored visiting research positions at Columbia University (2017) as a Fulbright award recipient, and the University of Geneva (2025). She serves as Associate Editor of the Journal of Educational Psychology. She received the Cyprus Research Award-Young Researcher in Social Sciences and Humanities from the Research and Innovation Foundation in 2015 for her work on argumentation skills.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Tetsuji Iseda
speakers.bio: Graduated from Kyoto University in 1991, and received Ph. D. in philosophy from University of Maryland in 2001. Employed at Nagoya University in 1999, and have been teaching at Kyoto University since 2008.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Liz Jackson
speakers.bio: Liz Jackson is Karen Lo Eugene Chuang Professor in Diversity and Equity at the University of Hong Kong and the Associate Dean (Research) of its Faculty of Education. She is President of the Comparative Education Society of Hong Kong and Editor-in-Chief of Educational Philosophy and Theory. She is the author of over 200 books, journal articles, and book chapters, including  Beyond Virtue: The Politics of Educating Emotions, Questioning Allegiance: Resituating Civic Education and Emotions: Philosophy of Education in Practice.

speakers.title: Miss
speakers.name: Sarah Jenkins
speakers.position: Senior Director, Research Integrity & Publishing Ethics
speakers.bio:
Sarah Jenkins is the Director for Research Integrity & Publishing Ethics at Elsevier.  Sarah and her team promote research integrity through policies, best practices, and education, support Elsevier’s Publishing Teams and Editors to investigate and resolve ethics cases and work with experts across Elsevier to build tools and develop processes to detect unethical practices during the manuscript submission and peer-review process. 

Sarah began her publishing career at Elsevier working with editors and learned societies in health and medical sciences.  Sarah later joined the Physical Sciences group as a Publishing Director where she focused on open access and open science through initiatives that promoted the deposition of code and software, and established links between original research articles and data journals.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Joonha Jeon
speakers.organization: Center for Science and Technology Studies (CWTS), Leiden University
speakers.bio: Joonha Jeon is a PhD candidate at the Centre for Science and Technology Studies (CWTS), Leiden University. He holds a Master’s degree from the Graduate School of Science and Technology Policy (STP) at KAIST. Prior to his doctoral studies, he worked in the IT industry in South Korea for several years, with a focus on data analytics and artificial intelligence. His research interests lie at the intersection of science and technology studies (STS) and valuation studies, particularly examining research systems and scholarly communication.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Lene Os Johannessen
speakers.position: Advisor and Director of the National Committee for Research Ethics on Human Remains
speakers.bio: Lene Os Johannessen is a Senior Advisor at the National Research Ethics Committees (NREC) in Norway and serves as Director of the National Committee for Research Ethics on Human Remains. She also leads NREC’s project on the development of training resources in research integrity and research ethics. Johannessen holds a PhD in Classical Archaeology from the University of Oslo, awarded in 2015. She has previously held positions as a lecturer in archaeology and as a research advisor at the University of Oslo.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Carl Walter Matthias Kaiser
speakers.bio: Matthias Kaiser is Professor Emeritus at the Center for the Study of the Sciences and Humanities (SVT), University of Bergen, and Senior Researcher at the Norwegian Institute for Sustainability Studies (NORSUS), having studied at the universities of Munich, Oslo, Stanford and Frankfurt. He is also an Affiliated Fellow at the Koi Tū: Centre for Informed Futures, University of Auckland, and Editor-in-Chief of the Springer journal Food Ethics. Areas of expertise: philosophy of science (Dr.phil.), ethics of science, food ethics, technology assessment and science-for-policy.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Takehito Kamata
speakers.bio: Takehito Kamata, Ph.D., is an Assistant Professor by Special Appointment at Sophia University in Tokyo, Japan. He was a fellow in the inaugural Japan-U.S. Science Communication & Policy Fellowship Network Program and received the Distinguished International Alumni Award from the University of Minnesota College of Education and Human Development in 2023. Currently, he serves as Vice Chair of the International Exchange Committee (2024-2026) at the Association for the Promotion of Research Integrity in Japan. In 2026, he was a Japan Chair Visiting Fellow at the Hudson Institute, conducting research on research security from January to February, with the Cabinet Office of Japan’s endorsement. His research interests encompass international research collaborations, research integrity, research security, higher education internationalization, and related subjects in science, technology, and innovation policy within the context of higher education.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Rosemary Kamuyu
speakers.bio: Rosemary Kamuyu is a health economist and a monitoring and evaluation officer at the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI) where she has been working for the last 7 years. Rose has been involved in a long-term bioethics training project funded by the National Institute of Health (NIH) and research regulatory and oversight projects funded by EDCTP II including SERCEA. She is involved in supporting ethics committees and regulators build capacity for their members especially with several new committees constituted. There are several new research projects that go through the KEMRI ethics committee so constantly supporting capacity strengthening is key. Rose is currently working on a new proposal to support establishment of a research integrity office in KEMRI. She is skilled in conducting qualitative and quantitative data analysis, generating data reports in compliance with procedures, agreements, and evaluation systems.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Kaixiang Kang
speakers.bio:
Kaixiang Kang is a Specially Appointed Assistant Professor at the Higher Education Promotion Organization of the Prefectural University of Hiroshima. His research interests focus on higher education, and he is dedicated to using simulation methods such as Agent-Based Modeling (ABM) to explain various phenomena in the field.
He is currently a doctoral candidate in Higher Education at Hiroshima University's Graduate School of Humanities and Social Sciences, where he also earned his master's degree. Since April 2024, he has held his current position at the Prefectural University of Hiroshima.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Panagiotis Kavouras
speakers.bio: Panagiotis Kavouras is a researcher at the Institute of Health and Society, Centre for Medical Ethics, Faculty of Medicine, University of Oslo and at the Association of Hellenic Academic Libraries, at the Center for Libraries and Informatics at the Aristotle University of Thessaloniki. For almost two decades, his research interests focused on materials technology. Since 2017, his research interests focused on Research Integrity. He has been involved in many EU-funded projects, related to Research Integrity and Research Ethics. Currently, he is the scientific coordinator of the TRUSTparency project. He is member of the program committee of the ENRIO Congresses on Research Integrity Practice and he was member of the local organizing committee of the 8th World Conference on Research Integrity. In addition to his academic track record, he has written extensively in the Greek language, mainly on issues related to Research Integrity, in a magazine for science, technology and society.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Kelebogile Kenalemang
speakers.bio:
Dr. Kelebogile Kenalemang is a Senior Lecturer and Programme Leader for International Finance and Banking at the Botswana Accountancy College (BAC). She is an early-career researcher whose work focuses on advancing ethical, inclusive, and technology-driven research and learning systems in higher education. Her research examines how emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence can be responsibly adopted within teaching, research, and entrepreneurship development across the Global South.

Dr. Kenalemang has published on knowledge-based economies, innovation in SMEs, and sustainable education practices. She contributes to the British Council–funded multi-country project on gender and sustainability careers and authored internationally published case studies with the UK Quality Assurance Agency (QAA) on inclusive student engagement. At BAC, she mentors student societies, leads programme development, and drives academic–industry collaboration that strengthens integrity, employability, and research impact. A regular contributor to national media and policy dialogues, she champions ethical innovation and the democratization of knowledge in African higher education.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Tim Kersjes
speakers.bio: Tim Kersjes is the Head of Research Integrity, Resolutions at Springer Nature. The Resolutions team consists of highly committed research integrity expects who support in-house editors and Editors-in-Chief in handling and resolving all types of research integrity and publication ethics issues. The cases vary from plagiarism and authorship disputes to peer review manipulation, medical-ethical concerns, data fabrication and image manipulation in submitted manuscripts and published articles. Tim has worked in publishing for over 10 years, almost 9 of which in research integrity. He has advised on thousands of research integrity cases small and large, and has written hundreds of retraction notices. He is also proud to serve as an elected trustee at the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Sora Kim
speakers.organization: Kistep(Korea Institute of S&T Evaluation and Planning)

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Timothy Kiplagat
speakers.bio:
Mr. Timothy Kipkosgei is a Research Scientist at the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI). He is currently the SERU Compliance officer for CGHR and CIPDCR and a member of committee A at SERU. Timothy obtained his Master of Science degree in International Health from JKUAT and a Bachelor of Science degree in Biochemistry from Egerton University.
He also holds a Post Graduate Diploma in Bioethics from the Centre of Biomedical Ethics and Culture (CBEC), SIUT, Pakistan. Timothy’s research interest focuses on Bioethics, Public Health and Health Systems research. He is part of the KEMRI SERU training team and a member of Bioethics society of Kenya

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Lois Klassen
speakers.organization: Emily Carr University Of Art + Design
speakers.bio: Lois Klassen is a research ethics coordinator, writer and artist working and living on Coast Salish territory (Vancouver, Canada). She has published and presented widely on research ethics in the context of art and design scholarship. Klassen is an Adjunct Professor at Simon Fraser University’s Critical Media Arty Studio and an Adjunct Researcher at Emily Carr University. Her texts have appeared in The Conversation, Media-N, Public Journal, Parse Journal, Fillip Magazine, Journal of Canadian Studies, and in the books Forced Migration In/To Canada (C. Clark-Kazak, ed), and Diffracting the North (G. Aceves Sepúlveda, A. Álvarez Hernández, Z. Zarza, eds.). Her art and research have been presented at Plymouth University, Concordia University, The Reach Gallery and Museum, Agnes Etherington Art Centre, SOMA (Mexico), and more.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Svetlana Kleiner
speakers.bio: I am a linguist working with Springer Nature Research Integrity

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Willie Koh
speakers.bio: Dr Willie Koh is the Director of the Research Integrity & Ethics Office at the Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore, where he leads initiatives in research governance and the proper conduct of research. He is dedicated to strengthening institutional frameworks that uphold research integrity, data management, and regulatory compliance, ensuring NTU remains at the forefront of responsible and trustworthy science. With a strong track record in shaping policies and guiding best practices, he plays a central role in supporting faculty, researchers, and students in building a culture of trust in research. Dr Koh’s background is in the life sciences, with a PhD in Virology from University College London and a B.Sc from the National University of Singapore.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Jos Kole
speakers.position: Associate Professor Professional and Research Integrity Education
speakers.bio: Associate professor in professional and research integrity education, specialised in ethics and moral philosophy, stationed at the Ethics of Healthcare Department of the Radboud university medical Center, Nijmegen, The Netherlands.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Francis Kombe
speakers.bio:
Francis Kombe is the current Chief Executive Officer, EthiXPERT, South Africa and a Public Health practitioner and bioethicist, with a passion in Research Integrity. He has a wealth of experience working in international health research institutions, where held various leadership positions. Kombe holds an MSocSc (research ethics) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal (UKZN), South Africa; Master of Public Health (MPH) and Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health (PGDPH) from London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine-UK (LSHTM) and is currently awaiting to graduate from a Wellcome Trust funded PhD at UKZN focusing on the implications of fieldworkers’ institutional support systems for research integrity practices in Africa.

He is a co-founder and chairperson of the African Research Integrity Network (ARIN), a member of the USA National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine (NASEM) and the Strategic Advisory Board for the EU’s AI ethics in human cognition and behaviour project. He served as a member of the programme planning committee during the 6th and 7th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong and Cape Town respectively. He has published widely in the field of research ethics, community engagement, frontline staff and research integrity among others.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Cynthia Komolafe
speakers.bio: Komolafe Cynthia is a researcher and holds a master’s degree in Bioethics with a focus on the Responsible Conduct of Research and a Bachelor’s degree in Microbiology. My research interests lie at the intersection of research integrity, scientific ethics, and biomedical science. I have conducted empirical studies on mechanisms for reporting and responding to research misconduct in Nigerian universities. I also explore issues in microbiology, antibiotic resistance, and the sustainable use of biological resources. My ongoing work seeks to strengthen institutional frameworks that foster transparency, accountability, and trust in research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Gowri Krishna
speakers.bio: Dr. Gowri Gopalakrishna is an epidemiologist and public health scientist with expertise in research integrity, open science, and responsible conduct of research. She led the Dutch National Survey on Research Integrity, one of the most comprehensive studies globally on research misconduct and questionable research practices. Currently, she coordinates and oversees efforts around research integrity and certification of journals in Elsevier with a research affiliation at Maastricht University, where she conducts empirical research on issues of research integrity. Prior to her current positions, she made notable contributions to the control and prevention of the SARS epidemic in 2003 and later in the Covid-19 pandemic. She also actively contributes to national and international advisory bodies, funding agencies and is a well-known public speaker on the topic of research integrity.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Paul Kutyabami
speakers.bio:
Dr. Paul Kutyabami is a Pharmacist and Senior Lecturer at Makerere University, specializing in Bioethics and health systems strengthening. He holds a Master's in Bioethics and is currently pursuing a PhD focused on ethical data-sharing in resource-limited settings.

Dr. Kutyabami is a key leader in research governance, currently chairing the School of Health Sciences Research and Ethics Committee and previously serving on the School of Medicine's REC. A pioneer in research ethics capacity building, he spearheaded national training programs in Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR), GCP, GCLP, and GRRP. His work, supported by grants from agencies like NIH and EDCTP, focuses on policy development, research regulation, and mentoring the next generation of health researchers and ethics professionals in Uganda.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Aliya Kuzhabekova
speakers.bio: Dr. Aliya Kuzhabekova is an Associate Professor in Leadership, Policy, and Governance specialization in the Werklund School of Education. Dr. Kuzhabekova has extensive experience as a faculty member, administrator and scholar in international and Canadian contexts. Upon graduation from PhD in Higher Education Policy and Administration, Aliya was a Postdoctoral Scholar at the Centre for Science, Technology and Public Policy in her alma mater in the U.S.. She then worked as an Assistant and then Associate Professor of Higher Education Leadership at Nazarbayev University in Kazakhstan, where she became a recognized scholar in international education and higher education reform in post-Soviet Central Asia.
