She has produced a number of publications on such topics as 1) research capacity building in higher education in transitional contexts, doctoral education reform and researcher development; 2) internationalization and international mobility (faculty mobility, mobility of graduate students, immigrant student experiences); 3) equity issues in higher education (women in higher education leadership, women and international mobility, women in STEM fields). After relocation to Canada in 2018, Aliya worked as a Postdoctoral Scholar at Carleton University and a part-time Professor at the University of Ottawa. Aliya has supervised over 30 Master's and graduate students, many of whom were non-native speakers of English. She has managed 10 funded research projects, including grants from the Ministry of Education of Kazakhstan, Norwegian Institute for International Affairs, and Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (Insight grant, Partnership Engage and Partnership Development grants). Aliya's research and teaching are based on her diverse administrative in higher education settings, including experience as an Executive Assistant to university presidents of two international universities in Kazakhstan, leadership positions on university committees and service as a member of the National Research Council in the area of education in Kazakhstan. Aliya is an active member of the Comparative and International Education Society and Canadian Society for the Study of Higher Education.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nancy Kwangwa
speakers.bio:
Dr. Nancy Kwangwa

Dr. Nancy Kwangwa is a distinguished library and information professional committed to promoting digital inclusion, research integrity, AI  and media literacy and equitable access to information. She currently serves as Deputy Librarian and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Champion at the Women’s University in Africa, where she leads initiatives in research support, information and digital literacy, and scholarly communication. Dr. Kwangwa has developed and implemented programmes on information and digital literacy that have impacted over 10,000 community members through training and resources.
Dr. Kwangwa is an award-winning researcher, and her work concentrates on media and information literacy, research integrity, AI literacy, and community development, highlighting the essential role of library professionals in empowering communities to navigate the digital age and uphold information integrity. She advocates for inclusive, rights-based access to information, especially for marginalised groups.
She holds a PhD from the University of Cape Town, South Africa, and degrees from the National University of Science and Technology, Zimbabwe. In pursuit of ongoing professional development, she participated in the U.S. Department of State’s Community Solutions Programme. In recognition of her contributions to community development and academic librarianship, she was nominated as a specialist and fellow of the U.S. Department of State’s Community Engagement Exchange Programme and an International Visitor Leadership Program (IVLP), respectively.
She is an active member of several professional networks, including UNESCO Women 4 AI Ethics, the Zimbabwe Library Association, and IFLA Law Libraries Section. Dr. Kwangwa is a changemaker shaping the future of information professionals and advancing digital inclusion in Zimbabwe through her leadership, research and community engagement.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Seán Lacey
speakers.bio:
Dr Seán Lacey received his BSc in Mathematical Sciences and Computing from the University of Limerick in 2003, followed by a PhD in Applied Mathematics in 2007. He has lectured at numerous Higher Education Institutions in Ireland. In March 2011, he joined the Department of Mathematics at Cork Institute of Technology, where he served as Chair of the Research & Innovation Committee (2016–2020) and Chair of the Department of Mathematics Research Committee (2016–2021). He was also a member of the National Forum for the Enhancement of Teaching and Learning in Higher Education Research Ethics Committee (2019–2021) and the National Research Ethics Committee (2023–2025). In 2022, he completed an MBA in Educational Leadership (International) at the Institute of Education, University College London.

In June 2021, Seán was promoted to Senior Lecturer at Munster Technological University (MTU, formerly Cork Institute of Technology). In October that year, he was elected the inaugural Chair of MTU’s Research Council. In January 2022, he was appointed as the University’s Research Integrity & Compliance Officer (RICO). In this role, Seán is responsible for fostering a research environment that upholds the highest standards of integrity, leading the development, implementation, and review of research-related policies and procedures. Working with relevant offices and postholders, he ensures that the University’s research training emphasises the importance of research integrity, promotes responsible conduct of research, discourages research misconduct, and raises awareness of the consequences of same.

Seán has played a pivotal role in shaping MTU’s research governance. He oversaw the creation of its Human Research Ethics Committee in 2022, and the Animal Research and Education Ethics Committee in 2025. He also served as co-lead for the European Commission-supported Network for Education and Research Quality, with a focus on Research Supervision and Leadership. 

With over 50 peer-reviewed publications in partnership with local, national, and international collaborators, Seán is an active researcher and mentor. He is currently supervising two PhD students and has successfully guided six MSc students to completion. His expertise in statistical analysis has made him the lead statistician on more than 40 industry collaboration studies, and he has designed and delivered over 70 Continuing Professional Development (CPD) workshops for industry professionals.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Heidi Lamb
speakers.bio:
Heidi Lamb (PhD, Peace and Conflict Studies; MA, Peace and Conflict Studies, 2020, University of Waterloo; BA[H], Drama and Communications, Media and Film, 2014, University of Windsor). Heidi is a PhD candidate in Peace and Conflict Studies at the University of Manitoba. Her SSHRC-funded research examines how post-secondary students experience digital misogyny and gender-based sexual violence on Canadian campuses, exploring how online narratives intersect with campus culture to shape attitudes and responses.
In addition, Heidi is a graduate student researcher on the We Interrupt This Programming project (Dr. Shannon Moore & Dr. Jennifer Watt, University of Manitoba), which investigates how K–12 educators perceive and respond to GBSV and consent, and how media can be used pedagogically to challenge rape culture. Beyond academia, she is actively involved in her local community through the Tavistock Agricultural Society and she enjoys spending time with her two shiba inus, Chance and Lucy.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Marina Lambert
speakers.bio: Dr. Marina Lambert is a postdoctoral researcher at the Danish Centre for Studies in Research and Research Policy at Aarhus University, Denmark. Her current work on the ETHOS project - Exploring Research Integrity Policies and Practices in “the Houses of Science” – focuses on the analysis of academic and policy discourses on research integrity. Dr. Lambert’s work on the RE4GREEN project - Research Ethics and Integrity for the Green Transition – aims to contribute to establishing an integrity and ethics framework for research and innovation on green energy transition, within the scope of the European Green Deal agenda. She holds a PhD in political science from the University of Copenhagen.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Evan Laye
speakers.bio: Evan Laye was born in Winnipeg, MB and raised by his parents, Sheldon and Elaine, in his home community of Selkirk, MB.  Evan is Red River Michif and has relations across Manitoba, with most of his family located in the Interlake and Northern regions of the province.  He has lived in various locations across Canada, including Saskatchewan, Ontario, British Columbia, and remote northern Manitoba.  Evan’s childhood experiences on the land inspired an interest in the natural world, which shaped his academic and professional careers.  Evan has received training in environmental science from Western academic institutions, completing a Bachelor of Environmental Science at the University of Manitoba (2015) and a Masters in Environmental Science at the University of British Columbia (2024), alongside Indigenous teachings from Elders, Knowledge Keepers, and cultural leaders with whom he has built relationships with over time.  As the NRC’s first Indigenous Scholar in Residence, Evan will continue his research training while completing a PhD in Indigenous Knowledge at the University of British Columbia.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Cédric Lemarchand
speakers.organization: Univ Rennes, Inserm, EHESP, Irset - UMR_S 1085
speakers.bio: Cédric Lemarchand is a PhD student in public health at the Université de Rennes and the IRSET (Research Institute for Environmental and Occupational Health), currently focusing on nepotistic practices among editors. His previous work includes research on the resurgence of hallucinogens in psychiatry and the French system for early access approvals, with an emphasis on research integrity.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Michael Lentze
speakers.position: Head Of The Department: Mathematics, Systems Engineering, Computer Science
speakers.bio:
Michael Lentze (born in 1974) studied physics at the Universities of Würzburg and Grenoble and received his PhD in experimental solid-state physics. Since 2007, he has been working at the German Research Foundation (DFG), where he initially oversaw the fields of electrical and systems engineering before taking responsibility for fluid mechanics from 2008 to 2019. From 2020 to 2023, he headed the team for process engineering, thermodynamics, and fluid mechanics. Since 2024, he has been serving as Head of the Mathematics and Computer Science Group.
His areas of special responsibility include artificial intelligence in research and administration, research security and dual use, and technology transfer.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Roxana Lescano
speakers.bio: Ms. Lescano has worked at the U.S. Naval Medical Research Unit SOUTH (NAMRU SOUTH) for over 35 years and since 2005, as the Head of the Research Administration Program. Under this position, she has founded and later coordinated the Scientific Review Board, the Research Ethics Committee, and the Human Subjects Protection Training Program. More recently, her responsibilities have increased to include the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC), among other functions. Ms. Lescano is an attorney and holds a Masters in Bioethics, in addition to having completed the course work for the Masters in Epidemiological Research.  Her experience extends from IRB administration to ethical oversight of IRB approved studies, in addition to supporting NAMRU SOUTH and other Peruvian organizations to develop their institutional policies and procedures, and their education programs in the field of human subject protections, scientific integrity and animal welfare in research. Ms. Lescano has also focused on research integrity education programs for over 10 years, preparing modules for the original Peruvian course on Responsible Conduct of Research, coordinating curricula for local courses, as well as workshops for undergrad and post-graduate programs, and participating as a guest speaker in academic and research organizations in Peru and neighboring countries. Additionally, Ms. Lescano has led and supported several research projects in this field. From 2019 to date, she has been a member of the organizing committee of the World Conferences of Research Integrity conducted in Hong Kong in 2019, Cape Town, South Africa in 2022, and in Athens, Greece, this coming June 2024.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Lisa Leventhal
speakers.bio: Lisa Leventhal is the Research Integrity Officer and Director of the Program for Responsible Research Practices at Oregon State University. Lisa also serves on the Board of Directors for the Association of Research Integrity Officers.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Clayton Lewis
speakers.bio: Clayton Lewis is the Manager of Outreach & Education in the Research Security Division at the University of Calgary. Previously he worked as a social policy researcher in municipal government. Clayton holds a PhD from York University where he conducted research on challenges to international liberal-democratic institutions as well as prospects and pathways for democratic renewal.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Tom Lindemann
speakers.bio: Tom Lindemann is Secretary-General of the Luxembourg Agency for Research Integrity (LARI). He is LARI’s chief executive and has overall responsibility for all support services LARI offers to its member organizations. Moreover, he represents LARI in national, European, and international networks and acts as an advisor to the National Commission for Research Integrity, an independent committee which investigates cases of alleged research misconduct at LARI member organizations. Before he joined LARI in 2023, he worked as a Research Project Manager and Co-Team Leader for the European Network of Research Ethics Committees (EUREC). In that position, he was involved in as work package and task leader in several EU-funded projects on research ethics, research integrity, and responsible research and innovation.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Shiwei Liu
speakers.bio: I am a PhD student in Information Sciences at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (ScienceNLP Lab), where I develop natural language processing methods to extract, evaluate, and organize knowledge from scientific literature. My current work builds automated tools for assessments of randomized controlled trial (RCT) reporting with respect to CONSORT and SPIRIT guidelines, to help authors, editors, and reviewers identify omissions before publication and to strengthen the evidence base used in systematic reviews. More broadly, I study how AI can advance research integrity by improving transparency, reproducibility, and auditability of biomedical reporting.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Leo Lo
speakers.bio: Leo S. Lo is University Librarian and Dean of Libraries, Advisor to the Provost for AI Literacy, and professor of education at the University of Virginia. He is internationally recognized for advancing AI literacy across higher education and workforce development, consulting and publishing widely on AI literacy, workforce development, and higher education innovation. As President (2024–2025) of the Association of College and Research Libraries, he established the ACRL AI Competencies Task Force and leads national efforts to develop AI competencies for library professionals. His initiatives include creating national AI literacy frameworks and leading large scale studies on how academic libraries adapt to AI. Lo holds a Doctor of Education from the University of Pennsylvania, an MS in Information Studies from Florida State University, and a Postgraduate Diploma in AI for Business from the University of Oxford. Guided by a human centered vision of technology, he challenges institutions to reimagine learning as an act of curiosity, creativity, and courage in the AI era.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Malcolm Macleod
speakers.position: Professor Of Neurology And Translational Neurosciences
speakers.bio: Prof Malcolm Macleod co-founded the CAMARADES collaboration in 2005 and is now at the University of Edinburgh as Professor of Neurology and Translational Neuroscience and co-Director of Edinbrugh Neuroscience. With Saran, he was co-Chair or the ESIC Working Group on Safe and Responsible Use of AI in Evidence Synthesis.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Fahad Mahmood
speakers.bio: I am associated with Department of Digital media, School of Communication Studies, University of the Punjab, Lahore, Pakistan as Assistant Professor. I was a resident scholar at Center for the Study of Religion and Conflict and Walter Cronkite School of Journalism and Mass Communication, Arizona State University, USA in 2017. I have a background of working in print and electronic media, and the development sector. I have published my research and have presented it at many forums including IAMCR. I am founding general secretary of the Association of Media and Communication Academic Professional (AMCAP) established in 2017 in Pakistan that represents the pioneer media association for the academics in the country.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: David Malwitz
speakers.bio: David Malwitz is the Global Non-Regulated External Audit Program Lead at Johnson & Johnson, where he drives quality initiatives supporting global preclinical research. With a B.S. in Chemistry from Cedarville University and an M.S. in Organic Chemistry from Wright State University, David began his career as a medicinal chemist at Wyeth and Amgen, contributing to drug discovery in neuroscience, immunology, and oncology. At J&J, he joined a pioneering team that developed a novel quality system for non-regulated environments. He later led the creation of an external audit program, resulting in over 150 audits of external partners, including industry partners, contract labs, and academic institutions. David continues to lead and support cross-functional initiatives to enhance data quality and reproducibility in early research. Outside of work, he enjoys cycling, backpacking, woodworking, and spending time with his family in North County San Diego.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Emmanuelle Marceau
speakers.bio: Emmanuelle Marceau, specialist in ethics and law, is adjunct professor in the Bioethics Program of the Department of Social and Preventive Medicine at the School of Public Health of the Université de Montréal (ESPUM). Particularly interested in modes of governance and their interactions from a justice-seeking perspective, her work explores the ethics of artificial intelligence, responsible conduct in research, professional ethics and public health. Alongside her graduate teaching in bioethics, she teaches philosophy at the Cégep du Vieux Montréal and is a lecturer at the Université de Sherbrooke. Co-director of the Ethics, Governance and Democracy branch at the International Observatory on the Societal Impacts of AI and Digital Technologies (Obvia), she is a member of the Centre de recherche en droit public (CRDP) and the Institut d'éthique appliquée (IDÉA), and completed a residence at the Institut des études et de la recherche sur le droit et la justice (IERDJ).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Marcel Martončik
speakers.organization: Institute of Social Sciences CSPS SAS, Slovakia
speakers.bio: Marcel Martončik (PhD) is interested in how to increase the credibility and replicability of behavioral research and seeks to promote these approaches as co-founder of the Slovak Reproducibility Network. His main research focus is gaming disorder, which he investigates as part of the ERC ORE longitudinal project aimed at building a multicultural understanding of how people play videogames at different intensity levels. He runs an initiative aimed at improving access to support for people in Slovakia with gaming disorder. His work also covers esports and human performance, particularly examining which psychological factors are important for success in different esports games.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Diane Martz
speakers.position: Senior Advisor Responsible Conduct Of Research

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Ana Marušić
speakers.organization: University Of Split School Of Medicine
speakers.bio: Ana Marušić is Professor of Anatomy, Chair of the Department of Research in Biomedicine and Health, and Head of the Center for Evidence-based Medicine at the University of Split School of Medicine. Her research interests include evidence-based medicine and research integrity. She has published over 400 articles in peer-reviewed journals and was heavily involved with creating the policy of mandatory registration of clinical trials in public registries which helped change the legal regulation of clinical trials worldwide. Prof. Marušić is the founder of Cochrane Croatia and also serves on the Advisory Board the EQUATOR Network. She was the editor in chief of the Croatian Medical Journal and the Journal of Global Health. She is currently the Senior Editor-in-Chief of ST-OPEN, an overlay journal of the University of Split. She serves on the Council of the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE), and as the president of The Embassy Foundation. She is the founding member of the Croatian Reproducibility and Integrity Network, CroRIN.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Limbanazo Matandika
speakers.bio: Limbanazo Mindiera Matandika, PhD, is a newly elected Advisory Member of the African Research Integrity Network. She has served as an Interim Steering Committee member since 2015 and co-founded Africa’s first network dedicated to promoting responsible research conduct in Africa. A bioethicist and social scientist by training, Dr. Matandika obtained her PhD in bioethics (2023) from the University of Malawi, College of Medicine. She holds a Master of Social Science degree in Health Research Ethics (2017) from the University of KwaZulu-Natal in South Africa and a master’s in public health degree (2014) from the University of Malawi, College of Medicine. Dr. Matandika currently works as a Research Ethics Committee Administrator at the Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences. She is active in bioethics and research integrity leadership both in Malawi and globally, having developed an online module on Research Integrity and currently serving as a Research Integrity Ambassador for Malawi. Currently she’s serving as a guest editor with the Journal of Clinical Epidemiology under the methodological aspects of research integrity culture series. This series aims to cover a broad range of methodological issues of relevance to research integrity, scientific misconduct, and questionable research practices.She has received various travel grants to present as a keynote speaker and panellist at international conferences, reflecting on research ethics and integrity from a low- and middle-income setting. Her research interests include conflict of interest, authorship, decolonization of global health, embedded ethics, and clinical ethics.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Matt McCoy
speakers.bio: Matt McCoy is a Director at Proofig AI, a platform using artificial intelligence (AI) to detect image manipulation and strengthen research integrity. He works with universities, research institutes, and publishers to integrate AI enabled screening into institutional workflows, focusing on behavioural, cultural, and systems factors that support ethical researcher practices. With over two decades of experience across education, research, and technology, Matt’s current work examines how AI can move beyond a compliance utility to build a proactive and sustainable integrity culture.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Lorrilee McGregor
speakers.bio: Dr. Lorrilee McGregor is an Anishinaabe from Whitefish River First Nation. She is an Associate Professor at the Northern Ontario School of Medicine where she teaches about Indigenous peoples’ health. For the past 30 years, she has been working with First Nation communities and Indigenous organizations in northern Ontario on research and evaluation projects.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Shawn McGuirk
speakers.organization: Natural Sciences And Engineering Research Council Of Canada (NSERC)
speakers.bio: Shawn McGuirk is Director for Research Security at the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC), where his team coordinates the harmonized implementation of research security measures at NSERC and across the Canadian federal granting agencies, working closely with Canada’s Research Security Centre and other federal partners. Shawn also represents NSERC as co-chair of the Global Research Council’s Responsible Research Assessment Working Group. He joined NSERC in 2018 as a Canadian Science Policy Fellow and is an alumnus of the Government of Canada’s Recruitment of Policy Leaders program. Previously, he conducted research at the Goodman Cancer Research Institute of McGill University centered on the role of cellular metabolism in cancer progression.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Suzanne McMurphy
speakers.bio: Suzanne McMurphy has been at the University of Windsor since 2009 and is currently an Associate Professor in the Department of Sociology and Criminology. Prior to coming to the University of Windsor, she was a member of the research faculty in the Department of Criminology and Senior Research Investigator in the School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania. Her doctoral research was funded by a Fulbright Scholarship to Sweden where she studied the integration of law and social work in child welfare and criminal justice systems across Europe and North America. In addition to her faculty position, she has also served as the Chair of the Research Ethics Board at the University of Windsor and has served on research ethics committees for over 25 years in Canada and the US, including over four years as a committee member of the Social and Behavioral Institutional Review Board at the University of Pennsylvania. She has an undergraduate degree in Religious Studies, a Master’s degree in Social Service, Master’s degree in Law and Social Policy, Master’s degree in Bioethics, and a Ph.D. in Social Policy.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Gráinne McNamara
speakers.bio: Gráinne leads the Research Integrity / Publication Ethics team at Karger Publishers, responsible for developing research integrity policies and conducting investigations for all Karger publications. She completed her PhD in Integrative Neuroscience at Cardiff University in the UK and then spent several years as a researcher at Cardiff University and Imperial College London. Since 2017 she has been working in research integrity teams at publishers and she established the research integrity / publication ethics team at Karger Publishers in 2021. In this role, she is particularly interested in research integrity promoting activities that enhance transparency and the reproducibility of research.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Thando Mdaka
speakers.bio:
Thando currently serves as a Research Integrity Coordinator: Investigations at the University of Cape Town (UCT), a position she has held since October 2024. In this capacity, she oversees research integrity investigations and contributes to the development, implementation, and enhancement of institutional policies and procedures that promote responsible research conduct.
Prior to joining UCT, Thando was a Research Integrity Officer in the Research Ethics and Integrity Unit at the University of South Africa (UNISA) (April 2021 – September 2024). Her work focused on strengthening institutional compliance through the design and delivery of research ethics and integrity training programmes and fostering a culture of ethical research practice across the institution.
Thando began her career in research integrity as an Intern at the National Research Foundation (NRF), where she gained foundational experience in research governance and integrity processes.
She holds a Postgraduate Degree in Operations Management, and her expertise in research integrity has been recognised internationally through poster presentations at the World Conference on Research Integrity (WCRI) and the International Network of Research Management Societies (INORMS).
Thando is dedicated to advancing research integrity frameworks, strengthening institutional capacity, and promoting ethical research practices in higher education.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: AKM Moinul Haque Meaze
speakers.bio:
AKM Moinul Haque Meaze is a Professor of Physics at the Asian University for Women (AUW), Bangladesh. He earned first class first positions in both his BSc (Honors) and MSc (Thesis) in Physics from the University of Chittagong (CU). He received his PhD in Physics from Kyungpook National University (KNU), Korea, and later completed a two-year postdoctoral fellowship at INFN, Bari, Italy, where he worked on the n_TOF experiment at CERN, Geneva. 
Professor Meaze is a permanent faculty member in the Department of Physics at CU. He is currently on lien leave from CU and serves as a full-time faculty member at AUW. His research interests include Nuclear and Medical Physics, as well as multidisciplinary areas such as STEM higher education and science policy. 
He was a regular associate of the ICTP, Trieste, for seven years. Professor Meaze is a Fellow of the Bangladesh Physical Society and the author of four peer-reviewed books and more than 50 journal articles.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Signe Mežinska
speakers.bio: Signe Mežinska is a bioethicist and sociologist with an MSc in Bioethics (Union Graduate College, US) and PhD in Sociology of Health and Illness (Riga Stradins University, Latvia). She teaches at the University of Latvia, Faculty of Medicine and Life Sciences, with over 25 years of experience in medical ethics and sociology of health and illness. Her research focuses on ethics in biobanking, psychiatry, reproductive health, and rational use of medicines. She has led research ethics training courses internationally, serves for several research ethics committees and  international bodies, including UNESCO’s International Bioethics Committee (Vice-Chair 2017–2021), Council of Europe’s CDBIO, and WHO’s COVID-19 Ethics and Governance group. She currently leads work packages in Horizon Europe projects BEYOND and FAMILY and has contributed to VIRT2UE, ROSiE, B1MG, EDITH projects and several COST actions.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Katharina Miller
speakers.bio:
Katharina Miller is founding partner of the boutique consultancy firm 3C Compliance and the multidisciplinary law firm Miller International Knowledge.She is a qualified lawyer in Germany and Spain with over 15 years of international practice across Western Europe. She has strong expertise in German and Spanish Corporate Governance and in the interface between Compliance & Ethics and business. Also beyond her professional activities, she is regularly consulted in ESG, CSR, and Corporate Compliance matters, for example on EU level.

Furthermore, she is a committed Non-Executive Member of various advisory and supervisory boards across industries and countries. Here, she contributes especially with legal, operational and risk management experience and in the fields ESG, Women Rights and Innovation & Technology worldwide.

Her professional activities are strongly related to the implementation of the Agenda 2030, because for her it's of utmost importance to find solutions for our living together in the "The Fourth Industrial Revolution" by implementing the 17 Sustainable Development Goals of the United Nations (SGDs).

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Adya Misra
speakers.bio: Adya Misra is Associate Director, Research Integrity at Sage and maintains responsibility over publishing ethics activity in Sage’s 1500+ journals portfolio. Adya leads the Global Research Integrity Team at Sage responsible for the resolution of all publication ethics and research integrity issues raised in Sage journals. Through this work, Adya is responsible for the development of policies and cross-functional processes to support the effective resolution of publication ethics issues, with overarching aims to prevent publication of problematic content. Adya has previously worked in senior editorial roles at PLOS and PeerJ bringing together subject level knowledge in molecular biology, medicine and public health along with research integrity expertise. As a journal editor and researcher in previous roles, Adya has broad interests in science communication, peer review and understanding the drivers of change in the research landscape. Adya has a PhD in Mitochondrial genetics and completed postdoctoral training at Karolinska Institute, Stockholm and Institute for Cancer Research, London. Adya was awarded the People of Publishing award in 2024 to recognise her contributions to scholarly publishing.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Langelihle Mlotshwa
speakers.bio: Dr. Langelihle Mlotshwa is a public health researcher specializing in research on vulnerable and marginalized populations. Her work examines the ethical challenges of conducting health research in low-resource and emergency contexts, including issues of informed consent, community engagement, and equitable partnerships. She has contributed to studies on strengthening ethical preparedness during public health crises and ensuring research practices uphold integrity and social justice. Dr. Mlotshwa is committed to bridging the gap between ethical principles and practical research, promoting approaches that protect participants’ rights, build trust, and enhance the impact of health research in communities often overlooked by traditional systems. Through her work, she seeks to advance responsible research that is both ethically rigorous and socially responsive, fostering collaborations that benefit those most affected by health inequities.

speakers.title: Prof
speakers.name: David Moher
speakers.bio: Dr. David Moher is a clinical epidemiologist, and professor of Epidemiology and Public Health, University of Ottawa. Professor Moher has been involved with the World Conference of Research Integrity for many years. He led an international team resulting in the “The Hong Kong Principles for assessing researchers: Fostering research integrity”, an output of the WCRI meeting in Hong Kong. Professor Moher spends his time trying help to improve publication science as director of the Centre for Journalology.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Amanda Montoya
speakers.bio: I am an Associate Professor at UCLA in the Department of Psychology - Quantitative Area. I received my PhD in Quantitative Psychology from the Ohio State University in 2018. My primary adviser was Dr. Andrew Hayes. I completed my M.A. in Psychology and my M.S. in Statistics at Ohio State in 2016. I graduated from the University of Washington with a B.S. in Psychology and a minor in Mathematics in 2013. My research interests include mediation, moderation, conditional process models, structural equation modeling, and meta-science.

speakers.name: Ry Moran
speakers.bio: Ry Moran is the inaugural Associate University Librarian – Reconciliation at the University of Victoria.   He is currently on secondment to the Royal British Columbia Museum as Interim CEO.  Prior to his appointment at the University of Victoria,  he was the founding Director of the National Centre for Truth and Reconciliation and served directly with the Truth and Reconciliation Commission as the Director of Statement Gathering.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Daniel Moreira
speakers.bio: Dr Daniel Moreira obtained his PhD in computer science from the University of Campinas, Brazil, and is an Assistant Professor at Loyola University in Chicago. His team applies machine learning and media forensics to maximise the application of AI for societal benefit, including the detection of AI misuse in research publications.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Philippe Emmanuel Mounmbegna Pouonkoussou
speakers.bio:
Philippe Mounmbegna holds a BSc in Biochemistry from the University of Yaoundé I, Cameroon, and MSc and PhD in Medical Biochemistry from the University of Benin, Nigeria. He joined Madonna University, Nigeria, in 2002, becoming a Professor of Biochemistry in 2018. His research focuses on Toxicology, Phytochemistry, and Phytomedicine, with over 80 published articles and numerous book chapters. He has presented over thirty papers at conferences globally.
He has gained international experience through fellowships and visits to laboratories in Cameroon, the UK, Ghana, Kenya, and Rwanda, and holds certifications in academic leadership and quality assurance. He has secured research grants and served as an external examiner for various universities. An active member of multiple professional societies, he has held leadership roles, including Deputy Vice Chancellor, Dean of the Postgraduate School at Madonna University. He is now a faculty member in the Department of Biochemistry at Kampala International University, Uganda

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Beatriz Antonieta Moya Figueroa
speakers.bio: Beatriz Moya Figueroa es doctora (Ph.D.) en Investigación con especialización en Liderazgo Político y Gobernanza por la University of Calgary (Canadá). Posee un magíster en Enseñanza del Inglés como Lengua Extranjera y es licenciada en Educación de la Universidad Católica de Temuco.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Martin Nganga Muigano
speakers.bio: Dr. Martin Ng'ang'a Muigano is a Kenyan biotechnologist and educator. He obtained his PhD in biotechnology from Jomo Kenyatta University in Kenya in 2025. He has taught at Kenyan universities and held research positions in various institutions. Currently, he is a lead scientist at Bio One Scientific Ltd in Kenya. His vision is to promote innovation in Africa through education, research, and collaborative partnerships.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Allen Mukhwana
speakers.position: Head Of Programmes, Strengthening Science Ecosystems
speakers.bio:
Allen Mukhwana is a Senior Research Management Professional and Head of Programmes for Strengthening Science Ecosystems at the Science for Africa Foundation. She leads a pan-African portfolio focused on advancing research governance, institutional capacity, and evidence-informed policy. Her work spans science policy, global partnerships, and systems-level capacity building across African research institutions.
With over 20 years of experience, Allen has driven initiatives in research integrity, science communication, and institutional reform. She is a leading advocate for Open Research and decolonised research security, promoting indigenous data sovereignty, equitable global partnerships, and ethical governance rooted in African epistemologies.
Allen’s participation in WCRI 2026 reflects her commitment to amplifying African perspectives in global research integrity discourse. She champions inclusive, trust-based collaborations that move beyond deficit narratives and strengthen responsible research ecosystems across the continent.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Felicite Naah Tabala
speakers.organization: Division of Health Operational research Ministry Of Public Health
speakers.bio: Naah Tabala Felicite is a public health professional and social scientist with over 12 years of experience in health research, ethics, and community engagement in Cameroon. She currently serves as Research Officer at the Ministry of Public Health’s Division of Health Operations Research, where she supports research protocol compliance, capacity building of Research Ethics Committees, post-approval monitoring, and coordination of national surveys. She has led or contributed to major projects including CAMPHIA (2015, 2024), IBBS 2023, PrEP pilot evaluations with USAID/PEPFAR, and COVID-19 research in Yaoundé. Felicite combines training in anthropology, psychology, public health, and health research ethics, and is completing a Master’s in Health Research Ethics at the University of KwaZulu-Natal. Her expertise spans ethics governance, compliance monitoring, stakeholder engagement, and community-based research. She has presented at national and international forums, published on HIV research and ethics, and facilitated workshops on informed consent and SOP harmonization.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Kasphia Nahrin
speakers.bio: I am a professor of urban and regional planning, possessing 20 years of research and teaching experience in the fields of housing, urban planning, environmental management, gender studies, and development in Bangladesh and the Global South. I have authored over 20 peer-reviewed publications and have been the recipient of numerous international fellowships and grants. I have gained experience in leading funded research projects, supervising postgraduate research, and actively participating in global policy debates on inclusive and sustainable urban development. I have completed my PhD in the UK and a TWAS–IsBD postdoctoral fellowship in Malaysia.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Masaki Nakamura
speakers.bio: Dr. Masaki Nakamura is a Professor at the University of Osaka, Japan, and a member of the Science Council of Japan. He received his PhD from the University of Tokyo in 2005. After serving as a research associate at the University of Tokyo’s Research Center for Advanced Science and Technology and as a research fellow at the National Institute of Science and Technology Policy (MEXT), he joined the University of Osaka in 2007 and was promoted to professor in 2021. He also serves on the board of the Association for the Promotion of Research Integrity (APRIN), Japan’s core national organization promoting research integrity. His research covers a wide range of topics, including research integrity, science communication, and the history of science and technology.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Daisy Namaganda
speakers.bio: Daisy Namaganda is a researcher and bioethicist with a Master’s degree in Health Sciences (Bioethics) from Makerere University in Uganda. Her work focuses on research integrity, responsible conduct of research, and the ethical challenges faced by students and early-career researchers. Her recent study on plagiarism among graduate students at Makerere University explored how access to online resources and new technologies shape attitudes toward academic honesty. Before moving into bioethics, Daisy worked on HIV-related studies and developed a strong interest in how ethical principles guide health and social science research. She is passionate about helping young researchers understand integrity not just as a rule, but as a value that strengthens trust in science. Currently, she is expanding her research interests into mental health ethics and hopes to contribute to improving ethical research practices in universities and health institutions across Africa.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Florence Navidad
speakers.bio: She is a Professor at the Department of Medical Technology at the Faculty of Pharmacy and Graduate School and a Resident Researcher at the Research Center for Social Sciences and Education (RCSSED) of the University of Santo Tomas (UST). She holds the position of UST Institutional Research Ethics Board Chair. She is also the Research Management Committee chair and co-chairman of the Research Ethics Committee of the Faculty of Pharmacy. She is a medical technologist and nurse by profession and teaches medical technology courses, including research, biostatistics, epidemiology, health information systems, and research ethics. In addition to teaching, she also serves as a research advisor and statistics consultant for students and fellow professors. Her research interests include social and health sciences, health education, public health, epidemiology, medical and nursing, and natural sciences. She is also involved in educational management, organizational dynamics, cooperative and union dynamics, and human resource development.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Mona Nemer
speakers.bio: Dr. Mona Nemer was first appointed Canada’s Chief Science Advisor in 2017 and reappointed for a fourth term in 2024. Prior to this role, Dr. Nemer was Professor and Vice-President of Research at the University of Ottawa and Director of the school’s Molecular Genetics and Cardiac Regeneration Laboratory.  Dr. Nemer has served on various national and international advisory committees and boards, including as an Executive Committee Member of the COVID-19 Immunity Task Force as well as an ex-officio member of the COVID-19 Vaccine Task Force and the Industry Strategy Council. During her mandate, she has promoted free, open, and responsible use of science in government through the creation of a model policy on scientific integrity and a roadmap for open science to guide efforts in making federal research available to Canadians. She has provided science advice on numerous issues ranging from research infrastructure to the COVID-19 pandemic. Dr. Nemer holds a PhD in Chemistry from McGill University and has done post-doctoral training in molecular biology at the Montréal Clinical Research Institute and Columbia University. Prior to joining the University of Ottawa, she was a Professor of Pharmacology at the Université de Montréal and directed the Cardiac Genetics Unit at the Montréal Clinical Research Institute.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Jeremy Y Ng
speakers.bio: Born and raised in Canada, Dr. Jeremy Y. Ng, MSc, PhD, is a Scientist at the Institute of General Practice and Interprofessional Care, University Hospital Tübingen & Robert Bosch Center for Integrative Medicine and Health in Stuttgart, Germany, as well as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Health Research Methods, Evidence, and Impact, Faculty of Health Sciences, McMaster University in Hamilton, Canada, and an Associate Professor (Adjunct) at the School of Public Health, Faculty of Health, University of Technology Sydney in Sydney, Australia. Dr. Ng is an experienced health research methodologist, having authored >100 peer-reviewed publications, who has an interest in evaluating and improving research practices applicable across all areas of medicine. Dr. Ng’s research interests include: open science; meta-research; publication science; research ethics, integrity, and misconduct; bibliometrics; artificial intelligence; and traditional, complementary, and integrative medicine. Dr. Ng was previously a MITACS-funded Postdoctoral Fellow at the Centre for Journalology at the Ottawa Hospital Research Institute in Ottawa, Canada. Dr. Ng earned a Doctor of Philosophy in Health Research Methodology from McMaster University, and a Master of Pharmaceutical Sciences (MSc) from the University of Toronto. Dr. Ng's research has been featured in Nature, Scientific American, Discover Magazine, Psychology Today, and PsychCentral, among other prominent media outlets.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Ayodeji Michael Obadire
speakers.bio:
Obadire is a dynamic scholar and research leader with keen interests in sustainability, research integrity and ethics, research management, and ESG. With more than a decade of experience in academia and management consulting, he combines strategic leadership with people-focused problem-solving skills. He currently manages his institution’s research and innovation portfolio, coordinating ideation processes and translating them into impactful projects and innovations. In this role, he fosters a culture of research excellence, advances knowledge creation, and provides strategic guidance for the development and implementation of institutional research policies and strategies.
A Chartered Accountant and Chartered Management Accountant, Obadire holds a PhD in Finance and Accounting from the University of Venda, South Africa. He has complemented this with a Certificate in Statistics from Stanford University (USA) and a Certificate in Research Ethics from North-West University (SA). His scholarly contributions include over 30 publications in peer reviewed journals, books and conference proceedings.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Sonja Ochsenfeld-Repp
speakers.bio: Dr. Sonja Ochsenfeld-Repp is Head of Division “Research Culture”, German Research Foundation (Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft | DFG). Before that, she served as Deputy Head of Division “Quality and Programme Management”. Her focus is on the promotion of equity, diversity, and inclusion, sustainability in research processes, research assessment and safeguarding research integrity. From 2012 to 2015 she was Deputy Director of the European Liaison Office of the German Research Organisations (Kowi) and Head of the Bonn office. She was co-coordinator of the National Contact Point for the European Research Council (ERC). Sonja is a member of the Science Europe (SE) Working Groups on Research Culture and Artificial Intelligence; she is engaged in the Coalition on Advancing Research Assessment (CoARA). Sonja is a fully qualified lawyer by training and has been awarded a doctoral degree from the University of Bonn. She has participated as a speaker or chair in many national, European and international conferences.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Brenda Odero
speakers.bio: Brenda Adhiambo Odero is an interdisciplinary researcher focused on applied ethics, AI governance, and health policy, especially in medical AI. Her work investigates how governance models can effectively balance AI use to promote safety, fairness, and accountability in local settings. With a background in health research ethics and increasing interest in AI ethics within healthcare, she combines research and practical experience in ethics and regulation. Her goal is to help develop trustworthy, human-centred AI systems.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Anthony Oko-Isu
speakers.bio:
Dr. Oko-Isu holds a Ph.D. in Agribusiness and Entrepreneurship Development, and he is currently pursuing an M.Sc. in Bioethics at the University of Ibadan. He is a distinguished scholar, policy advisor, and development consultant with over 13 years of experience at the intersection of agriculture, entrepreneurship, public policy, and data-driven development. He currently serves as Senior Lecturer and Head of the Department of Agribusiness and Management at Alex Ekwueme Federal University, Ndufu-Alike (AE-FUNAI), Ebonyi State, Nigeria.
He is a Postdoctoral Research Fellow of the Nigerian Institute of Social and Economic Research (NISER) and a globally recognized voice on food systems and data governance, having served as a member of the Technical Advisory Board of the Research Data Alliance (RDA-TAB) for Agriculture and Nutrition, representing Europe and Africa. Dr. Oko-Isu has participated in high-level research collaborations, trainings, and academic fellowships in Germany, Romania, India, and at Harvard Law School, USA.
He has made significant contributions to the domestication of the National Food and Nutrition Policy in Ebonyi State, led the development of the State's Costed Multi-Sectoral Plan of Action for Food and Nutrition, and facilitated capacity strengthening for the State Committee on Food and Nutrition. His teaching and policy advisory efforts are grounded in evidence-based approaches that emphasize the strategic use of quality data for transformative development.
Dr. Oko-Isu is a respected researcher and consultant with a rich portfolio of national and international projects supported by organizations such as UNICEF, USAID, and the European Union. His work spans agricultural value chain analysis, food and nutrition policy, and data-driven development planning.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Titilola Olojede
speakers.bio: H. Titilola Olojede is a Lecturer and the Head of the Department of Philosophy at the National Open University of Nigeria (NOUN). Titilola works at the intersection of ethics, African philosophy, and gender philosophy. Her current research focus is on the ethics of artificial intelligence. She is a recipient of several research and travel grants. She has published relevant papers, including, Influence of GenAI on Higher Education in Nigeria, Techno-solutionism a Fact or Farce? , and Towards Africa AI Ethical Principles. She is a member of committees on ethical integration of AI into higher education.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Edwin Onyango Were
speakers.bio: A highly proficient reproductive health practitioner with over 30 years of clinical, public health research and teaching experience in the context of a medical school. Have conducted HIV prevention clinical trials and public health evaluations employing rapid assessment, quantitative and qualitative methodologies for both local and international organizations. Have led the Institutional Research Ethics Committee for Moi University over the last 20 years, first as Secretary, and as Chairperson up to July 2025. Have developed and launched, with funding from NIH, Moi University Research Integrity Oversight Office to prevent and manage research misconduct. Also serving on the Medicins sans Frontiers Ethics Review Board since 2015. Currently involved in ongoing reproductive health research in the areas of Pharmacovigilance for Antiretroviral treatment (ART) in pregnancy and a pharmacokinetic and implementation science study on Long Acting ART and Long Acting Reversible Contraception among adolescent girls and young women in Kenya as co-Principal Investigator. Also lead Principal investigator in SAFE-T Implementation Research Project seeking to evaluate ways gender transformative approaches to shift community attitudes to child early and forced marriage among 10 -14 year old girls in Turkana County Kenya.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Victor Benjamin Oshodi
speakers.organization: Association For The Advancement Of Artificial Intelligence
speakers.bio:
Victor Oshodi is an accomplished policy and technology strategist, business consultant, and AI governance advocate with over a decade of experience advancing innovation, education, and international development across Africa. He serves as the Country Director of the Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI) – Nigeria, where he leads initiatives on AI safety, ethics, and governance, bridging research, policy, and community engagement.He has represented Nigeria at international conferences hosted by The Alan Turing Institute (UK), the University of Leeds, and other global research institutions, where he has presented research on AI governance, trustworthy digital infrastructure, and sustainable economic transformation.

Passionate about inclusive innovation, Victor is dedicated to shaping Africa’s contribution to global AI policy and governance, promoting ethical adoption of emerging technologies, and building collaborative frameworks that connect education, innovation, and economic growth.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Teresa Ottinger
speakers.bio: I am head of the grant application unit at the Swedish Research Council, with long experience in research grant funding and peer-review processes. I hold a special interest in research integrity issues and am part of the working group on research ethics and integrity at the Council.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Claudia Pallise Perello

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Irene Pasquetto
speakers.bio: I’m a scholar in the field of information and communication studies. I research contemporary knowledge production practices, with a focus on how digital technologies shape and mediate them. My most recent research work centers on knowledge production in the context of open science and public participation in science. I’m an Assistant Professor at the University of Maryland College of Information Studies. Previously, I was an Assistant Professor at the University of Michigan School of Information, where I taught ethics of information technologies and digital curation. I’m also a Senior Research Fellow at the Shorenstein Center on Media, Politics, and Public Policy, at the Harvard Kennedy School, where I co-founded the Harvard Kennedy School Misinformation Review in 2019.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Susan Passmore
speakers.bio: Susan Racine Passmore, PhD, is an Assistant Professor at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, School of Nursing. Her research focuses on promoting the equitable distribution of the benefits of health research. As a cultural anthropologist, Dr. Passmore’s research program focuses on the construction of researcher “trustworthiness” and the minimization of structural barriers to diversity. She co-directs the Just Research Program, an educational initiative that enables research teams to develop capacity in research equity. She has also explored innovation in the engagement of underrepresented communities in research and has received funding from the National Human Genome Research Institute, as well as supplement awards from the National Institute on Aging and the Centers for Disease Control. Through this work, Dr. Passmore aims to develop innovative and practical approaches to support the inclusion of communities facing health disparities in science as a fundamental component of achieving health equity.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Jay Patel
speakers.bio: As a Ph.D. candidate in Information Studies, I study communities and tools for evaluating research reports. I am inspired by problems in open science and metascience (the science of science) like scaling the critical evaluation of research and synthesizing research in a form capable of being directly relevant to laypeople and decision-makers. From Information Studies and HCI, I borrow user-centered thinking, design patterns, and technological solutions. Over the long term, I envision developing sociotechnical solutions across the research cycle that align our stated scholarly values with actual practices.

speakers.title: Miss
speakers.name: Dora Pejdo
speakers.organization: University Of Split School Of Medicine
speakers.bio: I am a researcher and PhD student at the University of Split School of Medicine in Croatia. I graduated my Master of Science in Psychology at a university in the UK and then returned to Croatia, where I joined the Horizon Europe project iRISE: Improving Reproducibility in Science. My work focuses on understanding and improving research reproducibility and integrity across different scientific fields. I am now in the third year of my PhD studies, exploring open science practices and how different stakeholders can work together to strengthen research quality. Alongside my research, I teach at the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences, Department of Psychology, at the University of Split. I am passionate about improving my own scientific practice, supporting responsible research, and helping to build a culture of transparency and trust in science.

speakers.name: Sammy Perez
speakers.bio:
Sammy Perez spent nearly a decade with Victoria Police, rising to Detective Acting Sergeant while managing complex, high risk investigations. Sammy’s commitment to protecting society's most vulnerable was recognised when her investigation into abuse in the disability sector earned her the Office of the Public Advocate’s Ben Bodna Award. The case was also featured on 60 Minutes Australia, highlighting the impact of her work.
Following her police career, Sammy transitioned to the University of Melbourne as Manager of Research Integrity Investigations, where she oversaw intricate investigations in the academic sector. Navigating the complexities of institutional policy and the Australian Code for the Responsible Conduct of Research, she applied her investigative expertise to cases involving research integrity.
Whether leading teams through emotionally charged criminal investigations or untangling complex research misconduct cases, Sammy has built her career on tackling cases that demand patience, precision, and a clear head.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Mike Perkins
speakers.bio: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mike Perkins serves as Associate Professor and Head of the Centre for Research & Innovation at British University Vietnam (BUV). With a PhD in Management from the University of York, he has emerged as a leading global voice on AI in assessment and the ethical integration of AI in education. Dr. Perkins is renowned for developing the AI Assessment Scale (AIAS), translated into 30 languages and implemented across more than 350 schools and universities worldwide. His work addresses the critical intersection of technology, academic integrity, and ethical implementation of AI in educational settings. He leads research in the equitable application of GenAI, and provides guidance to educators and policymakers responding to the challenges of the new GenAI landscape. Dr. Perkins' expertise has established him as a sought-after advisor to educational institutions globally, supporting them in ethically integrating Generative AI to enhance student learning while preserving academic integrity.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Lodewijk Pet
speakers.bio:
I am a PhD candidate in the Department of Clinical Epidemiology at Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC), the Netherlands. My research focuses on the Quality and Integrity of Biomedical Sciences.

Alongside my research, I teach epidemiological methods to students and PhD candidates, and I develop large language model (LLM) tools to support research practices.

Trained as a medical doctor with a passion for coding, I have built numerous websites and tools over the past decade using HTML, JavaScript, CSS, and Python for both professional and personal use.

I combine my expertise in medicine, coding, and epidemiology to enhance scientific quality throughout the entire research process from idea generation to post-publication evaluation. My goal is to enhance the trustworthiness and efficiency of science for all stakeholders.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Vasiliki Petousi
speakers.bio: Vasiliki Petousi is an Associate Professor of Sociology of Law and Deviance at the Department of Sociology, University of Crete, Greece.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nitya Phillipson
speakers.bio:
Dr Nitya Phillipson, 
Head of Integrity & Governance - Murdoch Children’s Research Institute, Melbourne Australia.
Nitya trained as a pharmacologist at King’s College London and the University of Melbourne before moving into research management. She brings extensive experience in research ethics and integrity, having led dedicated teams across major hospitals (including the Royal Children’s Hospital and Royal Melbourne Hospital), universities (Macquarie and Swinburne), and now at a national medical research institute. Nitya has overseen complex, high-profile investigations into serious research misconduct and is deeply committed to the human side of integrity. She is also passionate about advancing open science, AI ethics, and sustainable research practices.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Yaryna Pikulytska
speakers.bio:
A pediatrician and founder of the outpatient clinic "Vulyk," with a medical background
specializing in bioethics. Established the Institutional Review Board (IRB) at the Ukrainian
Catholic University and serves as a lecturer at its School of Bioethics. Passionate about
healthcare organization, pediatrics, family medicine, bioethics (particularly research ethics),
and evidence-based medicine and science.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Jason Pither
speakers.organization: University Of British Columbia, Okanagan Campus
speakers.bio: I am an ecologist with broad interests spanning community ecology, biogeography, and landscape ecology.  For almost a decade I have worked with faculty and staff at UBC to raise awareness about Open Science (OS) principles and practices, and have delivered OS seminars and instructional sessions to students, faculty, and staff.  I continue to advocate for integrating Open Science principles and practices into university policies.  More recently, I have collaborated with Indigenous communities in the region to help build capacity around Indigenous Data Sovereignty.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Merle-Marie Pittelkow
speakers.bio: Dr. Pittelkow (she/her) identifies as a meta-researcher with a background in clinical psychology. Her research interests include replication target selection, evidence-based decision (clinical) making, and transparent reporting.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Dena Plemmons
speakers.bio: Dr Dena Plemmons is the founding Director of the Research Ethics Education Program at the  University of California, Riverside.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Julia Priess Buchheit
speakers.bio:
Julia Priess-Buchheit (Prof. Dr. phil.) is an expert in learning open science, ethics, integrity, and social technologies. She leads the Zentrum für Konstruktive Erziehungswissenschaft and conducts her research at Kiel University. Before this transition, she was a professor of Education and Didactics at the University of Applied Sciences Coburg (Academic Centre for Sciences and Humanities), implementing an interdisciplinary study program for all faculties. In 2020, she became the Dean of Studies at the Academic Centre for Sciences and Humanities and founded the (German-speaking) Teaching and Learning Scientific Practice network. 
She coordinates IP4OS, a HorizonEurope project on Open Science and Intellectual Property (€ 2 million). She is one of the few coordinators of a Horizon2020 project (Path2Integrity, € 2.5 million) associated with a university of applied sciences in Germany (and Europe). 
The University of Kiel awarded her the Innovative and Trendsetting Teaching prize; she won the Genius Loci-Preis with the Coburger Weg team. Her digital learning settings for students and pupils are open source, and she won both the #WeForSchool Hackathon (Germany) and the #EUvsVirus Hackathon.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nandita Quaderi
speakers.bio:
Dr. Nandita Quaderi is a Senior Vice President and the Editor-in-Chief for Web of Science at Clarivate. Nandita has oversight of the Web of Science editorial division, the Institute for Scientific Information and the Research Professional News team. She has overall responsibility for editorial strategy, selection of Web of Science content and inclusion in Journal Citation Reports. Nandita’s focus is on protecting the integrity of the scholarly record, promoting the responsible use of bibliometrics and developing a responsible framework for evaluating the societal impact of research.

Nandita joined Clarivate as an accomplished senior publishing executive with a strong research background. Previously, she worked in leadership roles at BMC and Springer  Nature and was a Principal Investigator at the MRC Centre for Developmental Neurobiology, King’s College London. Nandita has a BA in Chemistry from the University of Oxford and a PhD in Molecular Genetics from Imperial College London.

Nandita is the incoming co-chair of United2Act, an international collaboration committed to addressing the challenge of paper mills in scholarly publishing, and currently sits on the Board of STM.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Lauran Qualkenbush
speakers.position: Senior Director, Research Integrity And Training
speakers.bio: Lauran Qualkenbush is Northwestern University’s Senior Director, Research Integrity and Training and Research Integrity Officer. She is responsible for and involved in Responsible and Ethical Conduct of Research education and coordination. She oversees Research Integrity, handling research misconduct and other research non-compliance, and the Research Training unit. She has been engaged in research integrity for over 20 years. Lauran previously worked as a clinical research coordinator and in Northwestern’s IRB Office. She is a founding member and current Vice President of the Association of Research Integrity Officers. She has been a teaching RIO for HHS ORI boot camps and served on ORI and NSF grant reviews.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Reese Richardson
speakers.bio: Reese Richardson is a postdoctoral research fellow in the Center for Science of Science and Innovation (CSSI) at the Kellogg School of Management, Northwestern University. Broadly, his research interests concern the formation of collective knowledge, maintaining the integrity of the scientific literature, and systematic studies of industrialized academic and scientific fraud. He earned his PhD in Interdisciplinary Biological Sciences at Northwestern University in 2024 in the laboratory of Luís A. Nunes Amaral. He is one of the maintainers of the Collection of Open Science Integrity Guides (COSIG, cosig.net), an open-source resource for performing post-publication peer review.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Christopher Rickerby
speakers.bio:
Christopher Rickerby

Data Integrity Analyst, EMBO Press

Christopher Rickerby is the Data Integrity Analyst for EMBO Press, responsible for ensuring image and data integrity across all journals, both pre- and post-publication. Since joining EMBO in 2018, he has developed and refined workflows to detect data manipulation, manage corrections, and strengthen transparency in scientific publishing.

A member of the STM Integrity Working Group, Chris collaborates internationally to advance best practices and evaluate emerging tools for research integrity, including AI-based systems designed to identify paper-milled and fraudulent submissions. His work supports the accuracy, reliability, and trustworthiness of EMBO Press publications.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Dominique Roche
speakers.organization: Social Sciences And Humanities Research Council Of Canada
speakers.bio: Dom Roche is a senior policy advisor at the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC), where he leads SSHRC’s policy work on research data management and sharing. Dom completed a PhD in aquatic ecology at the Australian National University and then undertook postdocs in Switzerland and Canada. In 2021, he co-founded the Society for Open, Reliable and Transparent Ecology and Evolutionary biology (SORTEE.org) and was the society’s president in 2022. Dom has a longstanding interest in metascience, research integrity, and open science practices. In 2020, he received a three-year Marie Slodowska-Curie Global Fellowship from the European Commission to study data sharing practices in environmental science. In his role at SSHRC, he chairs the Tri-Agency working group responsible for implementing the Tri-Agency Research Data Management (RDM) Policy and he leads the implementation of the Policy’s Data Deposit Requirement for SSHRC, NSERC and CIHR.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Jesus Rodríguez-Pomeda
speakers.bio:
Professor of Business Organization at Universidad Autonoma de Madrid (UAM).
He has served as Vice-rector for Strategy and Planning, UAM. He was also Consultant for the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
His production has been published in Journal of Cleaner Production, Higher Education Research & Development, Studies in Higher Education, Business Ethics-A European Review, Egitim Arastirmalari-Eurasian Journal of Educational Research, The Energy Journal, International Journal of Technology Management, International Journal of Innovation Management, among other outlets.
Member of the Research Institute on Higher Education and Science (INAECU, www.inaecu.com, ror.org/004aqbt71).
His current research interests cover Higher Education management and leadership, as well as Business models and Organizational Studies.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Michael Roellinghoff
speakers.bio: I am a historian of modern Japan specializing in the 19th century colonization of Hokkaido and the impact of colonization on the Indigenous Ainu people.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Frits Rosendaal
speakers.bio: Prof. Rosendaal studied medicine in Rotterdam, and obtained his PhD in Leiden (1989), with a thesis on haemophilia, and was appointed professor in 1997. His research is mainly focused on causes of cardiovascular disorders. He initiated several epidemiological studies, such as LETS and MEGA, that led to the discovery of a large number of causes of thrombosis, such as Factor V Leiden. An important long-standing research line is on the safety of hormonal drugs, notably oral contraceptive drugs, with regard to venous thrombosis. He developed models to understand how thrombosis occurs as a multicausal disorder, and to evaluate the quality of oral anticoagulant treatment. He takes part in large international consortia that search for genetic causes of several frequent disorders. He is initiator of the NEO-study (Netherlands Epidemiology of Obesity) in which 6700 individuals with overweight have been extensively examined and are followed through time. He is co-chair of the Committee on Scientific Integrity of LUMC and Leiden University.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Sue Russell
speakers.bio:
Sue Russell is Senior Funding Policy Advisor in Funding Operations and Governance at Wellcome based in the UK, having joined in 2025. Wellcome is a global charitable foundation that works towards everyone benefitting from science’s potential to improve health and save lives. 
Sue’s role at Wellcome spans a number of funding policy areas including promoting research integrity. She sits on the signatories group for the UK Concordat for Research Integrity and has been involved in rewriting the Concordat and drafting annual statement templates required under the Concordat.
Sue joined Wellcome in 2025, following 10 years in different roles leading funding policies and in grant management roles at Cancer Research UK (CRUK). Before that, Sue spent a further 10 years at various international development funders and non-governmental organisations in programmatic and funding roles, focusing on improving health and disaster relief across many low- and middle-income countries.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Bernhard Sabel
speakers.organization: Otto-v.-Guericke University of Magdeburg & Sciii Foundation
speakers.bio:
Bernhard A. Sabel, PhD

Present position: Full-Professor of Medical Psychology, Medical Faculty, Otto-v.-Guericke University of Magdeburg, Germany (since 1992); med. director of SAVIR-Center

Main domain of research: Brain plasticity after cerebral lesions with focus on visual system damage, vision training and non-invasive microcurrent stimulation for vision restoration; visual system functional imaging; quality of life research in low vision and prediction models of vision restoration; Fake-publishing in science

Academic Education: 
1976-82 Univ. Trier/ Clark Univ. (Worcester, USA)/ Univ. Düsseldorf, M.A. in Psychobiology
1984 Clark Univ., Ph.D. in Psychobiology
1984-86 Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), Cambridge, USA; Postdoctoral fellow in Neuroanatomy/Neurochemistry

Appointments: 
Since 1992 Full Professor, Univ. Magdeburg Medical School, Inst. of Med. Psych., Magdeburg/  
2012-2015 Adjunct Professor Capital Medical Univ., Beijing Tongren Hospital Eye Clinic/ China; 
2011-2013. Senior visiting professor, Chinese Acad. Science, Inst. Automation, Beijing; 
2008-2010 Vice President for Research, Univ. Magdeburg (Germany)/ 
1998-99 Visiting Research Fellow, Dept. of Psychology, Princeton University, USA) / 
1991 Visiting Neuroscientist, Mass Gen Hospital, Harvard Med. School, Boston, USA/ 
1986-92 Research Scientist, Univ. Munich Med. School/ 

Honors/ Awards 
1982-84 German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) scholar/ FULBRIGHT scholarship
2000 Maria Saveria Cinquegrani-Award, for “Best Innovation in Communication & Information Technologies”, Innov. Relay Centres (IRC), Europ. Community, Florenz, Italy 
2005 Leonardo Da Vinci-Award, World Organization for the Achievement of Human Potential, Philadelphia, USA
2008   Science4life Innovation Award, 3.place, Germany
2011   Innovation Achievement Award, German Ministry of Economics, Germany
2012   "Hai-ju", Beijing Overseas Talents Award– City of Beijing, China
2019   Gusi Peace Prize, Manila, Philippines
2020   EMA AWARD 2020, European Medical Association (with Dr. Olga Golubnitschaja)

Leadership in Organizations:  
University of Magdeburg, Vice-President for Research and Technology Transfer (2008-2012);  
International Brain Injury Association (IBIA), Board of Governors (2010-now)
International Society for Low Vision Research and Rehabilitation (ISLRR), Exec. Brd. Member, Secretary (2017-2022) & Vice-President (2022-2023)
Association of German Universities, Chairman of State Chapter, 2019-now.
Editor-in-Chief “Restorative Neurology and Neuroscience” (1997-2024)
International Council of Ophthalmology (ICO), voting member (since 2023)

 
Selected publications: 

>270 SCI publications; 10 books; 19 patents; 14,000+ Citations, H-index: 67

1.	Sabel, B.A. et al. (1984). GM1-ganglioside treatment facilitates behavioral recovery from bilateral brain damage. Science 225: 340-342.
2.	Sabel, B.A. and Stein, D.G. (1986). Pharmacological treatment of central nervous system injury. Nature 323: 493.
3.	Sabel, B.A. (1993). Science Reunification in Germany - "A Crash Program". Science 260: 1753-1758.
4.	Sabel, B.A., et al.  (1997) In vivo confocal neuroimaging of CNS neurons (ICON). Nature med 3: 244-247.
5.	Kasten et al. (1998) Computer-based training for the treatment of partial blindness. Nature med 4: 1083-87.
6.	Gothe et al. (2002) Changes in visual cortex excitability in blind subjects by TMS. Brain 125, 479-490
7.	Poggel et al. (2004) Attentional cueing improves vision restoration therapy. Neurology, 63,2069
8.	Sabel, B.A., Fedorov, A., et al. (2011). Vision restoration after brain damage: The “Residual Vision Activation Theory”. Progress in Brain Research 192: 199-262.
9.	Bola M, Gall C, … Sabel BA (2014) Brain functional connectivity network breakdown and restoration in blindness. Neurology 83:542-551
10.	Bola M, Sabel BA (2015) Dynamic reorganization of brain functional networks during cognition. Neuroimage 114:398-413
11.	Bola M, Gall C, Sabel BA (2015) Disturbed temporal dynamics of brain synchronization in vision loss. Cortex 67:134-146
12.	Gall C, …Sabel BA (2016) Alternating current stimulation for vision restoration after optic nerve damage: a randomized clinical trial. PLoS One 11: e0156134
13.	Sabel BA, et al. (2020) Vision modulation, plasticity and restoration using non-invasive brain stimulation- IFCN-sponsored review. Clin. Neurophysiol. 131 (4): 887-911.
14.	Tawfik M... Sabel BA (2021) Gene therapy with caspase-3 small interfering RNA-nanoparticles is neuroprotective after optic nerve damage. Neural Regen Res 16 (12):2534-2541.
15.	Wu, Z., Sabel, BA (2021) Spacetime in the brain: rapid brain network reorganization in visual processing and recovery. Scientific Reports 11:17940
16.	Sabel, B.A., Levi, D.M. (2021) Movie therapy for children with amblyopia: restoring binocular vision with brain plasticity. Science China Life Science 65, doi.org/10.1007/s11427-021-2050-9
