18.	Wu Z, Xu J, Nürnberger A, Sabel BA. (2022)  Global brain network modularity dynamics after local optic nerve damage following noninvasive brain stimulation: an EEG-tracking study.  Cereb Cortex. Oct 5:bhac375. doi: 10.1093/cercor/bhac375.
19.	Sabel B.A., Knaack E, Gigerenzer G, Bilc M (2023).  Fake Publications in Biomedical Science: Red-flagging Method Indicates Mass Production. www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2023.05.06.23289563v2
20.	Sabel, B.A. (2024) “Fake-Mafia in der Wissenschaft” [Fake-Mafia in Science], German Monograph, Verlag Kohlhammer, 260 pages.
Scientific achievements
•	Increasing our understanding mechanisms of recovery of function after eye and brain damage tby reactivation of neurons in partially damaged tissue.
•	Developed new therapies for blindness using vision training and neuromodulation.  It overcomes the long-held paradigm that blindness is irreversible. 
•	Discovered brain network reorganization processing speed at the millisecond scale which correlates with recovery because synchrony (“time”) in different brain regions (“space”) are tightly intertwined (“Spacetime of the brain”).

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Paula Saner
speakers.bio: Paula Saner is the Research Integrity Manager in the University of Cape Town’s Office of Research Integrity (UCT ORI) and a PhD candidate at Maastricht University. She holds a Master of Arts in Classics, a Master of Education in Higher Education Studies from UCT, and is a certified VIRT2UE Research Integrity trainer. Paula oversees ORI operations and liaises with key government stakeholders. She advises Senate-level Ethics Committees and leads UCT’s responsible conduct of research (RCR) education programme. Paula supports the UCT community on research integrity (RI) matters and helps maintain a strong, current RI policy framework. She served on the Local Organising Committee for the 7th World Conference on Research Integrity (WCRI) and at the 8th WCRI co-hosted a pre-conference workshop on RI leadership, presented a symposium on RI in the Global South, and helped launch a research integrity network for early career researchers and professionals.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Ashrita Saran
speakers.bio: Dr. Ashritta Saran is the Founding Executive Director of Campbell South Asia and Director, Evaluation and Evidence Synthesis Programme at the Global Development Network (GDN). She brings nearly a decade of expertise in systematic reviews, scoping reviews, rapid evidence assessments, and evidence and gap maps across diverse themes in international development. She was co-Chair or the ESIC Working Group on Safe and Responsible Use of AI in Evidence Synthesis.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Louisa Savereide
speakers.bio:
Dr. Savereide is a consultant with Maidstone Consulting Group LLC.  With over ten years of experience in heterogeneous catalysis and chemical engineering research, her technical expertise includes reactor design and construction, catalyst synthesis (including air-free techniques), imaging (STEM-EDX, TEM), spectroscopy (XAS, DRUV-vis, XPS, etc.), and additional catalyst characterization techniques (TPR, TGA, BET, XRD, etc.).  She is also interested in promoting international scientific collaboration and has worked as an intern at the National Academy of Sciences of Costa Rica and volunteered at an NGO in Bolivia. 
Louisa is currently based at the École polytechnique fédérale de Lausanne, where she serves as the research coordinator at the EPFL Energy Center, guiding the formation of projects in the field of energy. She has in-depth knowledge of the grant writing and submission process for small- and large-scale collaborative projects. She received her PhD in Chemical Engineering from Northwestern University and her BS in Chemical Engineering from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Jodi Schneider
speakers.bio: Associate Prof Jodi Schneider is at the Information School, University of Wisconsin-Madison US, where her research explores information quality, with the ultimate goal of ensuring public policy is informed by the best available scientific evidence. Recently, she has studied how unreliable research lingers in science, often through accidental citations to retracted research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Viktor Scholz
speakers.organization: Ombudsgremium Für Die Wissenschaftliche Integrität In Deutschland
speakers.bio: Viktor Scholz holds a Ph.D. in Islamic Studies and supports the Ombuds Committee for Research Integrity as deputy head of office. Before, he was scientific coordinator for research quality at the Berlin University Alliance, where he developed strategies and services to advance research quality and open science in the Berlin research area and a multimedia E-learning course on good research practice for doctoral researchers. Earlier, he developed new models for the professionalisation of Berlin ombuds systems, e.g. via workshops and a Toolbox, in the BUA-funded project „OBUA – Ombudswesen@BUA“ (2021-2023) together with project leader Simona Olivieri.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Thembeka Sdinane
speakers.bio: Thembeka Sdinane is a Lecturer in the Faculty of Health Sciences Research Directorate, Nelson Mandela University, South Africa.  She holds a PhD in Psychology, which focused on Religious Rituals and Psychological Well-being in Rural Communities. In her current role, she oversees research support, ethics and engagement in the faculty of health sciences.   Her research interests are: Indigenous Knowledge systems, Culture and Mental Health and  Research ethics

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Angela Seggio - Welch
speakers.bio: Angela is the Operational Excellence Manager within Elsevier's Research Integrity and Publishing Ethics Center of Expertise.  She is part of the multi-disciplinary team working on research integrity signal development and deployment across our pre- and post-publication investigative units.  In her 15 years with Elsevier, Angela has experience as a Senior Publisher managing journal portfolios across physical and health & medical sciences, Team Leader overseeing scientific editors responsible for facilitating the article transfer service, and most recently, as the Policy and Guideline Manager reviewing and developing policies, guidelines, and curated training programs within the research integrity team.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Bert Seghers
speakers.bio: Bert Seghers, mathematician by training, coordinates the Flemish Commission for Research Integrity, the second-advice body for research integrity complaints in Dutch-speaking Belgium. He is the President of ENRIO, the European Network of Research Integrity Offices.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Nihar Shah
speakers.bio: Nihar B. Shah is an Associate Professor in the Machine Learning and Computer Science departments at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU). His research is on the evaluation of science and the science of evaluation. His group develops computational tools with strong mathematical guarantees, and also designs and conducts controlled experiments for evidence-based policy design. His work has been used in the review of well over hundred thousand papers and thousands of proposals, in over two hundred venues. He is a recipient of the Young Alumnus Medal from the Indian Institute of Science, a JP Morgan faculty research award, Google Research Scholar Award, an NSF CAREER Award 2020-25, the 2017 David J. Sakrison memorial prize from EECS Berkeley for a "truly outstanding and innovative PhD thesis", the Microsoft Research PhD Fellowship 2014-16, the Berkeley Fellowship 2011-13, and several Best Paper Awards.

speakers.title: Prof
speakers.name: Mai Har Sham
speakers.organization: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
speakers.bio: Professor Sham is a Pro-Vice-Chancellor of The Chinese University of Hong Kong and Choh-Ming Li Professor of Biomedical Sciences. Professor Sham has delivered plenary and keynote lectures on the roles of universities on research integrity issues in different continents. She was an organizer of the Asia-Pacific Research Integrity Network meeting in Hong Kong in 2017, the Co-Chair for the 6th World Conference on Research Integrity held in Hong Kong in 2019. Professor Sham obtained her PhD in Biochemistry from the University of Cambridge. She received her postdoctoral training in Developmental Genetics in the National Institute for Medical Research in London, U.K. She had previously served as the Associate Vice-President (Research); Assistant Dean (Research), Head of the Department of Biochemistry, Director of the Centre for Reproduction Development and Growth in the Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine, the University of Hong Kong.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Kritika Sharma
speakers.bio: Kritika is the Research Integrity Advisor at the Luxembourg Agency for Research Integrity. She is also an international lawyer and academic with a focus on governance and oversight mechanisms.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Tanvi Singh
speakers.bio: Tanvi Singh holds a  M.Sc. in Biotechnology. She began her research career at the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) Headquarters, contributing to COVID-19 clinical data management, and later worked as a Scientist at ICMR–National Institute of Malaria Research, focusing on mosquito omics datasets and data organization systems. Currently, she serves as the Research Integrity Officer at the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS), where she supports institutional data governance, integrity compliance, and reproducibility initiatives. Her background spans molecular biology, biostatistics, and entomology, with ongoing research on the behavioral biology of Anopheles mosquitoes. She is also one of two Research Integrity professionals currently serving within the Government of India, contributing to national efforts to strengthen research transparency and data stewardship.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Carthage Smith
speakers.position: Head of the Global Science Forum
speakers.bio: Carthage Smith joined the OECD as head of the Global Science Forum (GSF) Secretariat in 2014. He is responsible for working with countries to define the overall strategy and priorities for the Forum.  This includes policy work on research infrastructures, Open Science, research funding mechanisms, science advisory processes and research careers. He has been leading the GSF team working on Research Security since 2021. Carthage was originally trained as a biochemist, with a PhD in neuroscience. Prior to joining the GSF secretariat, he was Deputy Executive Director of the International Council for Science (ICSU, Paris) for twelve years.  Before moving to France, he spent six years at the UK Medical Research Council, where he was Head of International Cooperation.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Jonathan Soeharno
speakers.bio:
Prof. dr. mr. Jonathan Soeharno is full professor of administration of justice and legal philosophy at the University of Amsterdam.

He holds degrees in law (PhD, LLM, LLB), theology (MA, BA) and philosophy (MA, BA) and has noted expertise on the rule of law, ethics and integrity. Prof. Soeharno is also practicing lawyer (Of Counsel), deputy judge at the Court of Appeal ‘s-Hertogenbosch (commerce chamber) and a former Senator of the State of The Netherlands. Among others, he is the author of The Integrity of the Judge (Ashgate 2009), Introduction to Logic for Lawyers (2021) and The Value of the Oath (2020).

Prof. Soeharno has worked on rule of law projects involving the judiciary and lawyers in, among others, Ukraine, Russia, Suriname, Curacao and Indonesia.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Zexing Song
speakers.bio:
Dr. Zexing Song is a dermatologist and PhD student in Clinical Epidemiology at
the Institute of Health Policy, Management, and Evaluation, University of Toronto. His research focuses on developing clinical prediction models, assessing the risk of bias in observational studies, and promoting open science practices to enhance transparency and reproducibility in clinical research

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Mads Sørensen
speakers.bio: Mads P. Sørensen is a Professor at the Danish Centre for Studies in Research and Research Policy at Aarhus University, Denmark. His current research focuses on Social Theory and Research on Research, including Research Integrity, Research Ethics, and Research Policy: pure.au.dk/portal/en/persons/mps@ps.au.dk

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Eleni Spyrakou
speakers.bio:
Eleni Spyrakou is a senior researcher at the School of Chemical Engineering of the National Technical
University of Athens (r-nano.gr/personnel/eleni-spyrakou-2/ ). She has studied Philosophy
(BA Hons, University of Patras), and she holds an MPhil in Philosophy focusing on Ethics and Political
philosophy (King’s College London, UK) and a PhD (Hons) in Political Philosophy (University of
Ioannina, Greece). Her main research interests include research ethics and research integrity, applied ethics (bioethics, environmental
ethics, business ethics), technology assessment,  the ethics-politics relation and its
epistemological problems, the methodology/ies of social sciences. During the last 7 years her
research has been focused on research ethics and research integrity through her participation in a number of Horizon 2020 and Horizon Europe “Science with and for Society” projects. She was a member of the local organising committee of the 8th WCRI in Athens, Greece in June 2024.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Inge Stegeman
speakers.bio: Inge Stegeman is epidemiologist in the UMC Utrecht in the Netherlands. She combines clinical research with meta-science.

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Brandon Stell
speakers.bio:
Brandon Stell is a neuroscientist with the CNRS in Paris, France. His research explores how the brain coordinates and adapts movement in response to a changing environment. He earned his undergraduate degree in biology from the University of Colorado and completed his PhD in 2005 at UCLA, where he trained with Istvan Mody. After his doctoral work, he moved to Paris as a postdoctoral researcher and has held a permanent position with the CNRS since 2014.

In 2012, in his spare time, he co-founded PubPeer, a website dedicated to the community-based evaluation of scientific publications, which he now runs with fellow CNRS researcher Boris Barbour. Together, they also lead the nonprofit PubPeer Foundation, which supports the platform. This work is carried out independently of their CNRS roles.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Stefan Stender
speakers.bio: A/Prof Stefan Stender obtained his MD from Aarhus University, and his PhD from the University of Copenhagen, Denmark. He is a specialist clinical biochemist and Associate Professor at Rigshospitalet, Copenhagen University Hospital, where his research team studies the genetics of common diseases using AI-ready human health datasets.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Dimity Stephen
speakers.organization: German Centre for Higher Education Research and Science Studies
speakers.bio: Dimity Stephen is a post-doctoral researcher at the German Centre for Higher Education Research and Science Studies (DZHW) in Berlin, Germany. She holds a bachelor’s in psychology and a PhD in public health from Queensland University of Technology in Brisbane, Australia. Dimity's main research focus lies in applying scientometric methods to investigate questions pertaining to questionable publishing practices, peer review, and the quality of research outputs. She is currently leading a four-year, internationally collaborative project funded by the Volkswagen Foundation that examines the effect of research evaluation systems on researchers' publishing practices in six countries in Europe, Asia, and Africa

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Michael Streeter
speakers.bio: Mike Streeter is Director of Research Integrity Strategy & Policy at Wiley, leading a cross-functional team representing a range of publishing services that support publication ethics and integrity.  He champions strategic alignment for research integrity and proactive policy development across our Research Publishing organization and with our external partners. Mike is a member of COPE Council, the STM Integrity Hub Governance Board, United2Act, and the STM Research Integrity Committee. He amplifies Wiley’s mission through frequent scholarly communications partnerships and events.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Amanda Sulicz
speakers.bio:
Amanda Sulicz is the Associate Program Manager, Publishing Ethics at IEEE. In this role, Amanda is responsible for upholding the integrity of the scholarly record by assisting with investigations and working with IEEE’s volunteers to resolve ethics and plagiarism allegations.

Prior to joining IEEE in 2023, Amanda started her publishing career in 2019 at AIP Publishing. Initially, she was as an Associate Editor where she made first editorial decisions and handled the peer review process for manuscripts submitted to AIPP’s flagship journal, Applied Physics Reviews. While serving as an Associate Editor, Amanda was involved in expanding the journal portfolio through the launch of two new titles, Biophysics Reviews and Chemical Physics Reviews. In 2021, Amanda transitioned into the role of Journal Manager for AIP Advances where she was responsible for the journal’s strategic and financial success. Simultaneously, Amanda worked to support the expansion of AIPP’s Open Access portfolio by helping to launch 3 new Gold Open Access journals.

Before starting her career in publishing, Amanda worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher in the lab of Dr. Kirk S. Schanze upon the completion of her Ph.D. in Chemistry from Case Western Reserve University in 2017.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Deborah Sweet
speakers.position: Executive Vice President, Journals, Nature Portfolio
speakers.bio: Deborah Sweet, PhD, Executive Vice President, Journals, Nature Portfolio has broad experience in high-quality journal publishing, including previous roles as the launch Editor-in-Chief of Cell Stem Cell and the VP of Editorial at Cell Press.  She joined Springer Nature in 2022 as Vice President, Journals for the Nature Journals and then in 2024 became Executive Vice President for Nature Portfolio. In this role she is responsible for leading the editorial teams working on Nature, the Nature Research journals, the Nature Reviews journals, Nature Communications, and the Communications and npj series journals,  and guiding the strategic direction of the Nature Portfolio overall.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Anu Tammeleht
speakers.bio: Dr Anu Tammeleht is a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Helsinki. She is also a research fellow in ethics and a methodologist/trainer of research ethics at the Centre for Ethics, University of Tartu, Estonia. She is a designer of research ethics and integrity training resources and a facilitator of trainings of different formats at higher education institutions in Estonia and at the University of Helsinki. Her research area involves development of ethics competencies and evaluating the effectiveness of research ethics and integrity training. She also contributes to developing the national research ethics and integrity system (Estonia) through national surveys, policy recommendations and presentations.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Gengyan Tang
speakers.bio:
My name is Gengyan Tang, and my academic journey has been shaped by a deep concern for integrity in research and education. During my master’s studies in China, I investigated predatory journals and the motivations behind authorship, which gave me first-hand exposure to the systemic challenges of misconduct. These experiences convinced me that questions of integrity cannot be reduced to individual failings—they are embedded in broader institutional and cultural contexts.

As a Ph.D. student at the University of Calgary, my current work weaves together three strands. I study how paper mills reorganize scholarly collaboration and reshape the trust networks of science. I also analyze research integrity policies, with particular attention to the global shift from punitive sanctions toward more restorative approaches that emphasize learning, dialogue, and reintegration. In parallel, I explore how minority and Indigenous cultural traditions might enrich integrity education by offering relational and community-centered perspectives that differ from dominant Western models.

Through these inquiries, I aim to reimagine integrity not only as compliance with rules, but as a shared practice sustained by cultures, policies, and communities.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Carl Taswell
speakers.bio: Dr. Taswell has been certified as a diplomate of both the American Board of Nuclear Medicine and the American Board of Psychiatry & Neurology, and serves on the faculty at UCSD with expertise in functional and molecular brain imaging. He received his BA in biochemistry from Harvard College, his MSc in mathematics and MD in medicine both from New York University, and his PhD in scientific computing and computational mathematics from Stanford University. He has completed a post-doctoral fellowship in biostatistics at Harvard School of Public Health, as well as clinical training in psychiatry at UCSD and in nuclear medicine at UCSF. Since receiving a top ten scholarship award in the 1974 Science Talent Search (administered by the Society for Science), he has published numerous research papers spanning the fields of biochemistry, immunology, medicine, statistics, mathematics, informatics and brain imaging.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Lucas Tersigni
speakers.bio: Lucas Tersigni is a Policy Advisor at Public Safety Canada's Research Security Centre and has been there since it was stood up in 2022. Lucas is responsible for providing advice to Canada's granting councils on national security matters as well as providing advice to the research community on how best to secure their research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Jackie Thompson
speakers.organization: University Of Bristol / University Of Oxford

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Joeri Tijdink
speakers.bio: Joeri Tijdink is an associate professor and principal investigator at Amsterdam UMC, location VUmc, and is affiliated with VU University in Amsterdam, the Netherlands. After studying medicine at Utrecht University (1999–2006) and specializing as a clinical psychiatrist in Amsterdam (2007–2012), he completed his PhD (2012–2015), entitled Publish & Perish: Research on Research and Researchers. His thesis focused on the impact of publication pressure on research quality and the mental health of researchers. His current research focuses on research integrity, reproducibility, research quality, mental well-being in academia, and research culture. He is involved in several national and international research projects, including the TIER2 project (www.tier2-project.eu), which explores the future(s) of reproducibility and develops tools to support reproducibility practices. He is also a principal investigator in the EU-funded TRUSTparency project, which implements promotion plans for different stakeholders to foster reproducibility practices. In addition, he leads several projects aimed at supporting a responsible research culture in diverse academic settings and studies how early career researchers can be empowered to speak up. He initiated the national Akademiethermometer survey, which investigates the mental health of academics in the Netherlands. Joeri is also the author of the book The Happy Academic – How to Thrive and Survive in Academia (2023), which offers guidance to early career researchers navigating the challenges of academic life. In his work, he consistently focuses on individual, cultural, and systemic factors that can help improve academia, with a strong emphasis on promoting mental health among researchers. Alongside his research, he continues to work as a clinical psychiatrist. In 2022, he was appointed as a member of The Young Academy of the Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences, highlighting his commitment to improving research quality, strengthening the societal relevance of research, and fostering a more supportive academic environment for early career researchers.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Andrea Tricco
speakers.bio: Andrea Tricco holds an MSc in Epidemiology and a PhD in Population Health. She is a Scientist and Executive Director at the Li Ka Shing Knowledge Institute, St. Michael’s Hospital, and a Professor at the University of Toronto’s Dalla Lana School of Public Health and Institute of Health Policy, Management & Evaluation. She also serves as Adjunct and Associate Graduate Faculty at Ontario Tech University. Her research focuses on advancing knowledge synthesis to support decision-makers, leading projects on rapid reviews, network meta-analysis, and scoping reviews. Dr. Tricco has over 420 journal publications and holds a Tier 1 Canada Research Chair in Knowledge Synthesis for Knowledge Users. Recognized as a highly cited researcher, she directs an online knowledge synthesis course completed by over 655 participants. She is also Co-Editor in Chief of the Journal of Clinical Epidemiology.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Daniel Ucko
speakers.position: Head Of Ethics And Research Integrity
speakers.bio:
Daniel Ucko is the Head of Ethics and Research Integrity at the American Physical Society (APS), where he is responsible for the publication ethics and integrity of the Physical Review journals. In this role he also shapes journal and ethics policy for the society. He has been an elected member of COPE Council since 2023. 

Before starting his career in academic publishing, he did research in condensed matter physics. He holds a PhD in Physics from University College London, UK (2001) and a PhD in Philosophy of Science from Stony Brook University, USA, on the subject of peer review, which he completed in 2020. His book: "The Subjectivities and Objectivities of Peer Review" was published by Brill in 2024.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Lesley Uttley
speakers.bio: Lesley is a Senior Research Fellow at the School of Medicine and Population Health, University of Sheffield. She is a career development award holder (2020-2024) and transition support award holder (2025-2026) for investigating human influences in systematic reviews and meta-analyses, funded by the UKRI Medical Research Council (MRC). Lesley leads a global research integrity initiative to monitor the problems with systematic reviews (systematicreviewlution.com). Lesley is a researcher for the ESRC funded Behavioural Research Hub (BR-UK). Lesley is also an EDI Fellow for the NIHR funded Sheffield Biomedical Research Centre (2024-2026) and an Editorial Board Member for the Journal of Clinical Epidemiology. Her work is particularly focussed on research integrity, research culture and human influences in research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Veerle Van den Eynden
speakers.bio: Expert advisor in Research Data Management, with extensive expertise in data management and data sharing for the social, environmental, and health sciences, providing advice, training, policy development, and practical implementation.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Susanne van den Hooff
speakers.bio:
Susanne van den Hooff is an assistant professor at the University of Humanistic Studies, Utrecht, The Netherlands. She is a qualitative social scientist with a PhD in Care Ethics and a Master's degree in Psychology and Health Law. Her research focus is on qualitative, interdisciplinary research. Among other things, she conducted research on good care for people with Korsakoff syndrome; she contributed to a juridical study concerning euthanasia carried out on dementia patients with an advance euthanasia directive. She is involved as a researcher in the Horizon Europe project, "Beyond Bad Apples: Towards a Behavioral and Evidence-Based Approach to Promote Research Ethics and Research Integrity in Europe” (BEYOND), a Horizon Europe project that investigates the root causes of research misconduct (see: beyondbadapples.eu/ ).
She is an active member of the CoARA working group ERIP: Ethics @ Research Integrity Policy in Responsible Research Assessment for Data and Artificial Intelligence (see: coara.org/working-groups/wg-erip/).

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Marijke Van Vlasselaer
speakers.bio:
Marijke Van Vlasselaer (marijke.van.vlasselaer@vub.be; ORCID: 0009-0001-2434-3695) is a 
doctoral researcher at the Data Analytics Lab, Vrije Universiteit Brussel. Her research focuses on 
academic integrity, generative AI in higher education, and policy reform in higher education. She also 
works on moral emotions, the philosophy of (alternative) education, and the didactics of philosophy.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Baptiste Vendé
speakers.bio: After earning a Master’s degree in Philosophy of Science from Leibniz University Hannover, Baptiste Vendé continued his studies as a PhD candidate. His thesis focuses on the intersection of scientific integrity, evidence-based policy, and causality. Funded by a COFRA grant, he is a student at Sorbonne Université while primarily working at his administrative employer, the Ofis (French office for research integrity).

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Anné Verhoef
speakers.position: Director Of The Nwu Ai Hub
speakers.bio: Anné H. Verhoef is a professor in Philosophy and Director of the North-West University (NWU) AI Hub. He holds a PhD from the University of Stellenbosch and a second one from the Free University in Amsterdam. His research interests are, amongst others, ethics, artificial intelligence and academic integrity, and the philosophy of happiness. He published various academic articles on academic integrity in Higher Education. He is the co-founder and former associate editor of the academic journal Transformation in Higher Education. He is also the co-founder of AICSA – the Artificial Intelligence Circle in South Africa for Higher Education.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Navin Kumar Verma
speakers.position: Director, Research Integrity and Ethics Office (RIEO)
speakers.bio: Dr Navin Kumar Verma is an Associate Professor of Immunology and Cell Biology at Lee Kong Chan School of Medicine (LKCMedicine), Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU). In addition, he serves as a head of Good Research Practice Office at LKCMedicine and Deputy Research Integrity Officer at NTU. He is an expert T-cell immunologist, internationally recognized for his work on the T-cell integrin lymphocyte function-associated antigen-1, has published over 80 papers, filed 5 patents, and won several national and international awards. He is also an elected Fellow of the Royal Society of Biology, UK.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Anna Catharina Vieira Armond
speakers.bio: Anna Catharina (PhD, MSc, DMD) is a postdoctoral fellow in the Metaresearch and Open Science Program at the University of Ottawa Heart Institute. She holds a Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD) degree and a PhD in Health Sciences. She has contributed to multiple European and North American projects on metaresearch, research ethics, and research integrity. Her research explores various aspects of metaresearch, with a current focus on data management and sharing, open science practices, and best practices in research reporting.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Retha Visagie
speakers.bio: Dr. Retha Visagie is a certified Senior Research Management Professional (IRRC) and a leading voice in research integrity and ethics governance across Africa. With over a decade of experience, she has developed tailored training programs, policies, and systems for researchers, administrators, and ethics committees. As co-founder and co-chair of SARIMA’s Northern Regions Community of Practice, she fosters regional collaboration and best practices. She leads Unisa’s role in the Gates-funded SARIMA Capacity Strengthening Project and co-authored the SADC Research Ethics Guidelines and Nagoya Protocol Toolkit. Currently chairing the EthiXPERT Board and co-PI on the RHInnO Ethics AI Project, she champions Afrocentric, AI-driven ethics governance. A UNESCO-trained ethics educator and one of three Virt2ue trainers in South Africa, she has trained over 7,000 individuals. Her work has earned multiple awards and advanced policy reform, capacity building, and institutional ethics. She continues to supervise postgraduates and publish widely in peer-reviewed outlets.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nathalie Voarino
speakers.bio: Nathalie is a Public Policy Project Manager for the French Office for Research Integrity. With a PhD in bioethics, she left her research on AI ethics to now support the implementation of the French research integrity policy; in conducting surveys and prospective work.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Hella von Unger
speakers.bio: Hella von Unger is Professor of Sociology with a focus on Qualitative Methodologies at Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München (LMU Munich), Germany. She studied Social Sciences at the University of Hannover and the University of California, Berkeley, and received her PhD in Sociology from the University of Hannover in 2005. Her dissertation – a qualitative study on multiply stigmatized lesbian and bisexual mothers living with HIV/AIDS – was funded by the HIV Center for Clinical and Behavioral Studies at Columbia University, New York. Before her appointment at LMU Munich in 2013, she held academic positions at Columbia University, the University of Magdeburg, and the Social Science Research Center Berlin (WZB), Germany. Her research focuses on qualitative and participatory social research, the sociology of health, migration studies, and research ethics. She is an elected speaker of neks (Network of Research Ethics Committees in the Social Sciences) and a partner in the EU-funded CHANGER project.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Namol Vorapreeda
speakers.organization: King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi (KMUTT)
speakers.bio:
My name is Namol Vorapreeda , now I serve as an Educational Development Officer at the Office of Research, Innovation, and Partnerships, King Mongkut’s University of Technology Thonburi (KMUTT). I hold a Master of Science in Biotechnology from KMUTT and a Bachelor of Science in Agro-Industrial Technology from King Mongkut’s University of Technology North Bangkok.
My professional journey has focused on advancing research integrity, biosafety, human and animal ethical oversight. I am certified in Biorisk Management by the International Federation of Biosafety Associations (IFBA), and I have served as Secretary of KMUTT’s Institutional Biosafety Committee since 2011. I also support the Human Research Ethics Committee and the Animal Care and Use Committee as Assistant Secretary, and since 2019, I have contributed to KMUTT’s Working Group on Research Integrity Promotion.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: COLBY VORLAND
speakers.organization: Indiana University School of Public Health-Bloomington
speakers.bio: Colby Vorland is an assistant research scientist at the Indiana University School of Public Health-Bloomington. His research focuses on error detection and correction, semi-automated methods to improve research quality, reproducibility in science, reporting quality, and other meta-research primarily related to nutrition, obesity, and aging. He is committed to enhancing the transparency and integrity of scientific research. He contributes to initiatives aimed at improving methodological standards through statistical support, education, and technological solutions.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Miro Vukovic
speakers.organization: University Of Split School Of Medicine
speakers.bio: Miro Vuković, MD, is a Research and Teaching Assistant at the University of Split School of Medicine, Department for Research in Biomedicine and Health. He earned his Doctor of Medicine degree from the same institution in 2022 and gained clinical experience as a General Medicine practitioner in Split–Dalmatia County. Miro completed two research internships at Harvard Medical School’s Department of Neurobiology, focusing on neuroscience and sleep research. His scientific contributions include publications in Scientific Reports, BMJ Open, and Therapeutic Apheresis and Dialysis. As an active member of the academic community, he has held leadership roles in non-profit organization called Med&X where he still serves as a vice-president. His research interests span health technology assessment and public health.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Lorna Waddington
speakers.bio: Dr Lorna Waddington is Associate Professor of International History at the University of Leeds and Academic Lead for Academic Integrity. Her research examines ideology, foreign policy, and atrocity in the twentieth century, including Britain's responses to genocide.

speakers.title: Ms
speakers.name: Karen Wallace
speakers.organization: Secretariat on Responsible Conduct of Research
speakers.bio:
Karen Wallace is the Executive Director for the Secretariat on Responsible Conduct of Research. The Secretariat is responsible for the implementation of the Tri-Agency Framework: Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR Framework) on behalf of Canada’s three research funding Agencies (the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR), the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC) and the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC)).  
She has been working in the research integrity and responsible conduct of research (RCR) space since joining CIHR more than 20 years ago. Karen played an integral role in the development of the first Canadian Framework on RCR. Since 2021, Karen has been the Executive Director of the Secretariat where she leads a dedicated team responsible for developing and interpreting guidance on responsible conduct of research and the ethical conduct for research involving humans in Canada.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Kari Weaver
speakers.position: Program Manager, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning and Learning, Teaching, and Instructional Design Librarian
speakers.bio: Kari D. Weaver is a librarian, educator, and currently the Program Manager, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning with the Ontario Council of University Libraries (OCUL) and the Learning, Teaching, and Instructional Design Librarian at the University of Waterloo in Canada. A noted scholar in the fields of information science and education, much of her recent work has focused on artificial intelligence citation, attribution, and disclosure practices, leading to the development of the widely adopted Artificial Intelligence Disclosure (AID) Framework.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Kathryn Weber-Boer
speakers.bio: Originally an anthropological archaeologist, Kathryn Weber-Boer transitioned from research (as a doctoral candidate at Cornell University) to research support, when she began a research intelligence project at the University of Groningen. She joined Digital Science when she saw the opportunity to make a broader impact in the world of responsible research evaluation. As a computational social scientist, she uses Altmetric and Dimensions data to explore non-traditional metrics, understand the use and perception of research, and improve research integrity–in a systemic way. Her recent work considers the importance of collaboration between social sciences and STEM, the role of mentorship on integrity practices, and the use and reuse of research data. As Director Scientometrics, Kathryn is responsible for engagement with scientometric researchers, managing access to Digital Science data for research and journalistic purposes, and collaborating with partners to improve and innovate Digital Science data and insights.

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Sakine Weikert
speakers.position: Head of Section, Center for International Academic Cooperation (KIWi)
speakers.bio: Sakine Weikert is head of the Centre for International Academic Cooperation (KIWi) at the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD). KIWi offers German universities individual advice on the strategic development and expansion of international cooperation under ever more complex framework conditions. Focus topics are research security, science diplomacy or legal frameworks, which are combined with DAAD’s worldwide regional expertise.

speakers.title: Ms.
speakers.name: Sarah Patricia Wendt
speakers.bio:
As a researcher at the Robert K. Merton Center for Science Studies, Sarah examines social configurations and practices in research assessment, especially in the academic publishing system and peer review processes. Her focus is on qualitative social research, epistemic diversity and (historically grown) power relations within academia.

Sarah studied Global Studies at Humboldt University Berlin, University of Pretoria, Jawaharlal Nehru University New Delhi and FLACSO Argentina. The various places, institutions and local and global collectives in which she was and is involved, especially with regard to the decolonization of knowledge, shape her understanding of concepts of epistemic justice and epistemic diversity. From 2022 to 2024 she worked at the BIH QUEST Center for Responsible Research at the Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin on the evaluation of programs for the transformation of biomedical research and global health, as well as on topics related to open science and reproducibility of research.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Kimberley Wever
speakers.organization: Radboud university medical center, Nijmegen, The Netherlands
speakers.bio: Dr. Kim Wever is an assistant professor at the meta-research team of the Radboud University Medical Center, Nijmegen. She has a strong skillset in meta-research methodology and leads training programs to improve research rigour and transparency. She leads the Dutch national coordinating centre of CAMARADES since 2014. Kim is co-founder and board member of PROSPERO4animals, preclinicaltrials.eu and GoEQIPD.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Karolyn White
speakers.bio: Karolyn White is the Director, Research Ethics and Integrity at Macquarie University, Australia.  Her role involves oversight of all research ethics at the University including Chairing the Human Research Ethics Committee. Her responsibilities involve promoting and teaching research ethics and integrity at the University and elsewhere. Karolyn is actively involved in ethics and integrity professional associations (AEN and ARMS). Karolyn was appointed by the NHMRC The Australian Code for the Responsible Conduct of Research Review Committee 2016/7 and Chaired the working party to develop the Guide to managing and investigating potential breaches of the Australian Code for the Responsible Conduct of Research.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Will White
speakers.position: Scholarly Communications Librarian and Assistant Professor
speakers.organization: University of New Mexico Health Sciences Library and Informatics Center
speakers.bio: Will White is the Scholarly Communications Librarian at the University of New Mexico's Health Sciences Library and Informatics Center. He was previously the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee's Health Sciences Librarian. Will co-presented with Dr. Leslie McIntosh on "A taxonomy of scholarly disinformation to enhance research and integrity" at the 8th WCRI in Athens.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nicolaus Wilder
speakers.bio: Nicolaus Wilder works as a research assistant at the Institute of Pedagogy at Kiel University in the Department of General Pedagogy. His research focuses on the theories and basic concepts of (general) pedagogy as well as its philosophical foundations and references.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: John Willinsky
speakers.bio: John Willinsky is Professor at Simon Fraser University, as well as Khosla Family Professor Emeritus at Stanford University. He is founder and Co-Scientific Director of the Public Knowledge Project, which conducts research and develops the world’s most widely used open source scholarly publishing platform in support of greater access to knowledge. His dozen books include Empire of Words: The Reign of the OED (Princeton, 1994); Learning to Divide the World: Education at Empire’s End (Minnesota, 1998); The Access Principle: The Case for Open Access to Research and Scholarship (MIT, 2006), The Intellectual Properties of Learning: A Prehistory from Saint Jerome to John Locke (Chicago, 2017) and Copyright’s Broken Promise: How to Restore the Law’s Ability to Promote the Progress of Science (MIT, 2023).

speakers.title: Mrs.
speakers.name: Laura Wilson
speakers.bio: Head of Research Integrity & Ethics at Taylor & Francis.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Charis Wong
speakers.bio: Charis is a neurologist and clinical fellow based at the University of Edinburgh. She is interested in developing and applying AI-enabled evidence synthesis methodology to inform drug prioritisation for clinical trials in neurodegenerative diseases. She was part of the trial development group for MND-SMART (mnd-smart.org), an adaptive platform trial in motor neuron disease (MND). For her PhD, supervised by Professor Malcolm Macleod, Professor Siddharthan Chandran, and Professor Neil Carragher, she developed methods to identify, evaluate, and prioritise candidate drugs for clinical trials in MND. She is continuing to develop this work with the aim of improving the framework and expanding its applications to other disease areas. She is interested in the responsible use of AI in evidence synthesis and evaluation of AI-enabled digital evidence synthesis tools.

speakers.title: Prof.
speakers.name: Patrick Chun Man Wong
speakers.organization: The Chinese University Of Hong Kong
speakers.bio: Patrick C. M. Wong is Professor of Linguistics and Cognitive Neuroscience and Founding Director of the Brain and Mind Institute at The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK). Wong’s research covers a wide range of basic and translational issues concerning the neural basis and disorders of language and music. In 2021, he was named a Guggenheim Fellow for Humanities. A versatile and effective teacher, research mentor, and clinical educator, Wong is a four-time recipient of the Faculty Outstanding Teaching Award at CUHK. He actively seeks to translate his research into clinical and educational solutions. One of his patented inventions, Precision Listening®, was awarded the Gold Medal with Congratulations of the Jury at the 2023 International Exhibition of Inventions Geneva. Wong has been Associate Vice-President (Research) at CUHK since 2023.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Josephine Woodhams
speakers.bio:
Dr Josephine Woodhams has a Ph.D. in Medicine (Clinical Studies). She previously worked as a Senior Research Associate with over 10 years’ experience of designing and performing experiments in photobiology and nanomedicine at London’s Global University – UCL. She has contributed to collaborative cancer research projects with partners within the EU and USA.
Josephine’s role as Senior Programmes Manager is to support the delivery of UKRIO’s programme of work and assist with the development of new activities, in accordance with the objectives of the charity. A key element of her role is liaising with UKRIO’s many subscriber institutions to ensure proactive support of the needs of their researchers.
Josephine collaborates closely with the Chief Executive, Trustees and Advisory Board, Expert Community, and the representatives and members of UKRIO subscriber institutions.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Jennifer Wright
speakers.position: Head Of Publication Ethics And Research Integrity

speakers.title: Dr
speakers.name: Liying Yang
speakers.organization: National Science Library, Chinese Academy Of Sciences
speakers.bio:
Dr. Liying Yang got her PhD degree in Scientometrics field from the Library of CAS in 2007. She is the director of Department for Scientometrics and Research evaluation, Library, CAS. 
The research of Dr. Yang focuses on quantitative analysis to provide decision making support. She has published more than 120 research articles in international journals of scientometrics field. In addition, she has been conducted abundant projects supported by Ministry of Science and Technology of the People´s Republic of China (MOST), National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC).
Dr. Liying Yang was awarded research fellow of Fraunhofer Institute for Systems and Innovation Research ISI, Karlsruhe, Germany. She is the co-editors in chief of Journal of Data and Information Science (JDIS). Over the past 20 years, she has led and released of a series of journal evaluation standards that have had a broad impact on the academic community in China.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Nicole Yeo
speakers.bio:
Nicole comes from the field of Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) and is supporting the efforts of Research Office at A*STAR (Agency for Science, Technology and Research) in Singapore. With over a decade’s experience as both a researcher and administrator, she focuses on developing policies and guidelines that are practical for researchers and beneficial to institutions.

She has contributed to investigations into academic and research misconduct, co-authored peer-reviewed publications, and collaborated on initiatives to promote research integrity and build awareness of RCR practices. Nicole’s work draws together science, ethics, and policy, with an emphasis on supporting a research environment grounded in fairness, accountability, and excellence.

She values opportunities to learn from and work alongside others in the research community, and shares insights on fostering integrity and navigating complex issues in a way that supports both individual researchers and the broader scientific enterprise.

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Vincent Yuan
speakers.bio: My name is Vincent and I am a medical student from Australia. My research interests are in spin and publication bias.

speakers.title: Dr.
speakers.name: Maria Zalm
speakers.position: Senior Editor, Team Manager Publication Ethics

speakers.title: Mr.
speakers.name: Er-Te Zheng
speakers.bio: Er-Te Zheng is a PhD student at the School of Information, Journalism and Communication at the University of Sheffield. His research focuses on computational social science, science of science, scientometrics and research integrity.

conference.name: WCRI 2026 - 8th World Conference on Research Integrity
